prime_award_base_transaction_description: TOPIC ID: ARL-BAA-0069 (BIOTRONICS) - GIVING CELLS A VOICE: AI AS A TRANSLATOR ACROSS BIOLOGICAL SCALES
subawardee_name: UNIVERSITY OF VERMONT & STATE AGRICULTURAL COLLEGE
subawardee_business_types: NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
THE COOPERATING INSTITUTION (UVM) WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR EIGHT OF THE PROPOSED WORK PACKAGES: FIVE IN YEAR 1, TWO IN YEAR 2, AND ONE IN YEAR 3.
YEAR 1
 TASK 1.2.1. “VATGPT”. IN THIS WORK PACKAGE UVM WILL DEVELOP AN AI PROTOTYPE THAT ALLOWS A HUMAN INVESTIGATOR TO CHAT WITH AN APPROXIMATION OF A VAT OF CULTURED CELLS, SUCH AS THE BIOREACTOR BEING DEVELOPED IN
TASK 1.1. AT FIRST, VATGPT WILL RECEIVE AS INPUT TWO DATA STREAMS. THE FIRST WILL BE A CONTINUOUS STREAM OF TEXT PROMPTS ENTERED BY HUMAN PROMPTERS. THE SECOND WILL BE SURROGATE PHYSIOLOGICAL DATA (SEE TASK
1.2.2). THE OUTPUT OF VATGPT WILL BE “SHOW” AND “TELL”: IT WILL RESPOND, IN ENGLISH, TO THE PROMPTER’S QUESTION (“TELL”), AND IT WILL ALSO PRESENT PHYSIOLOGICAL DATA THAT SUPPORTS AND VALIDATES ITS CLAIMS (“SHOW”).
VATGPT WILL BE TRAINED ON MANY TEXT-AND-TIME-SERIES SEGMENTS. EACH SEGMENT WILL BE DRAWN FROM ONE OF TWO SOURCES: THE TOY HOMEOSTAT (TASKS 1.2.2 AND 1.2.3) AND THE PHYSIOLOGY LITERATURE (TASK 1.2.4).
TASK 1.2.2. THE TOY HOMEOSTAT. THE FIRST SOURCE OF TRAINING DATA FOR VATGPT WILL BE A TOY HOMEOSTAT: A SET OF ORDINARY DIFFERENTIAL EQUATIONS THAT APPROXIMATE SOME PHYSIOLOGICAL SYSTEM, WHICH WILL ALLOW US TO
DEVELOP THE AI PORTION IN PARALLEL WITH THE BIOTECHNOLOGY WORK THAT NEEDS TO HAPPEN TO ENABLE REAL DATA COLLECTION FROM LIVING SYSTEMS.
TASK 1.2.3. THE “PHYSIOLOGICAL” TRAINING SET. PERIODICALLY, THE MOST RECENT DATA FROM THIS HOMEOSTAT WILL BE SKIMMED OFF AND A MANUALLY-WRITTEN PIECE OF CODE WILL GENERATE AND ATTACH A TEXT DESCRIPTION OF THAT DATA,
SUCH AS “X2 IS INCREASING”, “X3 IS OSCILLATING”, “X1 INCREASED JUST BEFORE X2 DID”, AND SO FORTH.
TASK 1.2.4. THE LITERATURE TRAINING SET. THE SECOND SOURCE OF DATA WILL BE DRAWN FROM HELA CANCER CELL DATABASES. TIME SERIES DATA WILL BE EXTRACTED FROM THESE DATABASES AND COMBINED WITH THE TEXT DESCRIPTIONS
OF THAT DATA FROM THE DATABASES. THE NAMES OF THE STATE VARIABLES FROM THIS DATABASE WILL BE COPIED INTO THE NAMES OF THE STATE VARIABLES IN THE TOY HOMEOSTAT. THIS WILL APPROXIMATE LATER TASKS IN WHICH REAL
PHYSIOLOGICAL DATA IS COMBINED WITH DATA DRAWN FROM THE LITERATURES DESCRIBING THAT PHYSIOLOGICAL SYSTEM.
TASK 1.2.5. PROOF OF PRINCIPLE. AFTER VATGPT IS TRAINED ON THESE TWO DATA SOURCES, IT WILL BE EXPOSED TO SEVERAL PROMPTS. THESE PROMPTS WILL BE POSED WHEN ONE OR MORE OF THE STATE VARIABLES IN THE TOY HOMEOSTAT
REACH EXTREMAL VALUES, APPROXIMATING A HUMAN PROMPTER WHO FEELS POORLY DUE TO FAR-FROM-BASELINE PHYSIOLOGICAL STATES AND ASKS VATGPT TO EXPLAIN WHY. IF THE VATGPT MODEL RESPONDS WITH TEXT RESPONSES THAT
DESCRIBE STATE VARIABLES EXHIBITING EXTREMAL VALUES AT THAT TIME, THIS WILL SERVE AS VALIDATION OF THIS PROTOTYPE.
YEAR 2
TASK 2.1.1. ELEVATING VATGPT TO A “LEFT BRAIN INTERPRETER”. IN THIS WORK PACKAGE, GAZZANIGA’S HYPOTHESIS THAT THE LEFT PART OF THE HUMAN BRAIN GENERATES LANGUAGE TO EXPLAIN THOUGHTS AND ACTIONS DRIVEN BY THE RIGHT
SIDE OF THE BRAIN WILL GUIDE CONNECTION OF VATGPT (THE “LEFT BRAIN INTERPRETER”) TO THE BIOREACTOR (THE “RIGHT BRAIN”).
TASK 2.1.2. TRAINING AND TESTING ON CAUSE AND EFFECT. TO ENABLE VATGPT TO DEVELOP AN UNDERSTANDING OF CAUSE AND EFFECT, WE WILL EXERT MANY RANDOMLY-GENERATED INTERVENTIONS ON THE CELLS WITHIN THE BIOREACTOR.
THIS WILL RESULT IN A TRAINING SET IN WHICH KNOWN CAUSES (THE INTERVENTIONS) CAUSE KNOWN EFFECTS (FUTURE STATE VARIABLE VALUES). TESTING WILL BE CONDUCTED USING INTERVENTIONS NEVER EXPERIENCED DURING TRAINING, AND
THUS REQUIRING NATURAL LANGUAGE EXPLANATIONS OF EFFECTS NEVER SEEN DURING TRAINING.
YEAR 3
TASK 3.1. “ZAPGPT”. WE WILL EXTEND VATGPT INTO A SYSTEM THAT CAN RECEIVE COMMANDS AS WELL AS QUERIES. COMMANDS WILL BE TRANSLATED INTO BIOELECTRIC INTERVENTIONS THAT PUSH THE PHYSIOLOGICAL SYSTEM INTO SOME
DESIRED STATE. THIS WILL BE ACCOMPLISHED BY EXTENDING THE AI MODEL SUCH THAT IT LEARNS TO TRANSFORM TEXT PROMPTS INTO BIOELECTRIC INTERVENTIONS, AND THEN LEARNS TO PREDICT HOW INTERVENTIONS, IF APPLIED, WILL PERTURB THE
PHYSIOLOGICAL SYSTEM.

subaward_description:
SCOPE OF WORK
THE NATIONAL CENTER FOR DISASTER MEDICINE AND PUBLIC HEALTH’S INTENT IS TO EXECUTE DENVER NDMS PILOT SITE OPERATIONS AND ACTIVITIES COLLABORATIVELY WITH UC ANSCHUTZ AND THE NDMS PILOT FIELD IMPLEMENTATION TEAM (FIT). IN COLLABORATION WITH THE NDMS PILOT PROGRAM FIT, UC ANSCHUTZ WILL SUPPORT THE MANAGEMENT AND EXECUTION OF DENVER NDMS PILOT SITE CORE COMPETENCIES THROUGH REGULAR STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENTS, NDMS RESPONSE PLAN IMPROVEMENT SUPPORT, AS WELL AS THE PLANNING AND EXECUTION OF HOMELAND SECURITY EXERCISE & EVALUATION PROGRAM (HSEEP) COMPLIANT NDMS EXERCISES WITH PARTNERS AND STAKEHOLDERS. AS THE NDMS PILOT IS PRIMARILY FOCUSED ON RESEARCH, EDUCATION, AND TRAINING FOR AN NDMS RESPONSE, ALL PILOT SITE ACTIVITIES SHOULD BE COMPLETED IN DIRECT SUPPORT, AND UNDER THE GUIDANCE AND DIRECTION, OF LOCAL STAKEHOLDERS AND FEDERAL ENTITIES (E.G., ASPR NDMS, VHA OEM, FEMA, USNORTHCOM, ETC.) RESPONSIBLE FOR EXECUTING AN ACTIVATION OF THE DENVER FCC IN THE REGION AND FOLLOW-ON NDMS OPERATIONS . THE PURPOSE AND INTENT OF TRANSITIONING PILOT PROGRAM EFFORTS IS TO ESTABLISH
A PARTNERSHIP WITH A TRUSTED AND WELL INTEGRATED LOCAL ORGANIZATION THAT WILL BE ABLE TO MANAGE AND SUSTAIN THE IMPROVEMENTS IN LOCAL NDMS RESPONSE CAPABILITIES ACHIEVED UNDER THE PILOT. NONE OF THE
REQUIREMENTS IN THIS SOW SHOULD BE INTERPRETED AS DIRECTION FOR UC ANSCHUTZ OR ANY LOCAL PARTNER TO
ENCROACH OR CIRCUMVENT THE AUTHORITY OF ANY DENVER NDMS STAKEHOLDER TO EXECUTE THEIR ASSIGNED
RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER THE NDMS.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: DISCOVERING & MODELING TURBULENCE AND CHEMISTRY INTERACTIONS IN HIGH SPEED REACTIVE FLOWS
subawardee_parent_name: REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF MICHIGAN
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
KEY TASKS OF OUR PROPOSED EFFORT IN THE MURI EFFORTS ARE:

1) YEAR 1: DEVELOP AN APPROACH TO ASSESS THE IMPACT OF THERMOCHEMICAL STATE PROLIFERATION IN HIGHSPEED COMBUSTING FLOWS ON CHEMISTRY MODEL ACCURACY AND UNCERTAINTY QUANTIFICATION. ONE OF THE
VIABLE APPROACHES IS THE USE OF PSR NETWORK TO MIMIC THE TURBULENT COMBUSTION CONDITION OF DIRECT RELEVANCE TO THE CURRENT HIGH-SPEED COMBUSTION PROBLEM. SUCH A NETWORK MAY BE USED TO IDENTIFY
KEY REACTIONS THAT WOULD REQUIRE HIGHER-LEVEL AB INITIO AND REACTION RATE THEORY CALCULATIONS IN ORDER TO REDUCE ITS UNCERTAINTY AND THUS THE IMPACT ON COMBUSTION CHEMISTRY PREDICTIONS.

2) YEARS 1 AND 2: DEVELOP A COMPLEMENTARY APPROACH TO ENABLE ONLINE, ADAPTIVE MECHANISM REDUCTION. THIS APPROACH WILL COMBINE ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORK (ANN) WITH PHYSICS-BASED
REDUCTION, INCLUDING REACTION RATES AND SENSITIVITY.

3) YEARS 1 AND 2: FACILITATE CHEMISTRY MODEL INTEGRATION IN EDNS DEVELOPMENT. WHEN NECESSARY, ENABLE SENSITIVITY ANALYSIS TO IDENTIFY KEY REACTIONS USING VALIDATED MATHEMATICAL APPROACHES TO BE
DEVELOPED BY THE MURI COLLABORATORS.

4) YEAR 1-3: FACILITATE COMBUSTION CLOSURE MODEL DEVELOPMENT.

5) YEARS 3-5: CHEMISTRY MODEL REFINEMENT WITH AN EMPHASIS ON UNCERTAINTY QUANTIFICATION AND MINIMIZATION.

THE ABOVE TASK SCHEDULE WILL BE MODIFIED AS NEEDED IN CONSULTATION AND DISCUSSION WITH THE PI AND TEAM MEMBERS.

subaward_description:
AGREEMENT: $401,517
AMEND1: $401,517 (P700000676)
AMEND2: $401,517 (P700000676)

EXPERIMENTS AT SITE 2 CONDUCTED BY DR. HEIDI REESINK ARE FOR THE PREVENTION OF PATHOLOGICAL FEATURES OF OA WILL BE THE PRIMARY ENDPOINT IN THIS MODEL. LAMENESS, RESPONSE TO FLEXION, AND SYNOVIAL EFFUSION WILL BE USED TO MONITOR THE CLINICAL SIGNS OF OA PROGRESSION IN
EXPERIMENTAL ANIMALS. RADIOGRAPHIC AND HIGH FIELD MAGNETIC RESONANCE IMAGING TOGETHER ASSESSMENTS OF SYNOVIAL FLUID CYTOLOGY AND CYTOKINE/CHEMOKINE PROFILE, AND SERUM BIOMARKER EXPRESSION (INCLUDING LEVELS OF PROSTAGLANDIN E2, AND TYPE I AND II COLLAGEN DEGRADATION FRAGMENTS (C1,2C), EPITOPES CS846 AND CPII) WILL ALSO BE EMPLOYED. AT THE COMPLETION OF THE STUDY, POST-MORTEM EXAMINATIONS WILL INCLUDE SEMI-QUANTITATIVE HISTOLOGICAL ASSESSMENT EMPLOYING A MODIFIED MANKIN SCORING SYSTEM, MOLECULAR PROFILING OF CARTILAGE AND
SYNOVIAL FLUID AND BIOMECHANICAL CHARACTERIZATION OF HEALTHY AND DAMAGED TISSUE. ACUTE TOXICITY AND IMMUNE RESPONSE TO HDV WILL ALSO BE ASSESSED BASED ON SYSTEMIC BLOOD COUNTS, METABOLIC PANEL, AND ANTIBODY DEVELOPMENT TO HDV. TISSUES WILL BE COLLECTED FROM ALL ORGANS FOR QUANTIFICATION OF TOXICITY AND VECTOR DISSEMINATION BY REAL TIME PCR QUANTIFICATION OF VECTOR DNA. EPRG4 EXPRESSION WILL BE ASSESSED IN SYNOVIAL JOINT FLUID BY ELISA AND OF JOINT TISSUES BY IMMUNOSTAINING.

subawardee_name: THE JOHNS HOPKINS UNIVERSITY APPLIED PHYSICS LABORATORY LLC
subawardee_parent_name: THE HENRY M JACKSON FOUNDATION FOR THE ADVANCEMENT OF MILITARY MEDICINE INC
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT FOR PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
INTRODUCTION/BACKGROUND:
THIS RESEARCH EFFORT WITH THE HENRY M. JACKSON FOUNDATION FOR THE ADVANCEMENT OF MILITARY MEDICINE, INC. (HJF), THE UNIFORMED SERVICES UNIVERSITY OF THE HEALTH SCIENCES (USUHS), AND THE JOHNS HOPKINS UNIVERSITY APPLIED PHYSICS LABORATORY (JHU/APL) WILL DEVELOP ADVANCED PROSTHETIC AND REHABILITATION TECHNOLOGIES TO IMPROVE FUNCTIONAL RECOVERY FOR WARFIGHTERS.
THE COLLABORATING TEAMS ACROSS HJF, USUHS, AND JHU/APL HAVE SUCCESSFULLY COMPLETED NUMEROUS
RESEARCH EFFORTS OVER THE PAST SEVERAL YEARS TO AID IN THE ADVANCEMENT AND DEVELOPMENT OF NOVEL
REHABILITATION AND PROSTHETIC LIMB TECHNOLOGIES. NAMELY, TWO PARTICIPANTS COMPLETED PILOT STUDIES TO TAKEHOME AND EVALUATE THE MODULAR PROSTHETIC LIMB IN AN EXTENDED AT-HOME STUDY. IN RECENT PROJECTS, NONINVASIVE SKIN STIMULATION WAS SHOWN FOR RESTORING SENSORY PERCEPTIONS OF TEMPERATURE TO THE PHANTOM LIMB OF AMPUTEES, AND FLEXIBLE TEXTILE-BASED ELECTROMYOGRAPHY (EMG) SLEEVES WERE DEVELOPED AND DEMONSTRATED FOR PROSTHESIS CONTROL. GOING BEYOND PROSTHETIC LIMB TECHNOLOGIES, RECENT COLLABORATIVE EFFORTS
HAVE ALSO LED TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF NOVEL VIRTUAL REALITY REHABILITATION SCENARIOS FOR AT-HOME UPPER LIMB EXERCISES TO PROMOTE RECOVERY AND RETURN TO FUNCTION THROUGH GAMIFICATION OF THERAPEUTIC MOVEMENTS. IN THE PROPOSED WORK, WE WILL CONTINUE PROGRESSING TOWARDS NOVEL TECHNOLOGIES AND SOLUTIONS THAT AIM TO AUGMENT, RESTORE, AND ENABLE FUNCTIONAL REHABILITATION FOR INDIVIDUALS WITH MUSCULOSKELETAL INJURIES, NEUROMUSCULAR DISORDERS, OR LIMB LOSS. IN OUR WORK WE WILL ASSESS TOOLS AND METHODS FOR IMPROVING CRITICAL METRICS, SUCH AS FUNCTIONAL RESTORATION, TASK PERFORMANCE, AND REHABILITATION OUTCOMES.
THE PRIMARY GOAL OF THE PROPOSED WORK WILL BE TO DEVELOP AND TEST NEW WEARABLE AND REAL-TIME PERFORMANCE MONITORING TECHNOLOGIES THAT ENABLE SUCCESSFUL REHABILITATION OUTCOMES AND PROMOTION OF FUNCTIONAL INDEPENDENCE FOR INDIVIDUALS WITH OSSEOATTACHED PROSTHETICS AND UPPER LIMB SENSORIMOTOR DEFICIT IN AN EFFORT
TO MORE RAPIDLY ENABLE A RETURN TO DUTY AND COMMUNITY REINTEGRATION. WE AIM TO LEVERAGE OUR EXPERTISE WITH UPPER LIMB PROSTHESIS AND REHABILITATION TECHNOLOGY, PHYSIOLOGICAL SIGNAL ACQUISITION AND PROCESSING, NOVEL WEARABLE GARMENTS, AND OSSEOINTEGRATION IMPLANTS TO DEVELOP AND VALIDATE NEW SYSTEMS AND TECHNOLOGY FOR FUNCTIONAL RESTORATION IN INDIVIDUALS WITH MUSCULOSKELETAL INJURIES. SPECIFICALLY, OUR CAPABILITIES ENABLE US TO INVESTIGATE THE TECHNICAL FEASIBILITY OF NEW APPROACHES AND ADVANCED WEARABLE AND REAL-TIME PERFORMANCE
MONITORING PROTOTYPES FOR THE PURPOSE OF RESTORING AND AUGMENTING FUNCTIONALITY AND REHABILITATION FOR
WOUNDED WARFIGHTERS INCLUDING NOVEL NON-PHARMACEUTICAL APPROACHES TO PAIN MANAGEMENT
THE DATA AND RESULTS GENERATED AS PART OF THIS STUDY WILL BE USED TO DEMONSTRATE TECHNICAL FEASIBILITY OF
EMERGING REHABILITATION TECHNOLOGIES INCLUDING CUSTOM OI LOAD SENSING COMPONENTRY AND RELATED DEVICES, WHILE MOTIVATING THE INVESTIGATION OF FURTHER RESEARCH THROUGH TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT, BENCHTOP VALIDATION AND CLINICAL EXPERIMENTS. A PRIMARY FOCUS OF THIS WORK WILL ALSO BE ON AS THE DESIGN OF WEARABLE DEVICES FOR REAL-TIME MONITORING OF OSSEOINTEGRATED IMPLANT LOADING AND TRAINING AND TRACKING PHYSIOLOGICAL PERFORMANCE IN REAL-WORLD USE SCENARIOS. THIS FOCUS CONTINUES TO EXPAND OUR ONGOING EXPLORATION OF SYSTEMS
THAT PROMOTE REHABILITATION, RESTORATION, AND RETURN TO ACTIVE DUTY. OTHER RESEARCH ACTIVITIES COULD INCLUDE INTEGRATION OF HAPTIC FEEDBACK AND OTHER PERFORMANCE MONITORING WEARABLES WITH VIRTUAL REALITY MOBILITY REHABILITATION TOWARD SYSTEMS BASED HOLISTIC SENSORY MOTOR RESTORATION.
EXECUTION OF THE RESEARCH PLAN IS BASED ON CLOSE COLLABORATION WITH WALTER REED NATIONAL MILITARY MEDICAL CENTER (WRNMMC), HJF, AND USUHS PERSONNEL FOR SUBJECT RECRUITMENT AND CONTINUED IRB APPROVALS, WHICH ARE NECESSARY IN ORDER TO CONDUCT THE PROPOSED RESEARCH.

subaward_description:
SCOPE OF WORK:
THE NATIONAL CENTER FOR DISASTER MEDICINE AND PUBLIC HEALTH’S INTENT IS TO EXECUTE NCR NDMS PILOT SITE OPERATIONS AND ACTIVITIES COLLABORATIVELY WITH GWU AND THE NDMS PILOT FIELD IMPLEMENTATION TEAM (FIT). IN COLLABORATION WITH THE NDMS PILOT PROGRAM FIT, GWU WILL SUPPORT THE MANAGEMENT AND EXECUTION OF NCR NDMS PILOT SITE CORE COMPETENCIES THROUGH REGULAR STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENTS, NDMS RESPONSE PLAN IMPROVEMENT SUPPORT, AS WELL AS THE PLANNING AND EXECUTION OF HOMELAND SECURITY EXERCISE & EVALUATION PROGRAM (HSEEP) COMPLIANT NDMS EXERCISES WITH PARTNERS AND STAKEHOLDERS. AS THE NDMS PILOT IS PRIMARILY FOCUSED ON RESEARCH, EDUCATION, AND TRAINING FOR AN NDMS RESPONSE, ALL PILOT SITE ACTIVITIES SHOULD BE COMPLETED IN DIRECT SUPPORT, AND UNDER THE GUIDANCE AND DIRECTION, OF LOCAL STAKEHOLDERS AND FEDERAL ENTITIES (E.G., ASPR NDMS, VHA OEM, FEMA, USNORTHCOM, ETC.) RESPONSIBLE FOR EXECUTING AN ACTIVATION OF THE NCR FCC COORDINATORS IN THE REGION AND FOLLOW-ON NDMS OPERATIONS . THE PURPOSE AND INTENT OF TRANSITIONING PILOT PROGRAM EFFORTS IS TO ESTABLISH A PARTNERSHIP WITH
A TRUSTED AND WELL INTEGRATED LOCAL ORGANIZATION THAT WILL BE ABLE TO MANAGE AND SUSTAIN THE IMPROVEMENTS IN LOCAL NDMS RESPONSE CAPABILITIES ACHIEVED UNDER THE PILOT. NONE OF THE REQUIREMENTS IN THIS SOW SHOULD BE INTERPRETED AS DIRECTION FOR GWU OR ANY LOCAL PARTNER TO ENCROACH OR CIRCUMVENT THE AUTHORITY OF ANY NCR NDMS STAKEHOLDER TO EXECUTE THEIR ASSIGNED RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER THE NDMS.

prime_award_cfda_numbers_and_titles: 12.800: AIR FORCE DEFENSE RESEARCH SCIENCES PROGRAM
subawardee_business_types: NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
DESIGNING A MATERIAL THAT SATISFIES MULTIPLE APPLICATION-SPECIFIC PROPERTY AND PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS IS
NON-TRIVIAL. WHILE SOME INTUITION-BASED GUIDANCE ON THE NEEDED PROPERTY AND PERFORMANCE METRICS MAY BE
AVAILABLE, THE SEARCH PROCESS TO FINDING A MATERIAL THAT MEETS THOSE NEEDS IS CONFOUNDED BY THE LACK OF
EFFICIENT AND ACCURATE PROPERTY/PERFORMANCE PREDICTORS, AND THE TYPICALLY LIMITLESS CHEMICAL AND PROCESS
VARIABLE SEARCH SPACE. MACHINE LEARNING (ML) MODELS, TRAINED ON AVAILABLE PROPERTY DATA OBTAINED EITHER
THROUGH EXPERIMENTAL MEASUREMENTS OR VIA COMPUTER SIMULATIONS, CAN SIGNIFICANTLY ALLEVIATE THIS BURDEN [1,
2]. WITHIN THE CONTEXT OF THIS SPACE-MAT PROPOSAL, INFORMATICS TOOLS WILL BE APPLIED TO (1) DEVELOP DATADRIVEN
(AND POSSIBLY PHYSICS-ENFORCED) ML MODELS TO PREDICT THE EFFECT OF THE FULL LEO ENVIRONMENT ON THE
PROPERTIES AND LIFETIMES OF POLYMERS (INCLUDING POLYIMIDE-BASED POLYMERS SUCH AS KAPTON), AND (2) DESIGN
NEW-TO-THE-WORLD POLYMERS THAT MEET THE GROWING NEED OF LEO ENVIRONMENTS THAT SURPASS CURRENT STANDARD
AVAILABLE/KNOWN MATERIALS. THESE ML INNOVATIONS ARE EXPECTED TO GUIDE NEXT EXPERIMENTS BY
RECOMMENDING PROCESS CONDITIONS AND CHEMISTRIES TO BE CONSIDERED ITERATIVELY.

subawardee_dba_name: OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY OFFICE OF SPONSORED PROGRAMS, THE
subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
THE OHIO REGIONAL TBI MODEL SYSTEM (ORTBIMS) AT THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY (OSU) WILL RECRUIT AND ENROLL SUBJECTS INTO ENRICH BRAIN HEALTH PROJECT 2 BY LEVERAGING EXISTING DATA IN THE TBIMS NATIONAL DATABASE. OSU WILL SECURE REGULATORY APPROVALS, MAINTAIN REGULATORY COMPLIANCE, RE-CONTACT TBIMS ENROLLEES FOR ADDITIONAL DATA COLLECTION (AIM 2 AND AIM 3), COLLECT DATA PER STANDARDIZED PROTOCOLS, ENTER DATA INTO FORMS CREATED BY THE TBIMS NATIONAL DATA AND STATISTICAL CENTER, AND CONTRIBUTE TO ANALYTIC INTERPRETATION, MANUSCRIPT PREPARATION AND RESULTS DISSEMINATION.

THE ORTBIMS WILL CONDUCT AT LEAST 50 INTERVIEWS FOR AIM 2, AND 20 INTERVIEWS FOR AIM 3. THE ORTBIMS HAS AGREED UPON A PER-SUBJECT REIMBURSEMENT PLAN AND HAS CALCULATED THE HOURLY EFFORT REQUIRED TO SECURE AND MAINTAIN IRB APPROVAL (AMENDMENT TO EXISTING TBIMS PROTOCOL), CONTACT PARTICIPANTS, OBTAIN INFORMED CONSENT, AND CONDUCT TELEPHONE INTERVIEWS.

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
WE AGREE TO RECRUIT AND ENROLL SUBJECTS INTO THE ENRICH BRAIN HEALTH PROJECT 2, WHICH LEVERAGES EXISTING DATA IN THE TBIMS NATIONAL DATABASE. WE WILL SECURE REGULATORY APPROVALS, RE-CONTACT TBIMS ENROLLEES FOR NEW DATA COLLECTION, COLLECT DATA PER STANDARDIZED PROTOCOLS, ENTER DATA INTO FORMS CREATED BY THE TBIMS NATIONAL DATA AND STATISTICAL CENTER, AND CONTRIBUTE TO MANUSCRIPT PREPARATION AND RESULTS DISSEMINATION.

WE WILL CONDUCT AT LEAST 50 INTERVIEWS OF PARTICIPANTS WITH LONGITUDINAL PERFORMANCE-BASED COGNITIVE DATA (BRIEF TEST OF ADULT COGNITION BY TELEPHONE) AT 1, 2, AND 5 YEARS TO COLLECT NEW DATA ON POTENTIALLY-MODIFIABLE RISK AND PROTECTIVE FACTORS FOR COGNITIVE DECLINE. WE WILL ALSO RE-CONTACT AT LEAST 20 TBIMS PARTICIPANTS WHO ENDORSED HIGH-RISK SUICIDAL IDEATION AND/OR SUICIDE ATTEMPT AT A PRIOR STUDY VISIT TO COLLECT DATA ON CURRENT SUICIDALITY AND IDENTIFY MODIFIABLE RISK AND PROTECTIVE FACTORS. IT IS ANTICIPATED THAT APPROXIMATELY 4-5 INDIVIDUALS WILL BE ELIGIBLE FOR BOTH STUDIES.

subawardee_parent_name: THE HENRY M JACKSON FOUNDATION FOR THE ADVANCEMENT OF MILITARY MEDICINE INC
subawardee_business_types: OTHER NOT FOR PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,VETERINARY COLLEGE
subaward_description:
SCOPE OF WORK:
THE NATIONAL CENTER FOR DISASTER MEDICINE AND PUBLIC HEALTH’S INTENT IS TO EXECUTE SACRAMENTO NDMS PILOT SITE OPERATIONS AND ACTIVITIES COLLABORATIVELY WITH UC DAVIS AND THE NDMS PILOT FIELD IMPLEMENTATION TEAM (FIT). IN COLLABORATION WITH THE NDMS PILOT PROGRAM FIT, UC DAVIS WILL SUPPORT THE MANAGEMENT AND EXECUTION OF SACRAMENTO NDMS PILOT SITE CORE COMPETENCIES THROUGH REGULAR STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENTS, NDMS RESPONSE PLAN IMPROVEMENT SUPPORT, AS WELL AS THE PLANNING AND EXECUTION OF HOMELAND SECURITY EXERCISE & EVALUATION PROGRAM (HSEEP) COMPLIANT NDMS EXERCISES WITH PARTNERS AND STAKEHOLDERS. AS THE NDMS PILOT IS PRIMARILY FOCUSED ON RESEARCH, EDUCATION, AND TRAINING FOR AN NDMS RESPONSE, ALL PILOT SITE ACTIVITIES SHOULD BE COMPLETED IN DIRECT SUPPORT, AND UNDER THE GUIDANCE AND DIRECTION, OF LOCAL STAKEHOLDERS AND FEDERAL ENTITIES (E.G., ASPR NDMS, VHA OEM, FEMA, USNORTHCOM, ETC.) RESPONSIBLE FOR EXECUTING AN ACTIVATION OF THE SACRAMENTO FCC AND FOLLOW-ON NDMS OPERATIONS. THE PURPOSE AND INTENT OF TRANSITIONING PILOT PROGRAM EFFORTS IS TO ESTABLISH A PARTNERSHIP WITH A TRUSTED AND WELL-INTEGRATED LOCAL ORGANIZATION THAT WILL BE ABLE TO MANAGE AND SUSTAIN THE IMPROVEMENTS IN LOCAL NDMS RESPONSE CAPABILITIES ACHIEVED UNDER THE PILOT. NONE OF THE
REQUIREMENTS IN THIS SOW SHOULD BE INTERPRETED AS DIRECTION FOR UC DAVIS OR ANY LOCAL PARTNER TO
ENCROACH OR CIRCUMVENT THE AUTHORITY OF ANY SACRAMENTO NDMS STAKEHOLDER TO EXECUTE THEIR ASSIGNED RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER THE NDMS.

subawardee_parent_name: THE HENRY M JACKSON FOUNDATION FOR THE ADVANCEMENT OF MILITARY MEDICINE INC
subaward_description:
INTRODUCTION/BACKGROUND:
THE HENRY M. JACKSON FOUNDATION FOR THE ADVANCEMENT OF MILITARY MEDICINE, INC. (HJF) WILL UTILIZE THE EXPERTISE OF ANGELA CONNOR AT RESCUE 22 FOUNDATION, HEREAFTER REFERRED TO AS “SUBRECIPIENT”, TO MEET THE OBJECTIVES IDENTIFIED IN THE BELOW REFERENCED PROJECT. SPECIFICALLY, SUBRECIPIENT WILL PURSUE COMPLETION OF THE WORK OUTLINED BELOW.
THE WWSDP’S OVERARCHING PURPOSE IS TO INCREASE CAPACITY AND CAPABILITY OF NON-PROFIT SERVICE DOG
TRAINING ORGANIZATIONS (SDTOS) TO RAISE, TRAIN, AND FURNISH SERVICE DOGS TO ELIGIBLE SERVICE MEMBERS
(SMS) AND VETERANS.
IN FURTHERANCE OF THIS OBJECTIVE, THE SUBRECIPIENT WILL:
1. EXECUTE A SUBAGREEMENT WITH THE HENRY M. JACKSON FOUNDATION (HJF)
2. TRAIN SERVICE DOGS IN ACCORDANCE WITH APPLICABLE BEST PRACTICES AND ACCREDITATION STANDARDS.
3. FURNISH SERVICE DOGS TO ELIGIBLE SERVICE MEMBERS (SMS) AND VETERANS.
4. IMPLEMENT AND PROVIDE EVIDENCE OF IMPLEMENTATION FOR BEST PRACTICES (SEE APPENDIX A) BY THE END OF QUARTER 1 IN COLLABORATION WITH CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY LONG BEACH RESEARCH
FOUNDATION (CSULB RF).
5. EVALUATE BEST PRACTICES ON AN ONGOING BASIS THROUGHOUT THE PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE IN COLLABORATION WITH CSULB RF.
6. PARTICIPATE IN MONTHLY MEETINGS WITH CSULB RF TO ADDRESS #4 AND #5 ABOVE.
7. PARTICIPATE IN QUARTERLY MEETINGS WITH THE HJF PROJECT MANAGER TO MONITOR PROGRESS.
8. INITIATE TIMELY COMMUNICATION WITH THE HJF PROJECT MANAGER BETWEEN QUARTERLY MEETINGS TO
PROVIDE PROGRESS UPDATES AND ADDRESS ANY POTENTIAL CHALLENGES OR DELAYS.
9. SUBMIT QUARTERLY REPORTS TO HJF UTILIZING THE PROVIDED TEMPLATE OR ELECTRONIC FORM PER HJF PROJECT MANAGER GUIDANCE.
10. ATTEND AND PARTICIPATE IN THE FOCUS GROUP DURING QUARTER 4 TO DISCUSS AND VOTE ON BEST PRACTICES.

subaward_description:
SCOPE OF WORK -SAGE BIONETWORKS
PI: ROBERT ALLAWAY
PROJECT TITLE: EXPLOITING ANEUPLOIDY IN NF1-MPNST:PROGNOSTICATION AND UNDERSTANDING
PATHOGENESIS
START AND END DATES: 9/1/22 – 8/30/25
PROJECT DESCRIPTION:
1. SUBRECIPIENT PROPOSED SCOPE
SAGE BIONETWORKS WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR DISSEMINATION OF RAW DATA GENERATED BY THIS
WORK. SAGE BIONETWORKS WILL COLLABORATE WITH THE HIRBE LAB AND OTHER DATA GENERATORS
INVOLVED IN THIS PROJECT TO MAKE THE DATA FINDABLE BY ASSIGNING APPROPRIATE ANNOTATIONS TO
THE DATA USING THE NF OPEN SCIENCE INITIATIVE DATA CONTRIBUTION AND METADATA STANDARDS.
SAGE WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR ENSURING THAT THE DATASETS GENERATED BY THIS WORK WILL BE
SHARED IN A TIMELY FASHION ON APPROPRIATE REPOSITORIES SUCH AS THE NF DATA PORTAL.
2. SUBRECIPIENT PROPOSED TASKS
SAGE BIONETWORKS WILL:
○ CREATE A DATA SHARING WORKSPACE ON THE SYNAPSE PLATFORM FOR THIS PROJECT
○ PROVIDE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO UPLOAD THE DATA IN THIS PROJECT
○ ANNOTATE THE DATA WITH METADATA PROVIDED BY THE PROJECT PI
○ ENSURE THE DATA IS IN A REUSABLE FORMAT
○ SHARE THE DATA (COPY NUMBER DATA, SINGLE CELL SEQUENCING, SPATIAL PROTEOMICS,
LOW PASS WHOLE GENOME SEQUENCING FROM CELL FREE DNA, FLUORESCENT IN SITU
HYBRIDIZATION (FISH), CLINICAL METADATA) ON THE NF DATA PORTAL
○ MAKE THE DATA PUBLICLY ACCESSIBLE WHEN APPROVED BY THE PROJECT PI
3. SUBRECIPIENT EXPECTED DELIVERABLES
○ A DATA SHARING WORKSPACE (SYNAPSE PROJECT) CONTAINING THE STUDY DATA
○ AN NF DATA PORTAL STUDY PAGE DESCRIBING THE STUDY, DATA, AND PUBLICATIONS
○ A DIGITAL OBJECT IDENTIFIER TO CITE THE FINAL DATASET AND METADATA
4. SUBRECIPIENT EXPECTED TIMELINE
○ WE ANTICIPATE PERFORMING THIS WORK WITHIN 1 YEAR OF DATA GENERATION, WITH THE
EXCEPTION OF MAKING THE DATA PUBLICLY AVAILABLE, WHICH WILL OCCUR WHEN DIRECTED BY
DR. HIRBE AND AT THE TIME OF PUBLICATION OF THE PROJECT FINDINGS

subaward_description: THIS PROPOSAL DOVETAILS WITH AN INDEPENDENT BUT HIGHLY COMPLEMENTARY PROPOSAL BY INVESTIGATORS NATARAJAN AND RAVINDRAN, WHICH EMPHASIZES THE ROLE OF HUMAN INTERACTION IN THE LEARNING OF RELATIONAL POLICIES. THEIR EFFORTS INCLUDE AN EXPLANATION MODULE THAT EXPLAINS ITS REASONING, AND AN ELICITATION MODULE THAT ACCEPTS DIFFERENT KINDS OF GOALS, PREFERENCES AND ADVICE FROM THE HUMAN USERS AND USES THEM TO ADAPT THE SYSTEM'S BEHAVIOR. TO MAXIMIZE THE SYNERGISTIC EFFECT OF THE COMPLEMENTARY EFFORTS, THE TWO TEAMS WILL MEET ONCE A WEEK (VIRTUALLY) AND ONCE A YEAR (IN PERSON) FOR COLLABORATION MEETINGS. IN ADDITION THE TEAMS WILL MEET EVERY YEAR IN DIFFERENT AI CONFERENCES TO DISCUSS THE NEXT STEPS IN THE COLLABORATION. WE WILL HAVE A SHARED GITHUB REPOSITORY WHERE THE CODEBASE WILL BE MAINTAINED AND DEVELOPED BY GRADUATE STUDENTS. OUR TEAMS WILL COLLABORATE CLOSELY ON EVALUATIONS OF ALL ASPECTS OF THE COMBINED SYSTEM. OSU-SPECIFIC WORK IN THIS PROJECT INCLUDES DEVELOPING NEW MODELS OF DECISION MAKING AND LEARNING THAT COMBINE NEURAL NETWORKS WITH RELATIONAL REPRESENTATIONS AND HIERARCHICAL PLANNING ALGORITHMS.

subaward_description:
WE ADDRESS OUR RESEARCH QUESTIONS THROUGH A NOVEL DISTRIBUTED BUT COLLECTIVE INTELLIGENCE (DCI) FRAMEWORK.
THE BUILDING BLOCK OF THIS FRAMEWORK INVOLVES TRANSLATING SPATIAL AND TEMPORAL INFORMATION INTO
HYPERDIMENSIONAL SPACES. WITHIN THESE SPACES, WE UTILIZE MULTI-LEVEL VECTOR SYMBOLIC ARCHITECTURES (VSAS)
TO ANALYZE THEM. THIS IS ACHIEVED BY ENCODING VARIOUS INPUTS LIKE MULTI-DIMENSIONAL PERCEPTIONS, RGB
CAMERAS, INFRARED SENSORS, TEMPERATURE SENSORS, AND ENERGY METRICS INTO SYMBOLIC VECTORS. THIS INCREASES
SITUATIONAL AWARENESS, REDUCES SOLDIER LOAD, AND ENABLES GREATER LEVELS OF SUSTAINABILITY AND
MANEUVERABILITY ON THE BATTLEFIELD. WE EXPECT VSAS TO PROVIDE A DISTRIBUTED MEANS OF MEMORY,
COMPUTATION, AND INTELLIGENCE FOR BOTH THE GROUP AND ASSET-LEVEL LIFELONG LEARNING POLICIES.

subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
OBJECTIVE OR PURPOSE: THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP AN ELECTRONIC SYSTEM FOR PERFORMING ANALOG-DIGITAL BEAMFORMING WITH A SERIES OF BENEFITS FOR ELECTRONIC WARFARE. RESEARCH TASKS TO BE CARRIED OUT AT UNIVERSITY OF ARKANSAS (UARK) FOCUS ON CHIP-3 IMPLEMENTING A PROGRAMMABLE DIGITAL ASIC AFFT ARCHITECTURE.

THIS PROJECT FOCUSES ON THE DESIGN, TAPEOUT, AND TESTING OF MULTIPLE DIGITAL APPLICATION-SPECIFIC INTEGRATED CIRCUIT (ASIC) CHIPLETS FOR PROCESSING HIGH-SPEED DIGITAL BEAM DATA.

A LIST OF DETAILED WORK REQUIREMENTS:

TASK 1: INITIAL IMPLEMENTATION OF AFFT 
UARK WILL WORK WITH FIU TO SYNTHESIZE THE AFFT ALGORITHM INTO GATE-LEVEL NETLISTS WITH PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION USING SYNOPSYS TOOLSET.
DELIVERABLE: A GATE-LEVEL NETLIST IMPLEMENTING AFFT

TASK 2: PHYSICAL IMPLEMENTATION OF AFFT 
UARK WILL PERFORM PHYSICAL DESIGN OF AFFT, E.G., FLOORPLANNING, PLACE-AND-ROUTE, DRC/LVS CHECKS, IR DROP ANALYSIS, SIGNAL INTEGRITY CHECK, PARASITIC EXTRACTION, SDF BACKANNOTATED SIMULATION, ETC.
DELIVERABLE: A GDS-II FILE IMPLEMENTING AFFT

TASK 3: PERFORMANCE EVALUATION AND OPTIMIZATION 
UARK WILL CONDUCT PERFORMANCE EVALUATION OF THE DESIGNED AFFT CIRCUIT IN SPEED, ACTIVE POWER, AND LEAKAGE POWER. TRADEOFFS WILL BE EVALUATED FOR OPTIMIZATIONS.
DELIVERABLE: PERFORMANCE EVALUATION RESULTS WITH ANY OPTIMIZATION

TASK 4: INTERFACING WITH CHIP-2 
UARK WILL WORK WITH BNL TO CONDUCT FLOOWPLANNING AND ASIC SYNTHESIS OF THE SERDES CIRCUITRY USING 3RD-PARTY IPS.
DELIVERABLE: IMPLEMENTATION FILES OF SERDES

subaward_description:
IN THIS RESEARCH PROGRAM, THE UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO WILL UNDERTAKE THE FOLLOWING TASKS:
PHASE 1
TASK 1: TESTING AND EVALUATION OF INTEGRATED PHOTONIC CHIPS
RESPONSIBILITY: UNIV. OF COLORADO, DIDDAMS AND STUDENT
DESCRIPTION: WORK WITH UNIVERSITY OF VIRGINIA AND OTHER TEAM MEMBERS TO EVALUATE
FABRICATED SIN CHIPS FOR PROPERTIES REQUIRED FOR EFFICIENT SQUEEZED LIGHT GENERATION.
TASK 1.1: MEASUREMENT OF LOSS AND EFFICIENCY OF PHOTONIC INTEGRATED CIRCUITS:
UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO WILL SCREEN CHIPS AND CHARACTERIZE DEVICES TO RAPIDLY DETERMINE
THOSE DEVICES WITH OPTIMAL PROPERTIES FOR SUBSEQUENT SQUEEZED LIGHT GENERATION AND

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
THIS STATEMENT OF WORK (SOW) OUTLINES THE ROLE THAT PROF. DIRK ENGLUNDÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S GROUP AT THE
MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (MIT) WILL HAVE WITHIN THE TECHNICAL PROPOSAL
ÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã…Â€ŒTOWARDS A PROGRAMMABLE PLASMONIC INFORMATION PROCESSOR BASED ON GRAPHENE, 2D
MATERIALS, AND RARE-EARTH ATOMSÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½, AFOSR FUNDING OPPORTUNITY #FA9550-23-S-0001.
THE GOAL OF THIS PROPOSAL IS TO ADDRESS THE TECHNOLOGICAL GAP OF IMPLEMENTING SCALABLE
COHERENT INTERACTIONS BETWEEN OPTICAL FIELDS AND ATOM-LIKE SYSTEMS. THE INTENDED
APPROACH TOWARDS THE ACCOMPLISHMENT OF THIS GOAL IS BASED ON THE IMPLEMENTATION OF
ACOUSTIC GRAPHENE SURFACE PLASMON POLARITONS (AGSPP) DEVICES. MIT WILL WORK WITH THE
UNIVERSITY OF CENTRAL FLORIDA (UCF), HEREBY REPRESENTED BY PROF. MICHAEL N.
LEUENBERGER, IN A COLLABORATIVE EFFORT TO ADDRESS THE TECHNOLOGICAL CHALLENGES THAT THIS
PROJECT PRESENTS.
IN PARTICULAR, MIT WILL HAVE AN ACTIVE ROLE IN:
- DESIGNING ACOUSTIC GRAPHENE SURFACE PLASMON POLARITONS (AGSPP) PROCESSING BLOCKS
AND MESH CIRCUITRY;
- FABRICATING METAL-INSULATOR-METAL (MIM) AND METAL-INSULATOR-GRAPHENE (MIG)
VERTICALLY STACKED PLASMONIC PLATFORMS FOR THE PURPOSE OF A PROGRAMMABLE PLASMONIC
INFORMATION PROCESSOR;
- PROVIDING AND INVESTIGATING PHOTONICS STRUCTURES SUCH AS WAVEGUIDES INTEGRATED WITH
ERBIUM IONS IN SILICON CHIPS FOR INTEGRATED PLASMONIC CIRCUITS;
- INVESTIGATING AND VERIFYING DEVICES BASED ON AGSPP FOR BOTH CLASSICAL AND QUANTUM
INFORMATION PROCESSING;
- DEVELOPING NON-VOLATILE PLASMONIC MEMORIES USING AN APPROACH BASED ON PHASE-
CHANGE MATERIALS;
- CHARACTERIZING SI PHOTODETECTORS FOR REFINING THE MULTISCALE MODEL;
- FABRICATING AND CHARACTERIZING METAL-NONLINEAR MATERIAL HETEROSTRUCTURE USING HIGH-
RESOLUTION-MICROSCOPE-SPECTROSCOPY SYSTEMS AND A SET OF TUNABLE LASER SOURCES;
- GROWING REA-DOPED TRANSITION METAL DICHALCOGENIDES (TMD) DEVICES;
- CHARACTERIZING REA SPINS AND DEMONSTRATING SINGLE-PHOTON, SINGLE-PLASMON,
ENTANGLED-PHOTON, AND ENTANGLED-PLASMON SOURCES.

subaward_description: THE WOOLF LAB AT BOSTON CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL WILL CONTRIBUTE TO THE IDENTIFICATION OF TARGETS SUITABLE FOR NOCICEPTOR SELECTIVITY BY CONDUCTING NUC-SEQ RUNS AND ANALYSES OF RNA TRANSCRIPTS IN HUMAN AND MOUSE DRGS AND OF IPSC DERIVED NEURONS, AND BY COLLECTING TISSUE/CELLS FOR PROTEOMIC ANALYSIS. THE LAB WILL DEVELOP AND OPTIMIZE ALL PHENOTYPIC ASSAYS (GCAMP) IN HUMAN IPSC DERIVED NOCICEPTORS FOR BASAL AND DISEASE-BASED ALTERED EXCITABILITY, AND THOSE FOR NEUROPEPTIDE RELEASE AND IMMUNE CELL ACTIVATION, AND WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR SUPERVISING THE GENERATION/DIFFERENTIATION OF ALL IPSC DERIVED NEURONS BY THE BCH HUMAN NEURON CORE. THE LAB WILL CONDUCT ALL PATCH SEQ EXPERIMENTS TO LINK EXCITABILITY AND GENE EXPRESSION. THE LAB WILL SUPERVISE THE RUNNING OF FIVE PHENOTYPIC SCREENS ON IPSC DERIVED NOCICEPTORS BY THE BCH ASSAY DEVELOPMENT AND SCREENING FACILITY AND WILL RUN ALL THE TARGET VALIDATION STUDIES USING CRISPR KNOCKOUT/SHRNA KNOCKDOWN AND WHERE AVAILABLE, DRUGS/COMPOUNDS AS WELL AS ANALYSES OF COMBINATORIAL ACTIONS ON THE TARGETS. ALL PARALLEL TARGET-BASED SCREENS USING ION FLUX MEASUREMENTS (30 SCREENS) WILL BE RUN BY THE LAB WITH THE ASSAY DEVELOPMENT AND SCREENING FACILITY. THE LAB WILL TEST ALL HITS FROM THE SCREENS BOTH IN THE PHENOTYPIC ASSAYS AND IN THE TARGET-BASED ASSAYS USING GCAMP AND MULTIELECTRODE ARRAY AND WILL CONDUCT FULL DOSE RESPONSE ANALYSES AND ENSURE THAT THE COMPOUNDS ARE NOT TOXIC. THE HITS WILL BE RUN THOUGH COUNTER SCREENS WITH HUMAN MOTOR AND CORTICAL NEURONS TO ENSURE SELECTIVITY. ALL COMPOUNDS GENERATED BY THE MEDCHEM AND CHEMISTRY PROGRAMS WILL BE TESTED BY THE WOOLF LAB IN THE PHENOTYPIC AND ION FLUX ASSAYS LOOKING FOR INCREASED POTENCY AND SELECTIVITY. THE LAB WILL CONDUCT ALL THE IN VIVO TESTING OF COMPOUNDS FOR PK, SELECTIVITY, TOLERABILITY AND EFFICACY THE LATTER INVOLVING MODELS OF NOCICEPTIVE, INFLAMMATORY AND NEUROPATHIC PAIN AS WELL AS INFLAMMATION. EFFICACY MEASURES WILL INCLUDE BOTH STANDARD MEASURES AND NEW OBSERVER INDEPENDENT MEASURES OF   SPONTANEOUS BEHAVIOR WITH MACHINE LEARNING AND ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE BASED ANALYSIS.

PERFORMANCE AND CRITICAL EVALUATION OF MATERIALS IN LOW EARTH ORBIT (SPACE-MAT). THE GOALS INCLUDE ESTABLISHING A CENTER OF
EXCELLENCE FOR SCIENCE, PERFORMANCE, AND CRITICAL EVALUATION OF MATERIALS IN LOW EARTH ORBIT (SPACE-MAT) THAT WILL EXPEDITE A
SYNERGISTIC ÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã…Â€ŒCLOSED-LOOPÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½ EFFORT IN LEO MATERIALS DEVELOPMENT THAT INTEGRATES MATERIALS SYNTHESIS, IN-SITU METROLOGY, AND MULTISCALE
EX-SITU CHARACTERIZATION WITH GUIDANCE FROM DATA-DRIVEN AND PHYSICS-BASED MODELS FOR DEGRADATION, PERFORMANCE, AND LIFETIME OF
SEMICONDUCTOR AND POLYMER MATERIALS APPROPRIATE FOR RF REGIMES. SPACE-MAT WILL LEVERAGE WORLD-CLASS EXPERTS AND FACILITIES TO
TRANSFORM THE MULTI-DISCIPLINARY EDUCATION OF THE NEXT GENERATION OF WORK-FORCE, FOSTERING CONTINUOUS INNOVATION IN THIS DOMAIN,
WHILE SIGNIFICANTLY ENHANCING COLLABORATIONS WITH AFRL THROUGH RESEARCHER EXCHANGES, ADJUNCT APPOINTMENTS FOR CO-ADVISING OF
STUDENTS, CO-DEVELOPMENT OF RESEARCH PROJECTS, AND TARGETED SKILL DEVELOPMENT FOR AFRL PERSONNEL, AS APPROPRIATE. RF
COMMUNICATIONS ARE BOTH UBIQUITOUS AND HIGH PRIORITY FOR DOD APPLICATIONS, AND THE CLASSES OF MATERIALS WE PRO-POSE TO
INVESTIGATE HERE ARE INTEGRAL TO IMPROVING LONGEVITY AND PERFORMANCE OF RF COMMUNICATIONS DE-VICES IN LEO VEHICLES. MOREOVER,
THESE MATERIALS ARE WIDELY USED AND SUFFER SIMILAR PERFORMANCE ISSUES IN OTHER LEO SYSTEMS, SUCH THAT IMPROVEMENT HERE HAS
BROAD IMPLICATIONS ACROSS DOD MISSIONS.

subaward_description:
BRINM SOW (MAGNETS)
GERARDO VILLARREAL, MD (SITE PI) IS MEDICAL DIRECTOR OF THE OUTPATIENT AND RURAL MENTAL HEALTH PROGRAM AT NMVAHCS. HE HAS CONDUCTED STUDIES OF NEUROIMAGING, PHARMACOLOGIC AND THERAPEUTIC MODALITIES FOR MILITARY PTSD, INCLUDING RECENT CONTROLLED TRIALS OF QUETIAPINE FOR MILITARY PTSD SYMPTOMS.  AS SITE PI, DR. VILLARREAL WILL OVERSEE ALL ASPECTS OF THE PROJECT AT NMVAHCS, INCLUDING REGULATORY SUBMISSION TO THE LOCAL AND CENTRAL IRBS, COMPLIANCE WITH REGULAR AUDITS AND REPORTING, THE PERFORMANCE OF BUDGETING AND PURCHASING, RESEARCH STAFF HIRING, AND TRAINING, RECRUITMENT AND SCREENING, AND PROTOCOL ADMINISTRATION. HE WILL CO-DIRECT THE AOC AND NRC FOR MAGNETS WITH DR. QUINN, AND WILL SUPERVISE THE ACTIVITIES OF THE RESEARCH COORDINATOR AND RESEARCH TECHNICIAN AT NMVAHCS. AS AN EXPERIENCED MILITARY PTSD CLINICIAN AND RESEARCHER, HE WILL PROVIDE INPUT ON STUDY DESIGN, ENROLLMENT PROCEDURES, SAFETY, CONFIDENTIALITY, AND DATA INTERPRETATION. HE WILL BE THE POINT OF CONTACT FOR THE LIVED EXPERIENCE CONSULTANT, ENSURING A COLLEGIAL AND PRODUCTIVE RELATIONSHIPS. HE WILL PARTICIPATE IN STUDY CONFERENCE CALLS, STUDY VISITS, AND BE INVOLVED IN THE PREPARATION OF PROGRESS REPORTS, MANUSCRIPT PREPARATION, AND PRESENTATION OF THE STUDY'S FINDINGS. HE WILL DEVOTE 10% EFFORT (1.2 CALENDAR MONTHS) TO THIS PROJECT.

SARAH PIRIO RICHARDSON, MD (CO-I) IS A NEUROLOGIST AND AN CHIEF OF THE NEUROLOGY SECTION AT THE NEW MEXICO VETERANS ADMINISTRATION HEALTH CENTER SYSTEM (NMVAHCS). subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
IN THIS PROJECT, WE AIMS TO ESTABLISH A PHYSICS-DRIVEN UNDERSTANDING OF THE ODOR-TRACKING FLAPPING FLIGHT IN NATURE AND UNRAVEL HOW INSECTS BALANCE AERODYNAMIC PERFORMANCE
WITH OLFACTORY SENSITIVITY. THE SPECIFIC RESEARCH OBJECTIVES ARE TO: 1) CHARACTERIZE THE
IMPACT OF UNSTEADY AERODYNAMICS ON WING MECHANOSENSATION; 2) DETERMINE THE
INFLUENCE OF UNSTEADY FLOW ON OLFACTORY SENSATION; 3) ELUCIDATE THE MECHANISMS OF
ODOR-GUIDED FLAPPING FLIGHT BY INTEGRATING MECHANICAL AND ODORANT STIMULUS. THE
PROPOSED STUDY WILL LAY A THEORETICAL AND COMPUTATIONAL FOUNDATION FOR EXPLORING THE
UNDERLYING MECHANISM OF THIS INTRICATE BEHAVIOR. THE PROPOSAL INTEGRATES UNSTEADY
AERODYNAMICS, FLUID-STRUCTURE INTERACTIONS, AND ODORANT TRANSPORT, TO FILL THE KNOWLEDGE
GAP IN THE ODOR-GUIDED AERONAUTIC NAVIGATION OF FLYING INSECTS. THE PROPOSED RESEARCH
REPRESENTS THE FIRST EFFORT TO INCORPORATE THE EFFECTS OF UNSTEADY AERODYNAMICS INTO THE
DISTRIBUTED SENSING SYSTEMS OF FLYING INSECTS. THE FINDINGS WILL ADVANCE THE
DEVELOPMENT OF SCALING LAWS FOR THE DESIGN OF BIO-INSPIRED FLYERS WITH SUPERIOR AERODYNAMIC PERFORMANCE AND OLFACTORY SENSATIONS.

OVERVIEW
THE CENTRE NATIONAL DE FORMATION ET DE RECHERCHE EN SANTÃƑÆ’Ã‚Â© RURALE (CNFRSR) IS A LEADING BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH INSTITUTION IN GUINEA, PERFORMING RESEARCH, TRAINING, AND PROVISION OF HEALTH SERVICES UNDER THE AUTHORITY OF THE MINISTRY OF HEALTH. IT HAS BEEN INVOLVED IN PAST EFFORTS TO IDENTIFY CIRCULATING ZOONOTIC PATHOGENS IN GUINEA WITH US-BASED PARTNERS, SUCH AS THE PREDICT PROJECT (LED BY METABIOTA AND THEN UC DAVIS). CNFRSR HAS ALSO BEEN INVOLVED IN NUMEROUS INITIATIVES RELATED TO VACCINE AND OTHER CLINICAL TRIALS (SUCH AS VIA THE NIAID-LED PREGUI PROGRAM, AS WELL AS A PARTNER IN THE LARGE PREVAC-UP CONSORTIUM). 
ORGANIZATIONAL STRUCTURE

THE NATIONAL CENTER FOR TRAINING AND RESEARCH IN RURAL HEALTH OF MAFÃƑÆ’Ã‚Â¨RINYAH (CNFRSR), WAS CREATED IN 1988 BY DECREE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE REPUBLIC OF GUINEA AND IS A DIVISION OF THE MINISTRY OF HEALTH AND PUBLIC HYGIENE OF GUINEA. IT OFFERS SERVICES AND EXPERTISE IN CARE, TRAINING AND RESEARCH. IT IS HEADQUARTERED IN MAFÃƑÆ’Ã‚Â¨RINYAH- RURAL COMMUNE OF MAFÃƑÆ’Ã‚Â¨RINYAH, PREFECTURE OF FORÃƑÆ’Ã‚Â©CARIAH, ADMINISTRATIVE REGION OF KINDIA, GUINEA. SINCE JUNE 12, 2015, THERE HAS BEEN THE SIGNING OF A MEMORANDUM OF COLLABORATION BETWEEN THE MINISTRY OF HEALTH OF GUINEA AND THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF ALLERGIES AND INFECTIOUS DISEASES (NIAID) OF THE NATIONAL INSTITUTES OF HEALTH (NIH) OF UNITED STATES OF AMERICA (USA) IN THE FIELD OF BIOMEDICAL RESEARCH IN GUINEA. FOLLOWING THE SIGNING OF THIS PROTOCOL, THE CLINICAL RESEARCH PROGRAM (PREGUI) WAS SET UP BY ORDER NO. 5214/2018/MS/SGG OF JULY 16, 2018 OF THE MINISTER OF HEALTH OF GUINEA. THE GOVERNANCE STRUCTURES ARE: BOARD OF DIRECTORS, STEERING COMMITTEE, OPERATIONS MANAGEMENT COMMITTEE, ADMINISTRATIVE AND FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT SUPPORT SERVICES (GUINEA SERVICE CENTER) AND SCIENTIFIC ADVISORY COMMITTEE.

FOR THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE RESEARCH PROJECTS, A CORE RESEARCH TEAM HAS BEEN SET UP THAT COMPRISES THE FOLLOWING:

1. DIRECTOR OF PREGUI
2. DEPUTY DIRECTOR OF PREGUI
3. HEAD OF RESEARCH
4. LABORATORY MANAGER
5. HEAD OF PHARMACY
6. HEAD OF MOLECULAR BIOLOGY UNIT

THE PREGUI PROGRAM FUNDING IS PROVIDED BY NIAID/NIH. THE PROGRAM AND NIH RESEARCH PROJECTS ARE MANAGED THROUGH LEIDOS BIO MEDICAL RESEARCH INC. AND THE MITCHELL GROUP, INC. (TMG). THE OTHER SOURCES OF PREGUI RESEARCH FUNDING ARE MANAGED BY GUINEA SERVICE CENTER WITH TECHNICAL AND MANAGEMENT SUPPORT FROM TMG.

THE DESIGNATED PI AT CNFRSR, DR. ABDOUL BÃƑÆ’Ã‚Â©AVOGUI, WILL COORDINATE DIRECTLY WITH THE OVERALL PROJECT PI, DR. CLAIRE STANDLEY, THROUGH SCHEDULED (APPROXIMATELY MONTHLY) TELECONFERENCES AS WELL AS REGULAR EMAIL AND TEXT COMMUNICATION AS NEEDED. DR. STANDLEYÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S DOCTORAL AND POSTDOCTORAL FELLOWS WILL ALSO DIRECTLY COORDINATE WITH DR. BÃƑÆ’Ã‚Â©AVOGUIÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S TECHNICAL STAFF TO EXECUTE THE PROJECT.

subaward_description: THIS MURI PROJECT WILL SUPPORT ONE GRADUATE STUDENT WORKING UNDER THE SUPERVISION OF PROFESSOR NORMAN YAO, WHO WILL CONDUCT RESEARCH ON USING DIPOLAR INTERACTIONS TO REALIZE SPIN SQUEEZING IN A CENTRAL SPIN MODEL AS WELL AS FOCUS ON EXPLORING NEW TECHNIQUES FOR ENHANCED METROLOGY BASED ON MANY-BODY CRITICAL POINTS. THIS WORK WILL BE CONDUCTED IN CLOSE COLLABORATION WITH OTHER MEMBERS OF THE MURI RESEARCH TEAM. SPECIFIC WORK TO BE CONDUCTED AND MILESTONES ARE AS FOLLOWS: WE WILL DEVELOP NEW APPROACHES TO UTILIZE A COMBINATION OF DRIVING AND DIPOLAR INTERACTIONS TO REALIZE SPIN SQUEEZING. WE WILL ALSO EXPLORE THE POSSIBILITY THAT GROUND STATES OF CERTAIN LONG-RANGE INTERACTING SPIN MODELS CAN HARBOR METROLOGICALLY USEFUL ENTANGLEMENT AS MEASURED BY THE QUANTUM FISHER INFORMATION. USING A THREE-DIMENSIONAL CENTRAL SPIN MODEL BASED UPON NV CENTERS AND P1 CENTERS IN DIAMOND, WE WILL EXPLORE THE DYNAMICS OF ENTANGLEMENT AND ATTEMPT TO MEASURE THE EMERGENT DIFFUSION CONSTANT IN A DISORDERED DIPOLAR SPIN ENSEMBLE. USING SPECIALLY FABRICATED SAMPLES FROM OUR MURI TEAM MEMBER (PROF. JAYICH), WE WILL EXPLORE THE POSSIBILITY OF LOCALIZATION IN A TWO-DIMENSIONAL LATTICE OF DIPOLAR SPINS. IN ADDITION, WE WILL ATTEMPT TO GENERALIZE TRANSITION EDGE SENSING FOR USE IN A RYDBERG TWEEZER ARRAY. WE WILL DEMONSTRATE CONTROL OF DISORDER, HAMILTONIAN INTERACTIONS, AND SPIN DENSITY IN AN NV-P1 SYSTEM BY USING A COMBINATION OF PULSED DRIVING PROTOCOLS AND EXTERNAL FIELD TUNING. WE WILL PERFORM EXTENSIVE NUMERICAL OPTIMIZATION TO FIND A SUITABLE PARAMETER REGIME FOR REALIZING DIPOLAR SPIN SQUEEZING. BUILDING UPON THE TOOLS DEVELOPED IN YEARS 3-4, WE WILL DEMONSTRATE SPIN SQUEEZING IN A CENTRAL SPIN NV MODEL. IN ADDITION, WE WILL USE PULSED DRIVING PROTOCOLS TO DEMONSTRATE AC MAGNETOMETRY N OUR COUPLED NV-P1 SYSTEM WHICH EXHIBITS AN ENHANCED SENSING BANDWIDTH WITH NO LOSS IN SENSITIVITY.

subaward_description:
PROPOSAL DESCRIPTION
ON THIS GRANT, APL ALONG WITH RESEARCH GROUPS FROM UNIVERSITY OF ROCHESTER, UCLA AND
VIRGINIA TECH WILL BE SUBCONTRACTORS TO UMBC. THE GOAL OF THE PROJECT IS TO CHARACTERIZE CHARGE
NOISE IN SPIN QUBITS, THEREBY IMPROVING OUR ABILITY TO MODEL AND MITIGATE IT. APL WILL FOCUS ON
NON-GAUSSIAN CHARACTERISTICS OF CHARGE NOISE.
PROBLEM STATEMENT
CHARGE NOISE IS A KEY FACTOR LIMITING THE PERFORMANCE OF SPIN QUBITS IN SEMICONDUCTOR QUANTUM
DOTS. MITIGATING CHARGE NOISE REQUIRES A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF ITS ORIGINS AND STATISTICAL PROPERTIES.
EVIDENCE SUGGESTS THAT CHARGE NOISE ORIGINATES IN THE SURFACE OF THE SEMICONDUCTOR DUE TO THE
PRESENCE OF PARTICLES WITH DIPOLE MOMENTS, KNOWN AS TWO-LEVEL FLUCTUATORS (TLFS). HOWEVER, LITTLE
IS KNOWN REGARDING THE DENSITY OF THE TLFS OR THE MAGNITUDES AND ORIENTATIONS OF THEIR ELECTRIC
DIPOLE MOMENTS. THE DENSITY, IN PARTICULAR, IS EXPECTED TO HAVE A CRITICAL ROLE IN DETERMINING THE
STATISTICS OF CHARGE NOISE. WHEN THE DENSITY IS LARGE AND A QUBIT INTERACTS WITH MANY TLFS, THE
CENTRAL LIMIT THEOREM IMPLIES THAT CHARGE NOISE IS GAUSSIAN DISTRIBUTED. ON THE OTHER HAND, WHEN
THE CONCENTRATION IS SPARSE AND A QUBIT INTERACTS STRONGLY WITH ONLY A FEW TLFS, THE CHARGE NOISE
SHOULD DISPLAY NON-GAUSSIAN STATISTICS.
WITH FEW EXCEPTIONS, EXISTING WORK ON SPIN QUBITS TREATS CHARGE NOISE AS GAUSSIAN, EVEN THOUGH
EXPERIMENTAL FINDINGS INDICATE THAT SPIN QUBITS OFTEN INTERACT PREFERENTIALLY WITH A LIMITED NUMBER
OF TLFS. WHILE THE DISTINCTION BETWEEN GAUSSIAN AND NON-GAUSSIAN STATISTICS MAY SEEM ACADEMIC,
NON-GAUSSIAN NOISE ALTERS THE SPECTRAL RESPONSE OF QUANTUM SYSTEMS TO CONTROL AND CONVENTIONAL
ERROR MITIGATION TECHNIQUES SUCH AS DYNAMICAL DECOUPLING. ADDRESSING CHARGE NOISE IN SPIN QUBITS
REQUIRES AN APPROACH TO DETECT, CHARACTERIZE AND MODEL NON-GAUSSIAN EFFECTS. THIS WILL ENABLE THE
DESIGN OF ERROR MITIGATION, GATES AND CONTROL, ROBUST TO THE UNIQUE DYNAMICAL EFFECTS OF NON-
GAUSSIAN CHARGE NOISE.
PROJECT GOALS
APL WILL DEVELOP STRATEGIES TO DETECT, CHARACTERIZE AND MITIGATE NON-GAUSSIAN CHARGE NOISE USING
NON-GAUSSIAN ARMA MODELS.ATTACHMENT 5A
MILESTONES:
1. YEAR 1:
A. DEVELOP A MULTI-AXIS NOISE MODEL FOR A SINGLET-TRIPLET SPIN QUBIT THAT ACCOUNTS FOR QUASI-
STATIC HYPERFINE NOISE AND CHARGE NOISE, TREATED AN AUTOREGRESSIVE-MOVING-AVERAGE
(ARMA) MODEL.
B. USE NOISE MODEL TO DESIGN A TEST ON EXPERIMENTAL DATA THAT DETERMINES WHETHER NON-
GAUSSIAN EFFECTS ARE DYNAMICALLY SIGNIFICANT.
2. YEAR 2:
A. DEVISE APPROACH TO EXPERIMENTALLY CHARACTERIZE NON-GAUSSIAN CHARGE NOISE BY FITTING THE
COEFFICIENTS OF A NON-GAUSSIAN ARMA MODEL TO MEASURED DATA.
B. PROVIDE SUPPORT TO THE EXPERIMENTAL TEAM AT THE UNIVERSITY OF ROCHESTER TO IMPLEMENT
NON-GAUSSIAN CHARGE NOISE CHARACTERIZATION ON A SINGLET-TRIPLET QUBIT.
3. YEAR 3:
A. USING NOISE MODEL COMBINED WITH NUMERICAL OPTIMAL CONTROL AND/OR ANALYTIC TECHNIQUES,
DETERMINE OPEN-LOOP CONTROL THAT MAXIMIZES THE FIDELITY OF A QUBIT SUBJECT TO NON-GAUSSIAN
NOISE.
B. COMPARE WITH EXISTING OPEN-LOOP ERROR SUPPRESSION TECHNIQUES.
4. YEAR 4:
A. APPLY THE IN SITU VALLEY NOISE PROBE TECHNIQUE DEVELOPED BY UMBC TO DESIGN AN APPROACH
FOR DYNAMICALLY UPDATING THE NON-GAUSSIAN NOISE MODEL WHILE A QUBIT IS IN OPERATION.
B. ESTABLISH ADAPTIVE CONTROL PROTOCOL IN WHICH THE MOST CURRENT VERSION OF NOISE MODEL IS
``FED FORWARDÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½ TO DYNAMICALLY UPDATE THE CONTROL STRATEGY ESTABLISHED IN YEAR 3.
DELIVERABLES
1. YEAR 1: QUARTERLY TECHNICAL AND FINANCIAL STATUS REPORTS.
2. YEAR 2: QUARTERLY TECHNICAL AND FINANCIAL STATUS REPORTS.
3. YEAR 3: QUARTERLY TECHNICAL AND FINANCIAL STATUS REPORTS.
4. YEAR 4: QUARTERLY TECHNICAL AND FINANCIAL STATUS REPORTS. FINAL TECHNICAL REPORT.

subaward_description:
SPECIFIC AIM 1: DETERMINE WHETHER THERE IS AN INTERACTION BETWEEN DIABETES AND OBESITY LEADING TO 
THE DIAGNOSIS OF LETHAL PCA, AND WHETHER THIS INTERACTION DIFFERS BY RACE/ETHNICITY. WE WILL DEVELOP A 
COHORT OF OVER 4,000 PROSTATE BIOPSY PATIENTS FROM THE DURHAM VAMC (54% BLACK, 46% NHW) AND OVER 
3,500 BIOPSY PATIENTS FROM THE SAN JUAN VAMC. DIABETES AND BMI WILL BE EXTRACTED FROM MEDICAL RECORDS, 
AS WELL AS DIABETES DURATION, MEDICATION HISTORY, AND HBA1C AS A MARKER OF CONTROL. WE ESTIMATE 1,050 
LETHAL PCA IDENTIFIED IN THIS COHORT (GRADE GROUP 4 OR 5, PSA > 20 NG/ML, NODE+ OR METASTATIC). IN 
MULTIVARIABLE ANALYSES, WE WILL INVESTIGATE THE PREVALENCE OF DIABETES AND OBESITY ACROSS PR, BLACK, AND 
NHW PATIENTS, AND THE IMPACT OF DIABETES IN OBESE VS. NON-OBESE MEN ON LETHAL PCA ACROSS RACE/ETHNICITY 
GROUPS. RESULTS WILL BE THE LARGEST INVESTIGATION OF LETHAL PCA IN PR, AND THE ONLY COMPARISON OF LETHAL PCA 
RISK ACROSS PR, BLACK, AND NHW MEN EVER PERFORMED.
SPECIFIC AIM 2: DETERMINE IF THERE ARE DIFFERENCES IN PROSTATE TISSUE GENE EXPRESSION AMONG PR, 
BLACK, AND NHW PCA PATIENTS WITH DIABETES, AND WITH AND WITHOUT OBESITY

subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
THE PROPOSED CATHOLYTES WILL BE INTEGRATED INTO THE SSSO-ARM TO PROVIDE THE NECESSARY MUSCULAR HYDROSTAT MECHANICS, GUIDED BY TASK 1. IMPORTANTLY FOR THE SSSO-ARM, THE DENSITY OF REACTANTS (~1.2-1.3 G/CC) AND SUPPORTING SOLVENT (1,3-DIOXOLANE AND DIMETHOXYETHANE, ~1 AND 0.9 G/CC) ARE SUBSTANTIALLY LOWER THAN LI-ION BATTERY MATERIALS (~2.5-5 G/CC) AND COMPRISE A LARGER SHARE OF THE TOTAL BATTERY WEIGHT, ALLOWING FOR THE ROBOT BUOYANCY TO BE MORE CAREFULLY ENGINEERED AND MATCHED WITH SALT WATER.
THE LI+ IN THE CATHOLYTE CAN ADDITIONALLY BE USED TO SUPPORT THE ELECTROCHEMICAL REACTIONS IN THE TASK 2 ACTUATORS AND HARVESTERS, AS LONG AS THERE IS A SUFFICIENT VOLTAGE WINDOW TO PREVENT UNWANTED SIDE REACTIONS. TO REALIZE THIS, PIKUL AND BAUGHMAN WILL MEASURE THE ABILITY OF THE ORGANOPOLYSULFIDE ELECTROLYTES TO OPERATE IN TASK 2 ACTUATORS AND HARVESTERS AS WELL AS EXPLORE THE POSSIBILITY OF USING CNT FIBER ELECTRODES INSTEAD OF THE GRAPHENE ELECTRODES USED IN PRELIMINARY RESULTS.
THE DESIGN FOR MULTIFUNCTIONALITY DOES NOT REQUIRE FULL REALIZATION OF THE ORGANOPOLYSULFIDE CATHOLYTE AS PIKUL CAN USE ZN-I3 CHEMISTRY TO TEST THE INTEGRATION OF ZN-I3 INTO THE SSSO-ARM AND THE USE OF ZN2+ OR I- IN ACTUATORS AND HARVESTER. ALTHOUGH THE ZN-I3 SYSTEM HAS LOWER CAPACITY, IT HAS BETTER UNDERSTOOD ELECTROCHEMICAL PROPERTIES AND PIKUL HAS PREVIOUS EXPERIENCE USING THIS CHEMISTRY AS A MULTIFUNCTIONAL ELECTROLYTE IN A SOFT ROBOTIC FISH.51
THE PROPOSED WORK WILL REALIZE FUNDAMENTAL ADVANCES IN MULTIFUNCTIONAL MECHANICAL SYSTEMS, WHILE ENABLING NOVEL ENERGY SELF-SUFFICIENT MATERIALS PLATFORMS FOR DOD OPERATIONS.

GOAL: THE GOAL OF THIS RESEARCH IS TO DEVELOP AN INSERT FOR A COLD WEATHER COMBAT BOOT THAT KEEPS
THE FOOT WARM (TOPIC 5). THIS INSERT WILL BE FULLY PRINTED AND DEVELOPED AS A COLLABORATIVE EFFORT
BETWEEN UML AND FLEXCON. THE LONG-TERM GOAL WILL BE TO FABRICATE THE HEATER USING A FULLY
DEVELOPED R2R MANUFACTURING PROCESS AT FLEXCON.
YEAR 1 OBJECTIVE: OPTIMIZE DESIGN AND MATERIALS FOR A PRINTED HEATER; FABRICATE THE PRINTED, FLEXIBLE
HEATER PROTOTYPE, AND CHARACTERIZE THE PRINTED HEATERS AT UML. THE PRINTED SENSORS WILL BE TESTED
FOR ADHESION, FLEXIBILITY, AND CHANGE IN CONDUCTIVITY AS A FUNCTION OF THESE PARAMETERS IN ADDITION
TO ENVIRONMENTAL TESTING. THIS WILL THEN BE TRANSITIONED TO R2R PROCESS AT FLEXCON. THE MAIN
DELIVERABLE FOR THIS PHASE WILL BE 5 PRINTED, FUNCTIONAL, FLEXIBLE HEATERS.
YEAR 2 OBJECTIVE: EXPLORE NOVEL APPROACHES FOR INCREASING FLEXIBILITY AND DURABILITY; PRELIMINARY
EXPLORATION OF THERMAL AND MOISTURE MONITORING AND ELECTRONIC INTEGRATION WILL COMMENCE AS
NEEDED.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: NEW START GRANT TITLED: HIGH-SPEED FLUID STRUCTURE INTERACTIONS WITH PROGRAMMABLE COMPLEXITY
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
COMPUTATIONAL MODELING WORKPLAN
THE WORKPLAN INCLUDES ADVANCES IN THE COMPUTATIONALLY COMPACT AND STATE OF THE ART MODELING
OF (1) TIME DEPENDENT FLUID DYNAMICS (UNSTEADY AERODYNAMICS) AND ALSO (2) NONLINEAR STRUCTURAL
DYNAMIC MODELS FOR ELASTIC PLATES CLAMPED ON ALL EDGES (SKIN PANELS OF A FLIGHT VEHICLE) AND ALSO
ELASTIC PLATES CLAMPED ON ONLY ONE EDGE (CANTILEVERED PLATES SUCH AS CONTROL SURFACE OR FINS).
THE FOCUS IS ON USING REDUCED ORDER MODELS OF BOTH THE FLUID FLOW AND THE ELASTIC STRUCTURE
(MODELS WITH SIGNIFICANT REDUCTION IN COMPUTATIONAL COST AND COMPLEXITY WITH NO ESSENTIAL LOSS
IN ACCURACY) THAT ENABLE A DEEPER UNDERSTANDING AND COMPACT COMPUTATIONAL MODELING FOR FLUID
STRUCTURAL THERMAL INTERACTION.
TIME DEPENDENT FLUID DYNAMICS (UNSTEADY AERODYNAMICS): THE CURRENT STATE OF THE ART IS BASED
UPON POTENTIAL FLOW FLUID MODELS AND THIS IS ADEQUATE FOR PREDICTING THE ONSET OF DYNAMIC
INSTABILITIES AND RESPONSE TO PRESCRIBED DYNAMIC FORCES FOR MOST STRUCTURAL CONFIGURATIONS, IF THE
FLUID BOUNDARY LAYER THICKNESS IS SMALL COMPARED TO THE LENGTH OF THE ELASTIC PLATE AND A SHOCK
WAVE DOES NOT ACT ON THE ELASTIC STRUCTURE ITSELF. ALSO FOR AN ELASTIC PLATE CLAMPED ON ALL EDGES, A
POTENTIAL FLOW MODEL IS ADEQUATE FOR PREDICTING NONLINEAR LIMIT CYCLE OSCILLATIONS BECAUSE THE
DOMINANT NONLINEARITY IS STRUCTURE RATHER THAN IN THE FLUID. SUCH A FLOW MODEL CAN BE USED WITH
CONFIDENCE FOR THE PURPOSE OF PREDICTING STRUCTURAL MOTION. ONCE THE STRUCTURAL MOTION IS
KNOWN WITH SUFFICIENT ACCURACY THIS KNOWLEDGE WILL ENABLE STATE OF THE ART FLUID DYNAMIC
MODELING OF THE ENTIRE FLOW FIELD USING RANS, LES AND DNS.
HOWEVER FOR CANTILEVERED PLATES WHOSE DEFLECTIONS ARE MUCH LARGER THAN THOSE OF A PLATE
CLAMPED ON ALL EDGES AND FOR ALL PLATES WHEN THERE IS A THICK FLUID BOUNDARY LAYER OR SHOCK
WAVES ON THE ELASTIC PANEL, A HIGHER LEVEL FLUID MODEL IS NEEDED SUCH AS RANS, LES OR EVEN DNS.
WORK IS CURRENTLY UNDERWAY TO INCORPORATE RECENT ADVANCES USING NEW CONCEPTS IN REDUCED
ORDER MODELING FOR RANS FLUID MODELS AND IT IS ANTICIPATED THIS WORK WILL BE COMPLETED BY THE
END OF YEAR 1 FOR THE PLATE CLAMPED ON ALL EDGES AND BY THE END OF YEAR 2 WHEN THE PLATE IS
CLAMPED ON ONLY ONE EDGE (CANTILEVERED PLATE). THERE ARE SEVERAL OPTIONS FOR FURTHER ADVANCES
IN YEAR 3 INCLUDING THE USE OF LES OR SOME FORM OF DNS MODELING. THIS WORK WILL BE LED BY LUISA
PICCOLO SERAFIM, A CURRENT SECOND YEAR PHD STUDENT IN OUR GROUP.
ELASTIC PLATES CLAMPED ON ALL EDGES: RECENT ADVANCES IN STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS USING REDUCED ORDER
MODELING HAVE INCORPORATED MANY OF THE PHYSICAL FEATURES ESSENTIAL FOR DESIGN OF AND
CORRELATION WITH WIND TUNNEL EXPERIMENTS. THIS INCLUDES NONLINEAR STIFFNESS, IN PLANE AS WELL AS
OUT OF PLANE BOUNDARY SUPPORTS OF FINITE STIFFNESS, THERMAL STRESSES (THAT MAY LEAD TO BUCKLING
AND IN TURN EFFECT DYNAMIC RESPONSE) AND OTHER MECHANICAL LOADS SUCH AS A STATIC PRESSURE
DIFFERENTIAL ACROSS THE ELASTIC PLATE. HOWEVER OTHER IMPORTANT PHYSICAL PARAMETERS HAVE YET TO
BE INCLUDED SUCH AS PLATE CURVATURE. COMPOSITE MATERIALS AND VISCOELASTIC EFFECTS. A NEW PHD
STUDENT WILL UNDERTAKE THIS WORK BEGINNING IN YEAR 1 OR 2.
ELASTIC PLATE CLAMPED ON ONLY ONE EDGE (CANTILEVERED PLATE): RECENT ADVANCES IN THE NONLINEAR
MODELING OF CANTILEVERED BEAMS HAS BEEN EXTENDED TO NONLINEAR PLATES WHICH MAY BEND IN TWO
DIRECTIONS RATHER THAN JUST ONE AS FOR A BEAM. HOWEVER THIS WORK IS VERY NEW AND SOME FUNDAMENTAL QUESTIONS REMAIN ABOUT THE REDUCED ORDER MODEL FOR CANTILEVERED PLATES. FOR
EXAMPLE, DO WE NEED TO CONSIDER A SYSTEM OF COUPLED PARTIAL DIFFERENTIAL EQUATIONS THAT INCLUDE
DEFLECTION COMPONENTS IN ALL THREE SPATIAL DIRECTIONS? FOR A CANTILEVERED BEAM, THE CONCEPT OF
INEXTENSIBILITY HAS BEEN SUCCESSFULLY USED TO EXPRESS THE IN-PLANE DEFLECTIONS IN TERMS OF THE OUT
OF PLANE DEFLECTION IN A RELATIVELY SIMPLE AND HIGHLY EFFECTIVE WAY TO PRODUCE A REDUCED ORDER
MODEL BASED UPON THE NATURAL MODES OF THE BEAM. THIS WORK FOR THE BEAM HAS BEEN EXTENDED
IN AN OBVIOUS WAY TO PLATES, BUT IN A HEURISTIC FASHION AND IS NOT YET ON A RIGOROUS FOUNDATION,
THOUGH IT APPEARS TO GIVE GOOD RESULTS FOR SOME CASES (BUT NOT FOR OTHERS). THIS REQUIRES MORE
FUNDAMENTAL ADVANCES AND WILL BE THE SUBJECT OF A THESIS BY A NEW PHD STUDENT BEGINNING IN
YEAR 1 OR 2.
IN ALL OF THE ABOVE WORK, THE COMPUTATIONAL MODELS WILL USED TO HELP DESIGN THE EXPERIMENTAL
CONFIGURATIONS AND MEASUREMENT PROTOCOLS AS WELL AS CORELATE WITH DATA FROM WIND TUNNEL
MEASUREMENTS. ALSO SOME BENCH TOP EXPERIMENTS WILL BE PERFORMED AT DUKE UNIVERSITY TO
VALIDATE THE EXPERIMENTAL STRUCTURAL MODELS PRIOR TO THEIR USE IN THE WIND TUNNEL CAMPAIGN.

subaward_description:
TASK 1: EXPERIMENT GUIDANCE AND IRB DOCUMENT DRAFTING
HIRSHFIELD WILL GIVE GUIDANCE ON THE DESIGN OF EXPERIMENTS TO BE SUITED FOR NEUROPHYSIOLOGICAL SENSING STUDIES, AND SHE WILL SECURE IRB APPROVAL FROM THE CU IRB BOARD TO RUN THE EXPERIMENTS USING THE NEUROPHYSIOLOGICAL SENSORS IN HER LAB AT CU.
TASK 2: DATA COLLECTION
HIRSHFIELD WILL DIRECT A PHD GRADUATE RESEARCH ASSISTANT AND AN UNDERGRADUATE HOURLY EMPLOYEE IN HER LAB TO COLLECT NEUROPHYSIOLOGICAL SENSING DATA ON THE EXPERIMENTS DESIGNED IN TASK 1.
TASK 3: DATA ANALYSIS
THIS TASK WILL INVOLVE CONDUCTING DATA ANALYSIS ON THE NEUROPHYSIOLOGICAL (FNIRS, EEG, EYETRACKING) DATA FROM THE EXPERIMENTS IN TASK 2, AS WELL AS ON THE BEHAVIORAL AND SELF-REPORT DATA FROM THE EXPERIMENTS. RESULTS WILL BE DISSEMINATED TO, AND DISCUSSED WITH, THE CSU TEAM.
TASK 4: SENSOR DOWN SELECTION AND ITERATIVE EXPERIMENTATION FOR AUGMENTED REALITY ENVIRONMENTS 
THIS TASK WILL INVOLVE RUNNING PEARSON AND KENDALL CORRELATIONS AND OTHER STATISTICAL TESTS ON THE DATA FROM TASK 2, IN ORDER TO DOWNSELECT THE SENSORS AND CHANNEL LOCATIONS, TO BE INTEGRATED WITH THE AUGMENTED REALITY (AR) HEADSETS USED IN PI ORTEGAÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S LAB. ITERATIVE HUMAN SUBJECT EXPERIMENTS WILL BE RUN WITH THIS DOWNSELECTED SUITE OF SENSORS IN THE AR ENVIRONMENTS, IN ORDER TO ENSURE WE MAINTAIN PROPER WORKLOAD DIAGNOSTICITY WITH THE DOWNSELECTED SENSORS AND CONFIGURATIONS.
TASK 5: PROJECT REPORTING AND RESULT DISSEMINATION
RESULTS FROM THE TASKS ABOVE WILL BE DISSEMINATED IN HIGH QUALITY CONFERENCE PROCEEDINGS, JOURNALS, AND VIA PRESENTATIONS AT HCI AND RELATED CONFERENCES.

subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
"WE WILL PRESENT SEVERAL PLATFORMS TO ENGINEER PRECISE STATE PREPARATION OF MOLECULES FOR STUDIES OF NOVEL  COLLISION  AND REACTION  DYNAMICS. THE STATE PREPARATION  OF KRB POLAR MOLECULES WILL BE FOCUSED ON REACHING DEEPLY INTO QUANTUM DEGENERACY. WE HAVE RECENTLY PRODUCED A KRB MOLECULAR GAS WITH T/TF -0.3, WHERE TF IS THE FERMI TEMPERATURE. AS WE MOVE FURTHER INTO THE QUANTUM DEGENERATE REGIME, WE WILL EXPERIMENT WITH THE INTERPLAY OF THE DIMENSIONALITY  OF SPATIAL CONFINEMENT  AND DIPOLAR  INTERACTIONS  TO STABILIZE SUCH SYSTEMS. BY ORIENTING THE MOLECULAR DIPOLE MOMEN T PERPENDICULAR TO THE PLANE OF 20 TRAPS, WE CAN ELIMINATE THE ATTRACTIVE PART OF THE DIPOLAR INTERACTIONS AND GREATLY SUPPRESS THE REACTIVE LOSS. OUR NEWLY ACCOMPLISHED CAPABILITY OF APPLYING A VERY LARGE DC ELECTRIC FIELD INSIDE THE VACUUM WILL FACILITATE SUCH AN EFFORT. THESE TWO-DIMENSIONAL TRAPS WILL ALLOW US TO INVESTIGATE EVAPORATIVELY COOLING OF MOLECULES FIRST, AS A FAVORABLE RATIO FOR ELASTIC OVER INELASTIC COLLISIONS IS EXPECTED, FACILITATED WITH THE USE OF AN ELECTRIC FIELD GRADIENT TO TILT THE TRAP IN THE HORIZONTAL DIRECTION FOR EFFICIENT REMOVAL OF HIGHER TEMPERATURE MOLECULES. WE WILL ALSO STUDY ELASTIC AND INELASTIC COLLISION PROPERTIES FOR MOLECULES AT DIFFERENT ROTATIONAL STATES, AS WELL AS AT DIFFERENT COHERENT SUPERPOSITIONS OF THE TWO ROTATIONAL STATES.  STACKED MULTI-LAYER  PANCAKES OF FERMIONIC POLAR MOLECULES WILL  ENABLE US TO INVESTIGATE NOVEL COLLECTIVE MOLECULAR DYNAMICS. TO ASSIST THE STUDY OF DIPOLE-DIPOLE INTERACTIONS IN THIS COLLECTIVE SYSTEM, WE WILL DEVELOP THE ABILITY TO IMAGE A SINGLE LAYER OF MOLECULES WITH SIGNIFICANTLY INCREASED IMAGING RESOLUTION. WHEN WE CONFINE THE MOLECULES IN EITHER ONE OR THREE  DIMENSIONS,  THE  TECHNICAL  CAPABILITY  FOR  HIGH-RESOLUTION,  IN-SITU  IMAGING  OF  THE MOLECULAR  QUANTUM GAS WILL BE USED FOR BOTH STATE READOUT AND ALSO STATE PREPARATION. THESE NEW IMAGING CAPABILITIES WILL ALLOW US TO LOOK FOR MOLECULAR PAIRING CORRELATIONS AND MEAN-FIELD EFFECTS IN FREE-FLIGHT EXPANSION FROM THE 20 GEOMETRY.

IN A SEPARATE EXPERIMENTAL PLATFORM WE WILL WORK ON EXPANDING A DIVERSE SET OF CHEMICALLY INTERESTING MOLECULES INTO THE QUANTUM REGIME. WE WILL BE COLLABORATING WITH OTHER PJ'S IN THIS TEAM TO WORK ON DIRECT COOLING OF MOLECULES ACROSS A WIDE VARIETY OF SPECIES, INCLUDING HYDROXYL FREE RADICAL OH, YTTRIUM MONOXIDE YO, AND BUCKMINSTERFULLERENE C6O. WE CAN PRODUCE THESE CHEMICALLY PROMINENT MOLECULES AT MODERATELY LOW TEMPERATURES (1 MK - 100 K). OUR LASER COOLING EFFORT WILL BE BUILT ON THE SUCCESSFUL CONSTRUCTION OF A MAGNETOÃƑÂ€ŠÃ‚Â­ OPTICAL TRAP OF YO MOLECULES. WE PLAN TO COOL YO TO A TEMPERATURE OF A FEW MICROKELVIN USING A SECOND-STAGE MOT WITH A NARROW OPTICAL TRANSITION. WE WILL THEN TRANSFER THE COLD MOLECULES TO A FAR-OFF-RESONANCE OPTICAL DIPOLE TRAP. THIS SHOULD GREATLY FACILITATE THE STUDY OF YO-YO COLLISIONS, IF THE SAMPLE DENSITY IS SUFFICIENTLY HIGH. THE WORK ON OH COLLISIONS WILL BE FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICAN TLY INCREASES OF THE MOLECULAR DENSITY. WE WILL AIM FOR TWO MAIN MILESTONES: I) SLOWING AND TRAPPING A HIGHLY INTENSE BEAM OF OH, II) COLLISIONAL STUDIES INVOLVING OH-OH AND OH-ATOM. WE WILL USE AN ALTERNATIVE CHARGING STRATEGY THAT BRINGS A CONVENTIONAL PULSED ELECTRODE ARRAY MUCH CLOSER TO A TRUE MOVING TRAP SLOWER IN OUR STARK DECELERATOR. THE TECH NIQUE OFFERS MANY-FOLD INCREASES IN MOLECULE NUMBER, WITH ONLY MINOR ADJUSTMENTS  TO THE TIMING OF THE DEVICE, AS CONFIRMED BY SIMULATIONS.  WE WILL DIRECTLY MEASURE COLLISIONAL EFFECTS BETWEEN OH RADICALS AND NE ATOMS AS THEY CO-PROPAGATE ALONG THE AXIAL DIRECTION OF OUR STARK DECELERATOR."

subaward_description: THE FIRST AIM IS TO DEVELOP A CONSTITUTIVE MODEL FOR HELMET SHELL MATERIALS THAT IS TO BE APPLIED TO AND VALIDATED FOR TWO BALLISTIC COMPOSITE MATERIALS AT HIGH STRAIN RATES RELEVANT TO BALLISTIC LOADINGS. THE MODEL WILL BE ANISOTROPIC AND ACCOUNT FOR ELASTICITY, PLASTICITY, AND DAMAGE. CHARACTERIZATION EXPERIMENTS CARRIED OUT BY COLLABORATORS AT THE COLORADO SCHOOL OF MINES.  THE SECOND AIM IS TO BUILD A HELMET SIMULATION PLATFORM FOR BALLISTIC LOADING. THE SIMULATION PLATFORM WILL UTILIZE THE CONSTITUTIVE MODEL FOR HELMET SHELL MATERIALS DEVELOPED IN THE FIRST AIM AS WELL AS THE NONLINEAR VISCOELASTIC CONSTITUTIVE MODEL FOR HELMET LINER MATERIALS DEVELOPED IN PRIOR PANTHER AWARDS. THE SIMULATION PLATFORM WILL BE UTILIZED TO CARRY OUT SIMULATION-BASED PARAMETRIC STUDIES TO UNDERSTAND (1) THE COUPLING BETWEEN HELMET DESIGN PARAMETERS, SUCH AS STANDOFF DISTANCE, FOAM LINER PLATEAU STRESS, AND SHELL MATERIAL BEHAVIOR, AND (2) THE TRADEOFFS ENCOUNTERED WHEN DESIGNING A HELMET FOR BALLISTIC LOADING VERSUS BLUNT IMPACT LOADING.

subaward_description:
STATEMENT OF WORK:
TECHNICAL DESCRIPTION, INCLUDING TASKS AND SUBTASKS.
TASK 1: (LLNL) CONSTRUCTION OF ALL-ATOM AND COARSE-GRAINED MOLECULAR DYNAMICS (MD) MODELS FOR THE FOUR SELECTED
RBPS. (MONTHS 0-6)
TASK 2: (LLNL) MD SIMULATIONS OF PROTEINS IN TASK 1, THEIR SELF-INTERACTION AND CROSS-INTERACTION WITH TDP43
(MONTHS 0-6)
TASK 3: (LLNL) DEVELOP AND RUN PHYSICS-BASED SIMULATIONS OF THE FOUR SELECTED RBPS AND TDP43 TO GAIN
MECHANISTIC INSIGHTS INTO THEIR MISLOCALIZATION AND CONDENSATION TDP43 (MONTHS 7-12)
TASK 4: (LLNL/GLADSTONE) IMAGED OF TDP43 MISLOCALIZATION AND CONDENSATION IN ALS USING LATTICE LIGHT SHEET
MICROSCOPE (LLSM). IPSC DERIVED MOTOR NEURONS (I-MNS) WITH FLUORESCENTLY LABELED PROTEINS PROVIDED BY
GLADSTONE. SETUP AND TEST PROTOCOLS AND SYSTEMS TO VERIFY VIABILITY OF LLSM TO DETERMINE PROTEIN COLOCALIZATION IN
PUNCTA IN THE CYTOPLASM. (MONTHS 0-6)
TASK 5: (LLNL/GLADSTONE) FOR A FEW SELECT CONDITIONS/TIME POINTS COLLECT IMAGES AS IN PROTOCOL ESTABLISHED IN TASK
4. (MONTHS 0-6)
TASK 6: (LLNL/GLADSTONE) THE MOST POTENT AND EFFECTIVE PROSETTA COMPOUND(S) WILL THEN BE PROVIDED TO LLNL AND
TESTED AT LLNL FOR THEIR ABILITY TO RAPIDLY BLOCK TDP43 FROM FORMING CONDENSATES IN THE CYTOPLASM USING LLSM AS
IN TASK 5. (MONTHS 7-12)
TASK 7: (LLNL) OPTIONAL TASK. SIMULATE THE INTERACTION OF SELECT PROSETTA COMPOUND(S) WITH TDP43 AND/OR SELECTED
RBPS. REQUIRES COMPOUND STRUCTURES FROM PROSETTA. (MONTHS 7-12)
TASK 8: (LLNL) FINAL REPORT. LLNL WILL WORK WITH GLADSTONE FOR THE DETAILS OF A FINAL REPORT, MINIMALLY WILL
SUMMARIZE RESULTS OF TASKS 1-6 AND PROVIDE MODELS AND RAW DATA AS NEEDED. (AT END OF PROJECT)

subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
"THIS PROPOSAL ENVISIONS AND PROPOSES A FIVE-YEAR PROGRAM DESIGNED TO DEMONSTRATE TOPOLOGICAL
PLASMA DEVICES POTENTIALLY CAPABLE OF SWITCHING, STORING, AND/OR FILTERING ELECTROMAGNETIC
ENERGY IN THE MICROWAVE AND MM-WAVE REGIONS (~1 GHZ TO BEYOND 350 GHZ). THE PURPOSE OF
THE UIUC PORTION OF THIS PROGRAM IS TO SUPPORT THE PROGRAMÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S THEORISTS BY DESIGNING, FABRICATING,
AND TESTING A WIDE RANGE OF DEVICES OVER THE LIFE OF THE PROGRAM. ALTHOUGH THE SPECIFIC TASKS TO
BE COMPLETED WILL NECESSARILY CHANGE AS CONSIDERABLE INFORMATION IS GAINED EARLY IN THE
PROGRAM, THE TASKS BRIEFLY DESCRIBED BELOW ARE THOSE CURRENTLY BELIEVED TO BE NECESSARY:
A. TASK 1
THE HIGHEST PRIORITY OF THIS PROGRAM WILL BE TO FIRST FABRICATE TWO-DIMENSIONAL
MICROCAVITY PLASMA ARRAYS ACCORDING TO THE DESIGNS DEVELOPED BY PROF. DAN SIEVENPIPER
OF UCSD. THESE ARRAYS WILL COMPRISE MICROCAVITY ARRAYS HAVING DIFFERENT CAVITY
GEOMETRIES AND CAVITY LAYOUTS SUCH AS SIMPLE LINEAR, HEXAGONAL, OR DIAMOND GEOMETRIES.
SOME OF THESE ARRAYS WILL BE SHIPPED TO PROF. SIEVENPIPER FOR TESTING AT HIS FACILITIES.
OTHERS WILL BE INVESTIGATED AT UIUC BY PLACING THE ARRAYS BETWEEN MAGNETIC FIELD COILS.
THESE COILS WILL BE PULSED BY DISCHARGING A CAPACITOR THROUGH ONE OR MORE COILS AT A
FREQUENCY THAT IS SYNCHRONIZED WITH SPECTRAL ANALYSIS EQUIPMENT. IT IS EXPECTED THAT
THESE EXPERIMENTS WILL CONTINUE FOR APPROXIMATELY TWO YEARS.
B. TASK 2
THE SECOND EXPERIMENTAL THEME ENTAILS THE DESIGN, CONSTRUCTION, AND TESTING OF 2D AND
3D PLASMA CRYSTALS HAVING TOPOLOGICAL STRUCTURES AND/OR COMPRISE MAGNETIZED PLASMAS.
THE FIRST STUDIES IN THIS AREA WILL INVOLVE INSERTING MICROCOLUMNS OF A MAGNETIC MATERIAL,
SUCH AS A FERROFLUID OR MAGNETIC NANOPARTICLES, INTO A PLASMA/METAL/DIELECTRIC CRYSTAL
SUCH AS THOSE WE HAVE BEEN BUILDING OVER THE PAST FIVE YEARS. WE PLAN TO START WITH 2D
CRYSTALS FOR THE SAKE OF THE THEORISTS WHO WILL BE FOCUSING ON 2D STRUCTURES OF VARIOUS
TYPES AT THE OUTSET OF THIS PROGRAM. THE FIRST CRYSTALS WILL COMPRISE A SINGLE ARRAY OF
PARALLEL MICROCOLUMNS, SOME OF WHICH WILL BE FILLED WITH PLASMA AND OTHERS WITH
MAGNETIC MATERIAL. THE ARRAY WILL BE PROBED ALONG THE COORDINATE ORTHOGONAL TO THE AXIS
OF THE MICRO-COLUMNS.
C. TASK 3
THE SUBSEQUENT SET OF EXPERIMENTS WILL MOVE TO THREE-DIMENSIONAL STRUCTURES, THEREBY
ALLOWING PLASMA AND MAGNETIC COLUMNS TO BE INSERTED INTO THE CRYSTAL ALONG ANY ONE OF
THREE AXES. WE WILL START WITH THE 3D PLASMA CRYSTALS ENCASED IN PDMS (POLY-DIMETHYL
SILOXANE). ALTHOUGH THESE CRYSTALS HAVE A SUBSTANTIAL BROADBAND LOSS DUE TO THE PDMS
ENCAPSULATING MATERIAL, THE WELL-DEVELOPED FABRICATION PROCESS DISCOVERED AT ILLINOIS
ALLOWS FOR US TO MAKE ULTRA-PRECISE AND SOPHISTICATED CRYSTALS QUICKLY. THAT IS, THE
TOPOLOGICAL AND PHOTONIC CRYSTAL EFFECTS WE WISH TO SEE WILL BE REALIZED MOST
EXPEDITIOUSLY WITH THIS DESIGN AND THE BROADBAND LOSS WILL BE DEALT WITH IN TASK 4
(BELOW). IN THESE EXPERIMENTS, EMPHASIS WILL BE PLACED ON THE JUDICIOUS PLACEMENT OF
THE MAGNETIC MICRO-COLUMNS SUCH THAT THE IMPACT OF THE MAGNETIZED PLASMAS ON CRYSTAL
PERFORMANCE WILL BE MAXIMIZED. FURTHERMORE, WE ARE VITALLY INTERESTED IN THE STORAGE OF
ENERGY IN THE CRYSTALS AND THE RELEASE OF THAT ENERGY IN AN ELECTRONIC SWITCHING
OPERATION. SEVERAL OF THESE CRYSTALS WILL BE SHIPPED TO THE LABORATORY OF PROF. MARK
CAPPELLI AT STANFORD WHO WILL INSERT THEM IN A 2 TESLA MAGNETIC FIELD SO OBSERVE EFFECTS
THAT ARE NOT DETECTABLE WITH THE COMPARATIVELY WEAK FIELD AVAILABLE WITH COLUMNS OF
MAGNETIC NANOPARTICLES. IT IS EXPECTED THAT THE EXPERIMENTS AND SIMULATIONS OF TASKS 2
AND 3 WILL REQUIRE YEARS 3 AND 4 OF THE PROGRAM.
D. TASK 4
THIS TASK WILL MOVE TO THE MICROTUBE SCAFFOLD STRUCTURE DEVELOPED IN THE PAST TWO YEARS
AT ILLINOIS FOR THE PURPOSE OF ELIMINATING THE BROADBAND LOSS CHARACTERISTIC OF THE PDMS
SCAFFOLD STRUCTURE OF TASKS 2 AND 3. THAT IS, WE HAVE DEVELOPED A TECHNIQUE FOR FUSING
POLYIMIDE MICROTUBES INTO COMPLEX 3D STRUCTURES HAVING AN AIR CORE. THE INTERIORS OF THE
MICROTUBES CAN

subaward_description: THE GOAL OF THE RESEARCH CONDUCTED BY OMER TAMUZ WILL BE TO DEVELOP THE THEORY OF AGENTS' BEHAVIOR IN COMPLEX STRATEGIC SETTINGS INVOLVING UNCERTAINTY AND COMPUTATIONAL CONSTRAINTS. THE METHODS WILL BE THEORETICAL / MATHEMATICAL IN NATURE, AND WILL INVOLVE FORMULATING MATHEMATICAL MODELS AND PROVING MATHEMATICAL STATEMENTS ABOUT THEM. THESE RESULTS WILL ALLOW PRACTITIONERS TO APPROACH STATISTICAL AND ENGINEERING CHALLENGES WITH A ROBUST TOOLSET. THIS RESEARCH WILL BE CARRIED OUT AT CALTECH, WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF A GRADUATE STUDENT WORKING AS A RESEARCH ASSISTANT. THE MAIN ACTIVITIES WILL INCLUDE (I) CONDUCTING A SURVEY OF CURRENT EMPIRICAL FINDINGS AND PREVIOUS THEORETICAL APPROACHES, (II) IDENTIFYING NOVEL MODELING APPROACHES, (III) EXPLORING THE MATHEMATICAL PROPERTIES OF THESE MODELS USING FORMAL METHODS AND (IV) WRITING PAPERS AND PRESENTING THE WORK.

subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
PROF. STEVEN ANLAGE (UMD) AND HIS POST-DOC WILL DEVELOP THE HIGH-THROUGHPUT LASER SCANNING
MICROSCOPE (LSM) TO GENERATE THE DATASETS REQUIRED TO TRAIN THE MACHINE LEARNING (ML)
ALGORITHM. PROF. Y. C. LAI (ASU), PROF. L. WRIGHT (YALE) AND PROF. ANLAGE WILL WORK TOGETHER ON
THE ML INVERSE DESIGN ALGORITHM TO MAKE PREDICTIONS FOR TAILORED PERTURBATIONS THAT LEAD THE
SYSTEM TOWARD SYNCHRONIZATION. THESE ALGORITHMS WILL BE TESTED IN THE LAB AND THEIR EFFICACY WILL
BE MEASURED. THROUGH SUCH AN ITERATIVE PROCESS THE NATURAL FORMS OF DISORDER WILL BE UNCOVERED
AND QUANTIFIED, AND THEIR IMPACT ON SYNCHRONIZATION WILL BE STUDIED. THIS WILL RESULT IN A
HIERARCHICAL UNDERSTANDING OF THE MOST INFLUENTIAL FORMS OF DISORDER, AND IDENTIFICATION OF THE
MOST EXPEDIENT MEANS TO COUNTERACT THEM. PROF. ANLAGE WILL ALSO PARTICIPATE IN THE COLLECTIVE
EFFORT OF THE ENTIRE MURI TEAM TO DEVELOP A UNIVERSAL, GENERALIST ALGORITHM FOR SYNCHRONIZING
COMPLEX SYSTEMS ACROSS DIFFERENT PLATFORMS AND REALIZATIONS.

subaward_description:
DR. YUE DENG IS A CORE DEVELOPER OF GITM FOR THE GLOBAL I-T SIMULATIONS. SHE WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE GITM SIMULATIONS AND SUPERVISE THE POSTDOC AND GRADUATE STUDENT TO IMPROVE THE MODEL TO MEET THE NEEDS OF THE PROJECT. THE POSTDOC AND GRADUATE STUDENT AT UTA WILL WORK CLOSELY WITH DR. DENG TO IMPROVE GITM, CONDUCT THE SIMULATIONS AND ANALYZE THE MODEL OUTPUTS.

THIS PROJECT WILL MAKE EXTENSIVE USE OF COMPUTATIONAL RESOURCES AND FACILITIES AT UTA AND TEXAS ADVANCED COMPUTING CENTER (TACC). UTA HAS EXCELLENT GENERAL-USE FACILITIES, WHICH INCLUDE A RANGE OF WORKSTATIONS, PERSONAL COMPUTERS, AND AN EXTENSIVE SYSTEM OF HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTIONS. GITM WILL BE RUN ON THE SUPERCOMPUTERS AT TACC, WHICH WILL PROVIDE A GREAT FACILITY FOR RESEARCHERS AND STUDENTS TO DEVELOP AND TEST MODELS FOR PARALLEL COMPUTING WITH RAPID TURNAROUND TIME.

subaward_description: THE UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA, UNDER THE TECHNICAL DIRECTION OF PROFESSOR GREGORY THOMPSON, WILL CONDUCT EXPERIMENTAL WORK IN SUPPORT OF THE PROGRAM ÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã…Â€ŒTAILORED TEMPERATURE DEPENDENT DEFORMATION IN ULTRAHIGH TEMPERATURE CERAMIC COMPOSITES.ÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½ IN THIS EFFORT, HE WILL OVERSEE THE FABRICATION AND EXPERIMENTAL CHARACTERIZATION OF A SERIES OF METALLIC AND TRANSITION METAL CARBIDES/NITRIDES THAT ARE DIRECTED FROM A COMPUTATIONAL MODELING DESIGN LEAD BY PROFESSOR CHRIS WEINBERGER AT COLORADO STATE UNIVERSITY. THE CHARACTERIZATION WILL INCLUDE ROOM TEMPERATURE FRACTURE TOUGHNESS MEASUREMENTS AND TENSILE TESTS TO DETERMINE DUCTILITY. USING A NON-CONTACT MEANS OF THERMO-MECHANICAL TESTING, THOMPSON WILL ALSO PERFORM HIGH TEMPERATURE FLEXURAL AND CREEP TEST. FINALLY, THE CHARACTERIZATION WILL INCLUDE MICROSTRUCTURAL QUANTIFICATION, INCLUDING THE USE OF ELECTRON MICROSCOPY TO DETERMINE THE PHASE, GRAIN SIZE, AND CHEMICAL DISTRIBUTIONS OF THE FABRICATED COMPOSITES. THOMPSON WILL PROVIDE MONTHLY STATUS REPORTS TO PROFESSOR WEINBERGER IN THE FORMS OF EMAIL COMMUNICATIONS, POWERPOINTS, AND TECHNICAL MANUSCRIPTS THROUGH THE COURSE OF THE PROGRAM OR AS NEEDED FOR REPORTING TO THE AGENCY.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: UNDERSTANDING AND ENGINEERING TRANSIENT MECHANICAL RESPONSES IN NANOPARTICLE-REINFORCED HETEROGENOUS PARTICULATE SYSTEMS
subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
HIGHLY HETEROGENEOUS MIXTURES OF ATTRACTIVE FINE AND INERT COARSE PARTICLES EXHIBIT A RICH ARRAY OF BEHAVIOR IN RESPONSE TO MECHANICAL LOADING. WE AIM TO UNDERSTAND THE REVERSIBLE TRANSITION BETWEEN SOLID AND FLOWING STATES IN HIGHLY HETEROGENEOUS PARTICULATE SYSTEMS WITH HIGH SOLIDS LOADING AND VARIABLE MOISTURE CONTENT, WHICH IS CRUCIAL FOR APPLICATIONS RANGING FROM SOILS WITH ENGINEERED STRESS-STRAIN-TIME RESPONSE TO NOVEL PARTICULATE INKS FOR ADDITIVE MANUFACTURING. TO ACHIEVE THIS OVERARCHING GOAL, WE WILL CONDUCT A TIGHTLY INTEGRATED PROGRAM OF RESEARCH THAT COMBINES MULTISCALE MODELING, GEOTECHNICAL AND RHEOLOGICAL CHARACTERIZATION, 4D IMAGING, AND MICROSCOPIC THEORY FOCUSED ON HIGHLY HETEROGENEOUS PARTICULATE SYSTEMS. OUR RESEARCH EFFORTS WILL BE DIVIDED INTO TWO MAJOR THRUSTS FOCUSED ON: (1) MULTISCALE THEORETICAL AND COMPUTATIONAL MODELING OF HIGHLY HETEROGENEOUS PARTICULATE SYSTEMS AND (2) FOUNDATIONAL EXPERIMENTS TO ELUCIDATE THEIR TRANSIENT MECHANICAL BEHAVIOR AND STRUCTURAL EVOLUTION UNDER MECHANICAL LOAD. THROUGH THESE CLOSELY INTERTWINING THRUSTS, WE WILL ESTABLISH A PREDICTIVE FRAMEWORK, VALIDATED BY EXPERIMENT, FOR ENGINEERING HIGHLY HETEROGENEOUS PARTICLE SYSTEMS FOR APPLICATIONS RANGING FROM GENERATING ROBUST SURFACE TERRAINS FOR IMPROVED VEHICLE MOBILITY TO META-STRUCTURED INKS FOR 3D PRINTING OF LARGE, HIGH-ASPECT RATIO STRUCTURES. A PRIMARY OUTCOME OF OUR EFFORT WILL BE DESIGN RULES FOR REALIZING A DIVERSE ARRAY OF MACROSCOPIC MECHANICAL RESPONSES BASED ON TUNABLE MICROSCOPIC INTERACTIONS THAT ARISE BETWEEN FINE PARTICLE AND GRANULAR NETWORKS.
THE MAJORITY OF THE PI'S (4 OUT OF 6) ARE FROM UIUC. THEIR ROLES ARE BROAD AND MULTIFACETED, BUT FALL INTO THE GENERAL CATEGORIES AS FOLLOWS. NANOSCALE STATISTICAL MECHANICAL THEORY (SCHWEIZER), MACROSCALE GRANULAR AND CONTINUUM MODELING (HASHASH), SYNTHESIS AND STRUCTURAL CHARACTERIZATION (BRAUN), AND MECHANICAL MEASUREMENTS ON ENGINEERED SOILS AND MODEL SYSTEMS (OLSEN).

subaward_description: AS A SUB-AWARDEE TO UM ON THE MURI GRANT OF ÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã…Â€ŒACTIVE AND RECONFIGURABLE TOPOLOGICAL MECHANICAL METAMATERIALS (TMM) FROM THE NANOSCALE TO THE MACROSCALE,ÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½ Q. CHENÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S ROLE AS A CO-PI IS TO UTILIZE HER EXPERTISE IN COLLOIDAL NANOPARTICLE SYNTHESIS, SELF-ASSEMBLY AND LIQUID-PHASE TRANSMISSION ELECTRON MICROSCOPY (TEM) IMAGING TO REALIZE, MAKE AND UNDERSTAND TOPOLOGICAL MECHANICAL METAMATERIALS AT THE NANO AND MICROSCALE. IN PARTICULAR, SHE WILL CONTRIBUTE TO BOTH THRUSTS PROPOSED IN THE WORK AS DETAILED AS FOLLOWS. FIRST, SHE WILL WORK ON PROJECT 1 (TMMS WITH RECONFIGURABLE SURFACE STIFFNESS) AND PROJECT 2 (TMMS WITH RECONFIGURABLE STRESS DISTRIBUTION) OF THRUST I (REAL-TIME RECONFIGURABLE ACTIVE TMMS) ON THE NANOSCOPIC REALIZATION OF THESE METAMATERIALS. SHE WILL COLLABORATE CLOSELY WITH THE PI MAO AND CO-PI KOTOV (BOTH AT UM) TO DESIGN PATCHY NANOPARTICLE BUILDING BLOCKS TO REALIZE, FOR THE FIRST TIME, THE MAXWELL LATTICES CONSISTING OF NANOSCALE BUILDING BLOCKS WITH RECONFIGURABLE STRESS DISTRIBUTION VIA SPONTANEOUS, IN-SOLUTION ASSEMBLY OF THESE PATCHY NANOPARTICLES. HER EXPERTISE IN LIQUID-PHASE TEM WILL ALLOW THE OBSERVATION OF THE MAXWELL LATTICES AS THEY STRUCTURALLY RECONFIGURE (THEREBY INDUCING DIFFERENT MECHANICAL RESPONSE), ESPECIALLY AT AGITATIONS (E.G., LIGHT, FLOW, TEMPERATURE). IN THESE PROJECTS, BESIDES THE SYNTHETIC EFFORTS, CHEN WILL ALSO UTILIZE LIQUID-PHASE TEM AS A NEW APPROACH TO CHARACTERIZE AND MEASURE THE MODES FROM THE MOTIONS OF INDIVIDUAL NANOPARTICLES IN THE LATTICE SITES OF TMMS, TO ASSIST THE THEORETICAL MODELING BY MAO AND SUN. THE CHARACTERIZATION WILL BE DONE NOT ONLY ON THE TMMS MADE BY CHEN, BUT ALSO ON THE NANOFIBER NETWORKS MADE BY KOTOV. SECOND, SHE WILL WORK ON PROJECT 2 (FRACTIONAL SOLITON DYNAMICS, BROKEN TRS, AND SELF-PROPULSION) OF THRUST II (FRACTIONAL TOPOLOGICAL EXCITATIONS AND ASSOCIATED NONRECIPROCAL ELASTICITY AND ACTIVE SELF-PROPULSION) BY USING SELF-ASSEMBLY OF JANUS PARTICLES, WHICH HAVE A GENERIC SYMMETRY BREAKING, TO ACHIEVE CHIRAL CHAINS AS MICROSCALE SOLITONS. THESE CHIRAL CHAINS CAN SWITCH THEIR CHIRALITY UPON THERMAL FLUCTUATIONS, AS A WAY TO MODULATE MECHANICAL RESPONSE AS GUIDED BY PI MAO AND THE CO-PI SUNÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S THEORETICAL EFFORTS.

PROJECT GOALS:
THE OVERARCHING GOAL OF THE PROPOSED WORK IS TO IMPROVE THE SURGICAL TREATMENT OUTCOMES OF FAI, WHICH IS AFFECTING AN INCREASING NUMBER OF MILITARY PERSONNEL AND YOUNG ACTIVE INDIVIDUALS IN THE GENERAL POPULATION. THIS PROJECT SPECIFICALLY SEEKS TO COVER THE FY2018 PRORP CTRA SURGICAL CARE FOCUS AREA OF OSTEOARTHRITIS. FAI IS THE MOST COMMON CAUSE OF HIP OA AND IS A COMPLEX PRE-ARTHRITIC DISORDER AFFECTING AN INCREASING NUMBER OF MILITARY PERSONNEL AND YOUNG ACTIVE INDIVIDUALS IN THE GENERAL POPULATION. THE PROPOSED STUDIES WILL INVESTIGATE CRITICAL PATIENT, DISEASE, AND SURGICAL TREATMENT PREDICTORS OF FAI SURGERY OUTCOMES. THESE INVESTIGATIONS ARE PARAMOUNT TO IMPROVEMENTS IN OPTIMIZING SURGICAL OUTCOMES, RETAINING THE FORCE FIT FOR DUTY AND IN DELAYING OR PREVENTING PROGRESSION TO HIP OA OVER THE LIFETIME OF MILITARY PERSONNEL AND THE GENERAL POPULATION.

subaward_description:
THE SPECIFIC ACTIVITIES THAT XULA, TU, AND JSU WILL ENGAGE IN INCLUDE:
1) SUMMER EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING, HOSTED BY USM.
2) HIRING OF OCEAN SCIENCE OUTREACH AND EDUCATION COORDINATORS.
3) JSU ONLY: JSU WILL CONDUCT A FEASIBILITY STUDY FOR ESTABLISHING JSU TIGERSUB TEAM.
SPECIFICALLY, JSU WILL EXPLORE THE POSSIBILITY OF LEVERAGING STRENGTHS IN THE AREAS OF ELECTRICAL AND
COMPUTER ENGINEERING, AI, AND COMPUTER SCIENCE TO OVERCOME THE LIMITATIONS OF A LACK OF A
MECHANICAL ENGINEERING PROGRAM TO FORM COMPETITIVE TEAM THAT WORKS COLLABORATIVELY TO BUILD A
CUSTOM AUTONOMOUS UNDERWATER VEHICLE TO COMPETE IN THE ROBONATIONÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S ROBOSUB COMPETITION.
THE TIGERSUB TEAM WOULD PROVIDE AN ADDITIONAL AVENUE FOR REGULAR STUDENT ENGAGEMENT
THROUGHOUT THE ACADEMIC YEAR AND COULD ALSO BE INCORPORATED IN CERTAIN COURSES (I.E., SENIOR
DESIGN). PART OF THE SCOPING ACTIVITIES WILL INCLUDE EXPLORING THE POSSIBILITY OF ESTABLISHING A
MULTI-INSTITUTIONAL TEAM THAT WOULD INCORPORATE THE ENGINEERING EXPERTISE OF TU AND XULA. IF
FEASIBLE, A SEPARATE FOLLOW-UP REQUEST FOR FUNDS TO ESTABLISH THE TIGERSUB TEAM WILL BE SUBMITTED,
PER ONR GUIDANCE. FOLLOWING THE FEASIBILITY STUDY, DEPENDING ON THE FINDINGS, WE WOULD CREATE A
SEPARATE REQUEST FOR THE FORMATION OF THE TIGERSUB TEAM.
4) DEVELOPMENT OF COLLABORATIVE 3+2 EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS BETWEEN XULA, TU,
AND USM.
5. RESEARCH MATCHMAKING BETWEEN POTENTIAL PIS AT THE MSIS WITH ESTABLISHED PIS AT R1
INSTITUTIONS AND NAVY SCIENCE ENTITIES SUCH AS NRL.
6. SUPPORTING STUDENT RETAINMENT THROUGH REGULAR OS-RELATED ACADEMIC YEAR EVENTS. BEYOND THE
SUMMER PROGRAMS AND SPECIAL EFFORTS SUCH AS A ROBOSUB TEAM, AS WELL AS OUR RECRUITMENT EFFORTS,
WE SEEK TO INCREASE AWARENESS OF ONR-ALIGNED CAREERS THROUGH REGULAR ON-CAMPUS ACTIVITIES
INTEGRATED INTO OCEAN EXPLORERS CLUBS (OEC). FOR TU AND JSU, THE OEC IS A STUDENT
ORGANIZATION, FUNDED IN PART BY THE NOAA OCEAN EXPLORATION COOPERATIVE INSTITUTE, THAT IS
FOCUSED ON INCREASING AWARENESS OF OS-RELATED CAREERS. THE CLUBS MEET REGULARLY FOR A RANGE OF
OS-RELATED EVENTS INCLUDING OKEANOS EXPLORER WATCH PARTIES, GUEST SPEAKERS, SHARK DISSECTIONS,
SEAGLIDER COMPETITIONS, WATER CHEMISTRY CHALLENGES, AND OTHERS. BASED ON THE EXPERIENCE OF TU
AND JSU, A SIMILAR CLUB WILL BE CREATED AT XULA. ALL SOUTH COAST MSI PARTNERS REQUEST FUNDS TO
TAKE STUDENTS FROM THE INSTITUTIONS TO NAVOCEANO AND NRL-STENNIS ONCE PER YEAR AND TO
PROVIDE ADDITIONAL ACTIVITIES FOR THE OEC. THIS COULD INCLUDE GAMES AROUND THE USE OF SONAR
FOR BATHYMETRY, ADDITIONAL SEAGLIDER KITS FOR STUDENT USE, FOOD FOR ONR-ALIGNED GUEST SPEAKERS,
AND OTHERS. WE ARE ALSO REQUEST FUNDS EACH YEAR TO BUY T-SHIRTS FOR THE OEC THAT WOULD INCLUDE
ONR-D-E AND NOAA-OECI BRANDING TO FURTHER PROMOTE THE CLUB AND ITS MISSION.
