prime_award_base_transaction_description: DIABETES SUBTYPES AND LETHAL PROSTATE CANCER ACROSS PUERTO RICAN, AFRICAN AMERICAN, AND NON-HISPANIC WHITE VETERANS
subawardee_business_types: NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION
subaward_description:
PUERTO RICAN MEN HAVE A HIGHER PROSTATE CANCER MORTALITY COMPARED TO NON-HISPANIC WHITE MEN OR HISPANIC MEN LIVING IN THE CONTINENTAL U.S. INDEED, PROSTATE CANCER MORTALITY AMONG PUERTO RICAN MEN IS SECOND ONLY TO BLACK MEN IN THE U.S. HOWEVER, PROSTATE CANCER IN PUERTO RICO REMAINS LARGELY UNINVESTIGATED. THE MAJORITY OF PAST STUDIES COMBINED MEN FROM ALL
SPANISH-SPEAKING REGIONS INTO A SINGLE GROUP, USUALLY LABELED AS HISPANIC OR LATINO, AND THUS IGNORED THE POTENTIAL DIFFERENCES IN CULTURE, ECONOMICS, LIFESTYLE, OR GENETIC RISK BETWEEN THESE REGIONS THAT COULD IMPACT PROSTATE CANCER RISK AND PROGNOSIS. OUR ANALYSES OF PROSTATE CANCER PATIENTS TREATED WITH RADICAL PROSTATECTOMY AT THE SAN JUAN VETERANS ADMINISTRATION (VA) MEDICAL CENTER FOUND THAT MEN IN PUERTO RICO HAVE A SIGNIFICANTLY HIGHER PROSTATE CANCER MORTALITY THAN OTHER VA MEDICAL CENTERS, DESPITE PATIENTS IN PUERTO RICO HAVING BETTER PROGNOSTIC MARKERS AND LESS ADVANCED TUMORS AT DIAGNOSIS. INDEED, PROSTATE CANCER IS THE
MOST COMMON INVASIVE MALIGNANCY DIAGNOSED IN PUERTO RICO, AND AT PRESENT, THE REASONS WHY MEN IN PUERTO RICO HAVE A HIGHER PROSTATE CANCER MORTALITY THAN OTHER HISPANIC OR NON-HISPANIC WHITE COMMUNITIES IS UNKNOWN. ONE POTENTIAL EXPLANATION IS THAT PUERTO RICO HAS THE HIGHEST PREVALENCE OF TYPE 2 DIABETES AMONG ALL U.S. STATES AND TERRITORIES. APPROXIMATELY 27% OF PUERTO RICANS REPORT HAVING DIABETES COMPARED TO 16% IN THE CONTINENTAL U.S. WE, AND OTHERS, HAVE REPORTED THAT PROSTATE CANCER PATIENTS WITH DIABETES (ESPECIALLY OBESE DIABETICS) HAVE A POORER PROGNOSIS, HOWEVER THE RESEARCH ON THIS QUESTION IS INCONSISTENT. ONE CHALLENGE IS THAT DIABETES IS NOT ONE DISEASE, BUT RATHER RECENT STUDIES SUGGEST DIABETES CAN BE DIVIDED INTO MULTIPLE DIFFERENT TYPES OF DIABETES, AND PATIENTS WHO APPEAR TO HAVE A SIMILAR TYPE OF DIABETES MAY VARY WIDELY IN THEIR RESPONSE TO TREATMENT. INDEED, IN WORK SUPPORTING THIS CONCEPT, WE DESCRIBE A SIGNIFICANT INTERACTION BETWEEN DIABETES AND OBESITY, SUCH THAT AMONG OBESE MEN, DIABETES WAS ASSOCIATED WITH AN OVERALL 3-FOLD INCREASED RISK OF PROSTATE CANCER DEATH.
IN CONTRAST, DIABETES WAS UNRELATED TO PROSTATE CANCER DEATH AMONG NON-OBESE MEN. THIS SUGGESTS THE COMPLEX METABOLIC DYSREGULATION FOUND IN OBESE DIABETICS AFFECTS PROSTATE CANCER PROGNOSIS. HOWEVER, THIS ANALYSIS WAS IN A LARGELY NON-HISPANIC WHITE STUDY POPULATION, AND IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER RACE/ETHNICITY DIFFERENCES IN THE PATHOLOGY OF DIABETES CONTRIBUTE TO RACE/ETHNICITY DIFFERENCES IN PROSTATE CANCER PROGRESSION. THE PURPOSE OF THIS STUDY IS TO TEST THE IDEA THAT DIFFERENCES IN HOW DIABETES AND OBESITY INTERACT BETWEEN PUERTO RICAN, NON-HISPANIC WHITE, AND BLACK MEN MAY HELP TO EXPLAIN AN UNEXPECTEDLY HIGH PROSTATE CANCER MORTALITY IN PUERTO RICO. WE PROPOSE A SERIES OF NEW STUDIES THAT COMPARES THE EFFECTS OF DIABETES AND OBESITY ON PROSTATE CANCER PROGNOSIS AMONG PUERTO RICAN PATIENTS SEEKING CARE AT THE SAN JUAN VA MEDICAL CENTER TO BLACK AND NON-HISPANIC WHITE PATIENTS SEEKING CARE AT THE
DURHAM VA MEDICAL CENTER IN NORTH CAROLINA. OUR FIRST STUDY WILL DETERMINE IF DIABETES INTERACTS WITH OBESITY TO INCREASE LETHAL PROSTATE CANCER IN PUERTO RICAN MEN OR BLACK MEN, SIMILAR TO WHAT WE PREVIOUSLY SAW IN WHITE MEN. AS BOTH DIABETES AND LETHAL
PROSTATE CANCER ARE MORE PREVALENT IN PUERTO RICO, WE HYPOTHESIZE A LINK BETWEEN DIABETES AND OBESITY LEADING TO LETHAL PROSTATE CANCER, WHICH WILL BE STRONGER AMONG PUERTO RICAN MEN COMPARED TO NON-HISPANIC WHITE OR BLACK MEN. OUR SECOND STUDY WILL RECRUIT DIABETIC PROSTATE CANCER PATIENTS IN SAN JUAN, AND WE WILL COMPARE GENE EXPRESSION PATHWAYS IN PROSTATE TISSUE BETWEEN PUERTO RICAN, BLACK AND NON-HISPANIC WHITE PATIENTS (THE NON-PUERTO RICAN MEN HAVE ALREADY BEEN ACCRUED). OUR ANALYSIS WILL BE THE FIRST TO INVESTIGATE THE IMPACT OF DIABETES ON PROSTATE TISSUE ACROSS PUERTO RICAN, BLACK, AND NON-HISPANIC WHITE MEN. OUR FINDINGS CAN THEN BE TRANSLATED TO PERSONALIZED CLINICAL INTERVENTIONS DESIGNED INTERVENE ON

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
"DETER DEVELOPS A NOVEL MULTI-MATERIAL ARCHITECTURE APPROACH TO REDUCE THE THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY OF STRUCTURAL MATERIALS AT 1800ÃƑÂ€ŠÃ‚Â°C AND ABOVE. THE ARCHITECTURES FUNCTION AS ULTRA-HIGH TEMPERATURE DIODES THAT DISRUPT ELECTRON TRANSPORT WITH SCHOTTKY METAL/INSULATOR INTERFACES, MINIMIZE PHONON PROPAGATION WITH APERIODIC LAYERS, AND REFLECT RADIANT ENERGY WITH METALLIC META-GEOMETRIES. HIGH ENTROPY CARBIDE CERAMICS WILL BE COMBINED WITH RARE EARTH ZIRCONATES AND ALUMINATES INTO STRUCTURES THAT ARE THERMOCHEMICALLY STABLE, MECHANICALLY ROBUST, AND THERMALLY INSULATING.
DETER IMPLEMENTS A CONCEPTUALLY-SIMPLE ALTERNATIVE STRATEGY BASED ON A MULTI-CARRIER DIODE APPROACH THAT WILL BE BROUGHT TO FRUITION BY A COMPLEMENTARY COMPUTATIONAL AND EXPERIMENTAL TEAM. DUKE-DETER RESPONSABILITIES FOCUSE IN TWO KEY AREAS: I. HARACTERIZING ELECTRON AND PHONON TRANSPORT AT INTERFACES; II. ELUCIDATING ULTRA-HIGH TEMPERATURE MECHANICAL AND THERMAL BEHAVIOR. THEORISTS HAVE USED COMPUTATIONS TO DEMONSTRATE THE FEASIBILITY OF THE DIODE APPROACH. DURING DETER, DUKEÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢ THEORETICIANS WILL REFINE MODELS AND PROVIDE INSIGHT INTO ELECTRON AND PHONON TRANSPORT MECHANISMS THROUGH INTERFACES AND MULTI-MATERIAL SYSTEMS. NON DUKE` EXPERIMENTALISTS WILL FABRICATE COMPOSITIONS AND DESIGNS DETERMINED BY THEORISTS THEN, CHARACTERIZE BEHAVIOR, AND PROVIDE DATA TO ENABLE REFINEMENT OF MODELS."

subaward_description:
FY 2024 (5 MONTHS): GUNEY WILL ASSIST YELIN TO DEVELOP THE GENERAL NONLINEAR QUANTUM OPTICAL THEORETICAL FRAMEWORK FOR ENZ MEDIA. ADDITIONALLY, WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF YELIN, GUNEY WILL DEVELOP THE NONLINEAR QUANTUM OPTICAL THEORY OF CROSS-PHASE MODULATION IN HOMOGENEOUS ENZ MATERIALS (E.G., CDO AND TOPOLOGICAL INSULATORS).
FY2025 (12 MONTHS): WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF YELIN AND OZDEMIR, GUNEY WILL DEVELOP THE QND THEORY BASED ON HOMOGENEOUS ENZ HOSTS AND DETERMINE THE REQUIRED MATERIAL PROPERTIES FOR HOMOGENEOUS ENZ MATERIALS (E.G., CDO, TOPOLOGICAL INSULATORS) TO MEET THE QND DETECTION CONDITIONS FOR SINGLE PHOTONS, AS WELL AS BALANCED HOMODYNE DETECTOR (BHD) REQUIREMENTS.
THESE REQUIREMENTS WILL BE PROVIDED TO MARIA AND XU TO CONSIDER IN THE FABRICATION AND SYNTHESIS, AND THE ACTIVITIES IN IRTS 2 AND 3, AND TO OZDEMIR AND CALDWELL TO CONSIDER IN THE QND MEASUREMENTS. ADDITIONALLY, WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF YELIN, GUNEY WILL CONTINUE TO EXTEND THE RESULTS FOR HOMOGENEOUS ENZ MATERIALS (CDO) AND QND THEORY TO WAVEGUIDE-PATTERNED GEOMETRIES AND DETERMINE THE REQUIREMENTS FOR SUCH WAVEGUIDES TO BE UTILIZED IN QND DETECTION OF SINGLE PHOTONS. THESE RESULTS WILL AGAIN BE PROVIDED TO MARIA, XU, OZDEMIR, AND CALDWELL TO GUIDE THEIR ACTIVITIES. ALSO, GUNEY WILL ASSIST XU TO SCREEN CANDIDATE TOPOLOGICAL MATERIALS WITH THE GOAL OF IDENTIFYING THE IDEAL TOPOLOGICAL MATERIALS WITH THE STRONGEST MWIR PHOTOVOLTAIC RESPONSE AND THE LOWEST DARK CURRENT.

FY2026 (12 MONTHS): WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF YELIN, GUNEY WILL EXTEND THE NONLINEAR QUANTUM OPTICAL THEORY OF CROSS-PHASE MODULATION AND THE QND THEORY TO FREE-SPACE METAMATERIALS (I.E., PLASMONIC METAMATERIALS AND DIELECTRIC ZERO-INDEX METAMATERIALS) AND THEIR WAVEGUIDE ARCHITECTURES. THESE WILL PROVIDE THE REQUIRED MATERIAL PROPERTIES FOR THE QND DETECTION OF SINGLE PHOTONS BASED ON FREE-SPACE ENZ METAMATERIALS, AS WELL AS THE NEW BALANCED HOMODYNE DETECTOR REQUIREMENTS. THESE REQUIREMENTS WILL BE PROVIDED TO MAZUR, MARIA, XU, OZDEMIR, AND CALDWELL TO CONSIDER IN THE FABRICATION, QND MEASUREMENTS, AND THE ACTIVITIES IN IRTS 2 AND 3.
ADDITIONALLY, GUNEY WILL ASSIST MARIA FOR THE DFT CALCULATIONS.

FY2027 (FIRST 7 MONTHS): GUNEY WILL ASSIST YELIN TO DESIGN A HETEROSTRUCTURE ENZ/2D MATERIAL FUNCTIONING AS A PERFECT ABSORBER WHERE THE OPTICAL ABSORPTION IS LOCALIZED IN THE ACTIVE REGION TO MAXIMIZE THE QUANTUM EFFICIENCY. THE ENZ SUBSTRATE IN THE HETEROSTRUCTURE WILL BE BASED ON FREE-SPACE ENZ (META)MATERIAL DESIGNS FROM FY25 AND FY26. THE BLUEPRINTS OF THE PERFECT ABSORBER WILL BE THEN PROVIDED TO MAZUR, MARIA, AND XU TO GUIDE THE FABRICATION AND INTEGRATION OF THE HETEROSTRUCTURE TO BE USED IN IRT 2.

subaward_description:
JON-PAUL MARIA:
1 PREPARE CDO THIN FILMS THAT WILL BE SUPPLIED TO ALL IRTS FOR FABRICATION AND TESTING OF (1) CROSS-PHASE MODULATION HOSTS, (2) OPTICAL FIELD CONCENTRATION STACKS FOR ADVANCED PHOTOVOLTAIC DETECTORS, AND (3) PLASMONIC THERMOPILE DETECTORS.
2 USING A THERMODYNAMIC APPROACH BASED ON TUNING CHEMICAL POTENTIAL DURING CDO FILM GROWTH, MARIA WILL IDENTIFY SYNTHESIS CONDITIONS THAT ALLOW IMPROVED CRYSTAL STRUCTURE, IMPROVED INTERFACIAL EPITAXY, AND BETTER CONTROLLED POINT DEFECT CHEMISTRY SO THAT HIGHER MOBILITY PLASMONIC LAYERS WITH SHARPER, HIGHER Q, PLASMON RESONANCES ARE ACHIEVED. THIS ACTIVITY WILL REQUIRE SERIAL ITERATION BETWEEN THIN FILM DEPOSITION, X-RAY ANALYSIS OF CRYSTAL STRUCTURE, IR ELLIPSOMETRIC MEASUREMENT FOR COMPLEX INDEX ANALYSIS, HALL MEASUREMENTS FOR TRANSPORT, AND TEMPERATURE DEPENDENT (CRYOGENIC) TRANSPORT MEASUREMENTS TO GLEAN IMPURITY LIMITED PROPERTIES.
3 COLLABORATIVELY DEVELOP AND DESIGN THERMOPILE STRUCTURES (GUNEY, OZDEMIR, CALDWELL) THAT EXPLORE DETECTIVITY AND SIGNAL-TO-NOISE LIMITS OF PLASMONIC BOLOMETERS. THIS WILL REQUIRE LITHOGRAPHIC PROCESSING AT THE PSU AND VANDERBILT NANOFABRICATION FACILITIES TO DEFINE METAL BILAYER STACKS, TO IMPLEMENT THERMAL ISOLATION STRATEGIES, TO MITIGATE STRESS GENERATION, AND TO GENERATE EFFICIENT AND PROBABLY PROBING SCHEMES TO QUANTIFY SCALING RELATIONSHIPS.
4 COLLABORATIVELY WORK WITH THE THEORY TEAM (GUNEY AND YELIN) TO TEST COMPUTATIONAL PREDICTIONS ASSOCIATED WITH CDO OPTICAL THIRD ORDER NON-LINEAR RESPONSES, WITH THE NANOPHOTONIC COMPUTATION TEAMS (MAZUR AND CALDWELL) TO TEST ARCHITECTURES FOR OPTICAL FIELD CONFINEMENT IN ENZ-BASED DEVICES, AND WITH THE QND TEAM (OZDEMIR, GUNEY, CALDWELL, YANG, MAZUR, YELIN) TO INTEGRATE CDO-BASED THERMOPILE DETECTORS FOR SINGLE PHOTON COUNTING AT ROOM TEMPERATURE.
5 PARTICIPATE ON ALL MURI COLLABORATIVE ACTIVITIES INCLUDING ANNUAL REVIEWS, MONTHLY MEETINGS, AND PROGRAM REVIEWS. THIS INCLUDES PREPARING ALL CONTENT NEEDED FOR ANNUAL REPORTING.
6 DISSEMINATE RESEARCH FINDINGS IN THE FORM OF TECHNICAL PEER-REVIEWED MANUSCRIPTS AND INTERNATIONAL SCIENTIFIC CONFERENCES.
SAHIN OZDEMIR:
(PSU) HAS A STRONG TRACK RECORD IN THEORETICAL AND EXPERIMENTAL ASPECTS IN QUANTUM PHOTONICS (E.G., PREPARATION, MANIPULATION AND CHARACTERIZATION OF ENTANGLED STATES, SINGLE PHOTON SOURCES, DECOHERENCE FREE SPACES, BALANCED DETECTION, ETC) AND PIONEERING CONTRIBUTIONS TO PARITY-TIME (PT) SYMMETRIC AND NON-HERMITIAN OPTICS. IN THIS PROJECT, HE IS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE OVERALL DESIGN AND IMPLEMENTATION OF THE PROJECT TASKS ASSIGNED TO HIS GROUP AT PENN STATE. HE WILL OVERSEE THE DIRECTION OF THE RESEARCH EFFORT AND GUIDE THE GRADUATE RESEARCH ASSISTANTS AND POSTDOCTORAL SCHOLAR. HIS GROUP WILL PERFORM THEORETICAL AND EXPERIMENTAL STUDIES ON QUANTUM NONDEMOLITIONAL DETECTION (QND) OF SINGLE- AND FEW-PHOTON STATES IN THE MID-IR.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: [MURI] DETER: DISTRUPTING ELECTRON TRANSPORT TO MINIMIZE THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY
subawardee_business_types: OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
"BACKGROUND:
REDUCING THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY OF STRUCTURAL MATERIALS AT ULTRA-HIGH TEMPERATURE (UHT) HAS BEEN AN UNATTAINABLE GOAL FOR THE LAST THIRTY YEARS. DESPITE SIGNIFICANT RESEARCH EFFORTS, VERY LITTLE ADVANCEMENT HAS BEEN ACHIEVED. TRADITIONAL RESEARCH INDIVIDUALLY OPTIMIZES THE THREE CONTRIBUTIONS TO TOTAL THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY: PHONON/VIBRATIONAL (KL), ELECTRON (KE), AND PHOTON (KP). AT LOW/MEDIUM T, THE MECHANISMS ARE INDEPENDENT AND CAN BE INDIVIDUALLY CONTROLLED BY MANIPULATING THEIR SOURCES: PHONONS, CHARGE CARRIERS IN CONDUCTORS, AND PHOTONS (NEGLIGIBLE). AT LOW/MEDIUM T, KL DOMINATES THE THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY OF MOST CERAMICS AND KL > KE, EVEN FOR ELECTRICALLY CONDUCTIVE CERAMICS LIKE CARBIDES OR CARBON-CARBON COMPOSITES. AT UHT, THE CONVENTIONAL APPROACH FAILS. FOR EXAMPLE, MINIMIZATION OF ONLY KL IS NOT SUFFICIENT AND NEEDS TO BE RECONSIDERED DUE TO THE EXPONENTIAL INCREASE IN THE POPULATION OF HEAT-CARRYING ELECTRONS, AND RADIATION THROUGH THE MATERIAL. FURTHERMORE, AT UHT THE DOMINANT HEAT-CARRIERS INTRINSICALLY CHANGE, AND OFTEN INCREASE EACH OTHERÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S POPULATIONS, INCREASING THE TOTAL THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY (KL
< KE, EVEN FOR ELECTRICAL INSULATORS). THEREFORE, EMBRACING AN APPROACH THAT CONSIDERS MATERIALS, MICROSTRUCTURES, AND INTERFACES AS A WHOLE IS ESSENTIAL TO EXTRINSICALLY CONTROL ALL THE THREE HEAT- CARRIERS SOURCES SIMULTANEOUSLY, WHILE MAINTAINING STRUCTURAL INTEGRITY. WE PROPOSE DISRUPTING ELECTRON TRANSPORT TO MINIMIZE THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY (DETER) TO TARGET STRUCTURAL AND THERMOCHEMICAL STABILITY WITH GOALS OF FLEXURAL STRENGTH =300 MPA AND THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY =5 W/MÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã‚Â¢K AT =1800ÃƑÂ€ŠÃ‚Â°C. THE MATERIALS OUTCOME WILL BE AN APERIODIC ORGANIZATION OF DISORDERED CERAMICS, WITH BOTH METALLIC AND INSULATING CHARACTERISTICS: I. METAL/INSULATING INTERFACES WILL CREATE SCHOTTKY BARRIERS AGAINST ELECTRON TRANSPORT; II. FIBONACCI APERIODICITY WILL DISTURB PHONON PROPAGATION BY INCREASING THEIR RECOMBINATION; AND III. REFLECTIVE META-GEOMETRIES WILL DIRECT THE RADIANCE TOWARD THE HEAT SOURCE.

OBJECTIVES
THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS RESEARCH IS TO INVESTIGATE NOVEL MATERIALS AND MATERIAL ARCHITECTURES TO SUBSTANTIALLY LOWER ELECTRON TRANSPORT CONTRIBUTIONS TO THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY. THREE PRIMARY THRUST AREAS WILL BE EXPLORED INCLUDING ELECTRICAL CONTROL, VIBRATIONAL CONTROL, AND RADIATIVE CONTROL TO REDUCE ALL THREE PRIMARY CONTRIBUTIONS TO THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY. MATERIAL SYSTEMS WILL LIKELY FOCUSED ON ULTRA-HIGH TEMPERATURE CARBIDE CERAMICS, RARE-EARTH ZIRCONATES, AND DISPERSED PHASE ADDITIVES.

ARL-PENN STATE TASKS ARE TO:
1.	ASSIST IN IDENTIFYING INSULATING PHASE MATERIAL AND MATERIAL SYSTEMS
2.	CONDUCT PRELIMINARY MATERIALS SYNTHESIS TRIALS UTILIZING FIELD ASSISTED SINTERING TECHNOLOGY (FAST), PHYSICAL VAPOR DEPOSITION (PVD), AND/OR CHEMICAL VAPOR DEPOSITION (CVD)
3.	EXPLORE CONCEPTS INCLUDING MULTILAYER, COMPOSITE, AND CORE-SHELL/ENCAPSULATED STRUCTURES
4.	FABRICATE MATERIALS WITH COMPLEX ARCHITECTURES
5.	CONDUCT ANALYTICAL MATERIALS CHARACTERIZATION
6.	COLLABORATE WITH MURI TEAM MEMBERS DURING BIWEEKLY MEETINGS AND ANNUAL REPORTING

DELIVERABLES
SELECT DATA, RESULTS, MATERIAL, AND RECOMMENDATIONS ON A BEST EFFORT BASIS."

subaward_description:
"1.0	SCOPE. THIS SECTION SHOULD DESCRIBE, IN GENERAL TERMS, THE WORK EFFORT THAT WILL BE PERFORMED BY THE CONTRACTOR.

THE EFFORT WILL INCLUDE 3 MAIN COMPONENTS; ONE FOCUSED ON CHARACTERIZING BUILT-IN POTENTIAL AT PHASE BOUNDARIES, ONE FOCUSED ON CHARACTERIZING VIBRATIONAL MODES AROUND INTERFACES, AND ONE FOCUSED ON CHARACTERING INTERFACIAL STRESS-STRAIN RESPONSE AT BICRYSTALS. THE FIRST TWO COMPONENTS WILL BE PERFORMED BY A POSTDOC APPOINTED TO THE PROJECT, WHILE THE LATTER COMPONENT WILL BE PERFORMED BY A GRADUATE STUDENT. THIS WORK WILL SUPPORT THE OVERALL PROJECT BY PROVIDING 1) PROVIDING INSIGHTS INTO LOCAL MECHANISMS FOR ELECTRON AND PHONON TRANSPORT ACROSS INTERFACES THAT CAN BE USED TO VALIDATE THEORY WORK AND INTERPRET BULK EXPERIMENTAL RESULTS AND 2) ENABLE AN UNDERSTANDING OF HOW THERMAL PROPERTIES AND MECHANICAL PROPERTIES COULD BE CO-OPTIMIZED WITHIN THE PROPOSED DESIGN SCHEME.


2.0	REQUIREMENTS. THIS SECTION MUST DESCRIBE EACH TASK(S) THE CONTRACTOR SHALL PERFORM USING COMPLETE SENTENCES, ACTIVE VOICE, AND MANDATORY TERMS (ÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã…Â€ŒTHE CONTRACTOR SHALLÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½). THE TASK(S) SHALL BE ARRANGED SYSTEMATICALLY AND LOGICALLY SO THAT BOTH PARTIES (CONTRACTOR AND GOVERNMENT) UNDERSTAND THE DESIRED EFFORT. ALL TASK(S) MUST BE LISTED IN CHRONOLOGICAL ORDER. THE TASK(S) MUST BE EXPLAINED IN CLEAR AND UNDERSTANDABLE TERMS. INCLUDE ANY INDUSTRY SPECIFIC STANDARDS THAT NEED TO BE ADHERED TO IN FULFILLING THE SOW.

1.1.	THE CONTRACTOR SHALL EMPLOY 4D-STEM TECHNIQUES TO CHARACTERIZE THE BUILT-IN POTENTIAL ACROSS INTERFACES IN METALLIC-SEMICONDUCTING SYSTEMS BASED ON METALS, BORIDES, CARBIDES AND OXIDES, WHERE SYSTEM SELECTION IS DRIVEN BY THEORETICAL DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN THE PROJECT.
1.2.	THE CONTRACTOR WILL USE PRECESSION BASED MONITORING OF MOMENTUM TRANSFER TO THE BEAM CENTER OF MASS AS THE BASIS FOR DETERMINING THE BUILT-IN POTENTIAL.
1.3.	THE EXPERIMENTS WILL SYSTEMATICALLY INVESTIGATE INTERFACES BETWEEN SYSTEMS SUCH AS GADOLINIUM ZIRCONATE AND HIGH ENTROPY CARBIDES AND/OR GADOLINIUM ALUMINATE AND HIGH ENTROPY CARBIDES, WHERE SYSTEMS OF INTEREST MAY VARY BASED ON INPUT FROM TEAM COLLABORATORS.
2.1.	VIBRATIONAL ELECTRON ENERGY LOSS SPECTROSCOPY WILL BE UTILIZED TO CHARACTERIZE THE SPATIAL DISTRIBUTION OF PHONON MODES AROUND THE SAME INTERFACES WITHIN THE SAME AND ADJACENT TO THE SAMPLE.
2.2.	THE EXPERIMENTS WILL SYSTEMATICALLY INVESTIGATE PHONON MODES AT AND AROUND INTERFACES BETWEEN SYSTEMS SUCH AS GADOLINIUM ZIRCONATE AND HIGH ENTROPY CARBIDES AND/OR GADOLINIUM ALUMINATE AND HIGH ENTROPY CARBIDES, WHERE SYSTEMS OF INTEREST MAY VARY BASED ON INPUT FROM TEAM COLLABORATORS.
3.1.	IN SITU HIGH TEMPERATURE NANOMECHANICAL TESTING WILL BE EMPLOYED TO CHARACTERIZE THE HIGH TEMPERATURE MECHANICAL RESPONSE OF INDIVIDUAL INTERFACES AND THE LATTICE IN SPECIFIC METAL, OXIDE, AND BORIDE SYSTEMS DESIGNATED BY THE LEAD

3.2.	THE BICRYSTAL DATA WILL BE INCORPORATED INTO MECHANISM INFORMED DEFORMATION MECHANISM MAPS
3.3.	THE COUPLING BETWEEN THERMAL AND ELASTIC RESPONSE WILL BE USED TO MEASURE THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY LOCALLY AS A FUNCTION OF TEMPERATURE IN SYSTEMS SUCH AS GADOLINIUM ZIRCONATE AND HIGH ENTROPY CARBIDE COMPOSITES AND/OR GADOLINIUM ALUMINATE AND HIGH ENTROPY CARBIDE COMPOSITES. AGAIN, THE SYSTEMS OF INTEREST COULD EVOLVE BASED ON DEVELOPMENTS WITHIN THE PROJECT.

subaward_description:
EFFORTS TO INCREASE REPRESENTATION OF UNDERREPRESENTED MINORITY (URM) STUDENTS IN ONR-ALIGNED
CAREERS ARE HINDERED BY THE SCARCITY OF STUDENTS OF COLOR IN EXISTING PIPELINES. TO OVERCOME THIS,
ADDITIONAL EFFORTS ARE NEEDED IN THREE AREAS: A) INCREASING AWARENESS OF CAREERS AND OPPORTUNITIES
IN THE OCEAN SCIENCES (OS) AMONG STUDENTS OF COLOR AND THEIR SUPPORT NETWORK (E.G. PARENTS,
GUIDANCE COUNSELORS, K-12 EDUCATORS), B) PROVIDING EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING AND INTERACTIVE TRAINING
OPPORTUNITIES THAT GENERATE AND MAINTAIN STUDENT INTEREST IN OS-RELATED CAREERS, AND C) ON CAMPUS,
ACADEMIC YEAR ACTIVITIES THAT MAINTAIN STUDENT INTERESTS IN, AND COMMITMENT TO, PURSUIT
OF OS-RELATED CAREERS IN THE FACE OF COMPETING DEMANDS FOR THEIR ATTENTION THROUGH ON-GOING
RESEARCH AND TRAINING OPPORTUNITIES. THE SPECIFIC ACTIVITIES THAT XULA, TU, AND JSU WILL ENGAGE
IN INCLUDE:
1) SUMMER EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING, HOSTED BY USM. AS DETAILED IN THE MAIN PROPOSAL, URM
UNDERGRADUATE STUDENTS WILL BE ENGAGED IN A TIERED, MULTI-YEAR SUMMER PROGRAM AT USM. THIS
WILL INCLUDE: A) A WEEK-LONG FIELD CAMP THAT IMMERSES STUDENTS IN OCEAN SCIENCE AND DIFFERENT
METHODS EMPLOYED IN THE FIELD, WHILE UTILIZING THE R/V JIM FRANKS TO EXPLORE THE COASTAL
ENVIRONMENTS OF MS INCLUDING THE MISSISSIPPI SOUND AND BARRIER ISLANDS; B) 5-WEEK SUMMER
FIELD PROGRAM COURSE SESSIONS IN OCEAN SCIENCE; C) 10-WEEK LAB, FIELD, AND/OR AT-SEA EXPERIENCE IN
WHICH STUDENTS COLLABORATE WITH MENTORS TO DEVELOP HYPOTHESIS-DRIVEN PROJECTS, EXECUTE THEIR
RESEARCH DESIGN TO COLLECT ENVIRONMENTAL DATA, AND ANALYZE DATA TO ADDRESS THEIR SPECIFIC RESEARCH
QUESTION; AND, D) SUMMER INTERNSHIP OPPORTUNITIES IN NAVY LABS AT NRL-STENNIS OR RELATED
ENTITIES.
4. XULA AND TU: DEVELOPMENT OF COLLABORATIVE 3+2 EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS BETWEEN XULA, TU,
AND USM. IN THESE PROGRAMS, STUDENTS WOULD COMPLETE THE MAJORITY OF THEIR B.S. REQUIREMENTS (3
YEARS) AT THEIR HOME MSI CAMPUS (XULA OR TU) AND THEN ROUND OUT THEIR B.S. AS WELL AS
COMPLETE AN M.S. WITH AN EMPHASIS IN MARINE SCIENCE AT USM. WE ANTICIPATE THE B.S. TO BE
AWARDED BY THE MSI AND M.S. BY USM. NOTE THAT BECAUSE IMPLEMENTING SUCH PROGRAMS WILL
REQUIRE HIGH LEVEL APPROVAL BY THE UNIVERSITIES (AND POTENTIALLY STATE BOARDS), WE CANNOT PROMISE
MORE THAN A DEVELOPMENT EFFORT AT THIS STAGE. OTHER COLLABORATIVE EDUCATIONAL OPPORTUNITIES, SUCH
AS USM PROVIDING MSI STUDENTS WITH MICRO-CREDIT OR BADGE PROGRAMS FOR SKILLS PERTINENT TO
OCEAN SCIENCE RESEARCH, WILL ALSO BE EXPLORED. ENROLLMENT IN THE USM UNCREWED MARITIME
SYSTEMS (UMS) CERTIFICATE PROGRAM IS ANOTHER OPTION. WE ANTICIPATE MOST PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT
TO TAKE PLACE IN YEARS 1-2, WITH APPROVAL IN YEARS 2-3, AND IMPLEMENTATION IN YEARS 4-5. XULA
AND TU HAVE BUDGETED FOR STUDENT RESEARCH ASSISTANTS WHICH COULD SERVE AS STUDENT SUPPORT TO
JUMP-START THE PROGRAMS.
5. RESEARCH MATCHMAKING BETWEEN POTENTIAL PIS AT THE MSIS WITH ESTABLISHED PIS AT R1
INSTITUTIONS AND NAVY SCIENCE ENTITIES SUCH AS NRL. BOTH XULA AND TU ALSO
REQUEST FUNDS FOR ACADEMIC YEAR STUDENT RESEARCH INTERNSHIPS WHICH WILL BOTH INCREASE ACADEMIC
YEAR STUDENT ENGAGEMENT AS WELL AS FACILITATE THE RESEARCH MATCH-MAKING PROCESS, WHICH CAN BE
DIFFICULT FOR JUNIOR FACULTY WITH SIGNIFICANT DEMANDS ON THEIR TIME SUCH AS EXTENSIVE TEACHING
LOADS.
6. SUPPORTING STUDENT RETAINMENT THROUGH REGULAR OS-RELATED ACADEMIC YEAR EVENTS. FOR TU AND JSU, THE OEC IS A STUDENT
ORGANIZATION, FUNDED IN PART BY THE NOAA OCEAN EXPLORATION COOPERATIVE INSTITUTE, THAT IS
FOCUSED ON INCREASING AWARENESS OF OS-RELATED CAREERS. THE CLUBS MEET REGULARLY FOR A RANGE OF
OS-RELATED EVENTS INCLUDING OKEANOS EXPLORER WATCH PARTIES, GUEST SPEAKERS, SHARK DISSECTIONS,
SEAGLIDER COMPETITIONS, WATER CHEMISTRY CHALLENGES, AND OTHERS. BASED ON THE EXPERIENCE OF TU
AND JSU, A SIMILAR CLUB WILL BE CREATED AT XULA.

subaward_description: OUR TEAM AT THE DAS LAB WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR CONDUCTING EXPERIMENTS WITH OUR ESTABLISHED LUNG ORGANOID AND PRIMARY CELL MODELS. IN THE PAST FOUR YEARS, MY LABORATORY HAS DEVELOPED SIGNIFICANT EXPERTISE IN HUMAN ORGANOID CULTURES. WE HAVE ESTABLISHED APPROXIMATELY 60 GASTRO-INTESTINAL AND 6 LUNG ORGANOID LINES FROM HUMAN TISSUES, BASED ON A PROTOCOL FROM MIYOSHI AND STAPPENBECK [1] AND CLEVERS LAB [2]. WE HAVE ALREADY PUBLISHED THE PROTOCOLS FOR THE ISOLATION OF STEM CELLS IN RECENT PAPERS [3-6]. THE PREVIOUSLY PUBLISHED PROTOCOL WAS MODIFIED TO ISOLATE LUNG ORGANOIDS FROM HUMAN SUBJECTS [7, 8]. WE HAVE SHOWN THAT THE LUNG ORGANOID MODEL HAS PROXIMAL AND DISTAL AIRWAY CELLS AND CAN BE USED FOR SARS-COV2 INFECTIONS [9]. MY LAB HAS A HIGHLY SKILLED TEAM OF EXPERTS IN SPECIFIC COMPONENTS OF THE OVERALL PROCEDURE. AS A CO-FOUNDING DIRECTOR OF HUMANOID, I HAVE ACCESS TO OTHER BIOBANKED DEIDENTIFIED LUNG ORGANOID LINES. I WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR CONSULTATION ON EXPERIMENTAL DESIGN AND TRAINING/EDUCATION ABOUT STEM-CELL-BASED DISEASE MODELING. WE HAVE THE ABILITY TO ISOLATE, EXPAND, AND BIOBANK 3D ORGANOIDS FROM HEALTHY AND DISEASED HUMAN TISSUES, ISOLATES, AND BIOBANKS PRIMARY CELLS AND DEVELOP MODELS TO STUDY ORGAN PHYSIOLOGY AND HUMAN DISEASE. THE LUNG MODEL WILL BE USED TO UNDERSTAND THE ROLE OF ERBBS IN SARS-COV-2 INFECTION AND PATHOGENESIS AND DEFINE THE THERAPEUTIC POTENTIAL OF PAN-ERBB INHIBITORS FOR COMBATING MULTIPLE CORONAVIRUSES. DR. DAS WILL HELP IN THE EXECUTION OF FUNCTIONAL ASSAYS WITH ORGANOID MODELS RELEVANT TO THIS PROPOSAL. THE STEM-CELL-BASED ORGANOID MODEL FOR SARS-COV2 INFECTION AND THE COCULTURE WITH LUNG FIBROBLASTS AND IMMUNE CELLS WILL BE BENEFICIAL FOR THIS PROPOSAL. UML ONLY PERFORMS UNCLASSIFIED FUNDAMENTAL RESEARCH, AND THEREFORE SUBMITS THIS PROPOSAL WITH THE UNDERSTANDING THAT IT IS UNCLASSIFIED, FUNDAMENTAL RESEARCH AS DEFINED BY U.S. EXPORT CONTROL REGULATIONS. IT IS UNDERSTOOD THAT ANY DEVELOPMENTAL ITEMS AND SPECIALLY DESIGNED PARTS, COMPONENTS, ACCESSORIES AND ATTACHMENTS FABRICATED UNDER ANY DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE AWARD RESULTING FROM THIS PROPOSAL ARE BEING DEVELOPED FOR BOTH CIVIL AND MILITARY APPLICATIONS.

subaward_description:
OSRS OUTGOING SUBAWARDS TEAM
SCOPE OF WORK GUIDE

ELEMENTS OF THE SCOPE OF WORK

AIMS OF PROJECT: PROFESSOR MICHAEL FRACHETTI, DEPARTMENT OF ANTHROPOLOGY, WASHINGTON UNIVERSITY IN ST. LOUIS, HAS RECEIVED A RESEARCH GRANT MODELLING SCALES OF SOCIETAL RESILIENCE TO CONCURRENT SHOCKS IN THE ASIA-PACIFIC REGION, THROUGH THE US DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE MINERVA INITIATIVE. THE MAIN GOAL OF THIS THREE-YEAR PROJECT IS TO CONDUCT FOCUSED RESEARCH ON THE UNDERLYING FACTORS THAT DETERMINE SOCIETAL RESILIENCE TO ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSES SUCH AS NATURAL DISASTERS AND CLIMATE CHANGE ACROSS ASIA. THIS WILL BE DONE THROUGH A COMBINATION OF (1) DEVELOPING NOVEL THEORETICAL FRAMEWORKS FOR CONCEPTUALIZING SOCIETAL RESILIENCE; (2) DEVELOPING SPATIAL STATISTICAL MODELS FOR QUANTITATIVE ANALYSIS OF SOCIETAL RESILIENCE; AND (3) DETAILED ANALYSIS OF A LARGE CORPUS OF DATA ABOUT SOCIETAL IMPACT AND RECOVERY FROM MAJOR HISTORICAL AND RECENT ENVIRONMENTAL SHOCKS ACROSS THE ASIA-PACIFIC REGION. AS CO-PI AND SUB-AWARD RECIPIENT, PROFESSOR ZUBROW WILL PLAY AN INSTRUMENTAL ROLE IN ALL ADDRESSING ALL THREE OF THESE INITIATIVES, AS DETAILED BELOW.

subaward_description:
PROF. STEVEN ANLAGE (UMD) AND HIS POST-DOC WILL DEVELOP THE HIGH-THROUGHPUT LASER SCANNING
MICROSCOPE (LSM) TO GENERATE THE DATASETS REQUIRED TO TRAIN THE MACHINE LEARNING (ML)
ALGORITHM. PROF. Y. C. LAI (ASU), PROF. L. WRIGHT (YALE) AND PROF. ANLAGE WILL WORK TOGETHER ON
THE ML INVERSE DESIGN ALGORITHM TO MAKE PREDICTIONS FOR TAILORED PERTURBATIONS THAT LEAD THE
SYSTEM TOWARD SYNCHRONIZATION. THESE ALGORITHMS WILL BE TESTED IN THE LAB AND THEIR EFFICACY WILL
BE MEASURED. THROUGH SUCH AN ITERATIVE PROCESS THE NATURAL FORMS OF DISORDER WILL BE UNCOVERED
AND QUANTIFIED, AND THEIR IMPACT ON SYNCHRONIZATION WILL BE STUDIED. THIS WILL RESULT IN A
HIERARCHICAL UNDERSTANDING OF THE MOST INFLUENTIAL FORMS OF DISORDER, AND IDENTIFICATION OF THE
MOST EXPEDIENT MEANS TO COUNTERACT THEM. PROF. ANLAGE WILL ALSO PARTICIPATE IN THE COLLECTIVE
EFFORT OF THE ENTIRE MURI TEAM TO DEVELOP A UNIVERSAL, GENERALIST ALGORITHM FOR SYNCHRONIZING
COMPLEX SYSTEMS ACROSS DIFFERENT PLATFORMS AND REALIZATIONS.

SPARK THERMIONICS CURRENTLY OPERATES A 11,000 SQFT FACILITY IN BERKELEY, CA. SINCE ITS FOUNDING, SPARK THERMIONICS HAS BUILT UP UNIQUE FACILITIES AND EXPERTISE THAT MAKE IT UNIQUELY SUITED TO EXECUTE ON THE PROPOSED EFFORT. SPARK IS USING ITS LAB SPACE FOR RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT WORK UNDER SEVERAL OTHER PROGRAMS, INCLUDING DEVELOPMENT AND DEMONSTRATION OF THERMIONIC CONVERTERS VIA PHASE II SBIRS UNDER DARPA, NASA, ARMY, AND USAF AND A PREVIOUS ARPA-E AWARD. BEYOND THESE SPECIALIZED FABRICATION AND TEST CAPABILITIES, SPARK OPERATES SEVERAL HIGH TEMPERATURE FURNACES HEAT TREATMENTS FOR DEVICE DEVELOPMENT, DEMONSTRATION, AND SCALING. THE LAB AND MANUFACTURING SPACE ALSO CONTAINS FINISHING TOOLS LIKE POLISHERS, AS WELL AS A HE LEAK CHECKER AND PROFILOMETER FOR EXAMINING BULK MATERIAL PROPERTIES LIKE SURFACE FINISH AND HERMETICITY FOR SCALING UP OF THERMIONIC ENERGY CONVERTERS. SPARKÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S LAB SPACE ALSO CONTAINS GENERAL PURPOSE TOOLS SUCH AS A GLOVE BOX AND A SMALL MACHINE SHOP, ALLOWING US TO MANUFACTURE COMPONENTS WITH SHORT LEAD TIMES.

IMPORTANCE OF THE WNT SIGNALING PATHWAY IN PEDIATRIC GERM CELL TUMORS TO ADULT TESTICULAR CANCERS. subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING,VETERINARY COLLEGE,SCHOOL OF FORESTRY
subaward_description:
THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA WILL PERFORM THE FOLLOWING WORK ON THE DARPA ANSR PROJECT.
1. PROF. EWETZ WAS THE ORIGINAL PI OF THE DARPA ANSR PROJECT AT THE UNIVERSITY OF CENTRAL
FLORIDA. HIS EXPERTISE IS REQUIRED TO COORDINATE THE DARPA ANSR PROJECT AT THE UNIVERSITY
OF FLORIDA (HIS NEW INSTITUTION) AND THE UNIVERSITY OF CENTRAL FLORIDA (IN COLLABORATION WITH
THE NEW PI PROF. HAO ZHENG). PROF. EWETZ AND PROF. ZHENG WILL MEET ON A WEEKLY BASIS TO
ENSURE THAT TIMELY PROGRESS IS MADE ACCORDING TO THE PROJECT PLAN.
2. TASK 1 (PHASE 1): CREATE JOINT LATENT ENCODINGS OF SYMBOLIC AND NEURAL REPRESENTATIONS.
WE WILL DEVELOP ALGORITHMS TO ALIGN UNIVERSAL SCENE DESCRIPTION LANGUAGE AND IMAGES
USING CONTRASTIVE LEARNING. THE OBJECTIVE IS TO ALLOW IMAGES TO SEAMLESSLY BE CONVERTED
INTO TEXT AND THEN BACK INTO IMAGES AGAIN.
3. TASK 3 (PHASE 1): DECISION MAKING FROM TEMPORAL LOGIC SPECIFICATIONS USING GRAPH
NEURAL NETWORKS AND ALTERNATING AUTOMATA. THIS WILL INCLUDE DEVELOPING ALGORITHMS FOR
NAVIGATION BASED ON NATURAL LANGUAGE DESCRIPTIONS. WE WILL USE AN LLM TO CREATE CONTROL
ALGORITHMS AND GRAPH NEURAL NETWORKS USING THE LLMS INTRINSIC CODE GENERATION ABILITIES.
4. TASK 4 (PHASE 1): DEMO AND EXPERIMENTAL SUPPORT: THE UF TEAM WILL DEVELOP THE HYBRID
AI SYSTEM SOLUTION DUE TO DARPA IN OCTOBER 2024. THE SYSTEM WILL CONSIST OF AN
INTEGRATED MANEUVER AND PERCEPTION COMPONENT.
5. TASK 1 (PHASE 2): SCALABLE REASONING BY COMPUTING CONFIDENCE USING BAYESIAN HYPOTHESIS
TESTING WITH SPATIO-TEMPORAL PRIORS: WE WILL INVESTIGATE BAYESIAN HYPOTHESIS TESTING TO
COMPUTE THE CONFIDENCE OF A PREDICTION FROM OUR NEURO-SYMBOLIC APPROACH.
6. TASK 3 (PHASE 2): LEARNING ALTERNATING AUTOMATA AS SYMBOLIC REPRESENTATIONS OF
ENVIRONMENT AND MODEL CHECKING AGAINST TEMPORAL LOGIC SPECIFICATIONS: THIS WILL INCLUDE
CONVERTING NATURAL LANGUAGE SPECIFICATION INTO LINEAR TIME LOGIC (LTL) TO VERIFY THE PLAN
GENERATED BY THE LLM. WE WILL DESCRIBE THE BEHAVIOR OF AUTONOMOUS AGENTS USING
ALTERNATING AUTOMATA, TO ENABLE SWIFT VERIFICATION FOR HIGH ASSURANCE.
7. TASK 4 (PHASE 2): DEMO AND EXPERIMENTAL SUPPORT: THE UF TEAM WILL DEVELOP THE HYBRID
AI SYSTEM SOLUTION DUE TO DARPA IN PHASE 2 (DUE DATES TO BE DETERMINED). THE SYSTEM WILL CONSIST OF AN INTEGRATED MANEUVER AND PERCEPTION COMPONENT THAT WILL MEET REAL-
TIME SIZE, WEIGHT, AREA, AND POWER CONSTRAINTS.
DELIVERABLES
1. 2. 3. 4. 5. 6. EVALUATION 2: THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA WILL SUBMIT TO EVALUATION 3 IN OCTOBER 2024.
REPORT: THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA WILL PROVIDE THE UNIVERSITY OF CENTRAL FLORIDA WITH A
PHASE ONE PROJECT REPORT ON 12/30/2024.
EVALUATION 3: THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA WILL SUBMIT TO EVALUATION 4 DURING SPRING 2025.
EVALUATION 4: THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA WILL SUBMIT TO EVALUATION 5 DURING FALL 2025.
EVALUATION 5: THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA WILL SUBMIT TO EVALUATION 6 DURING SPRING 2026.
REPORT: THE UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA WILL PROVIDE THE UNIVERSITY OF CENTRAL FLORIDA WITH A FINAL
PROJECT REPORT ON 6/30/2026.

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
THE GOAL OF THE PROPOSED MURI IS TO UNDERSTAND BIOHYBRID ACTUATOR INTERFACES, LIFE-SUPPORT, DESIGN, AND LOCAL CONTROL TO MOVE BIOHYBRID ACTUATORS BEYOND THE PROOF-OF-CONCEPT PETRI DISH SCALE. WE AIM TO UNDERSTAND THE EFFECTS OF 3D SYNTHETIC SCAFFOLDS, LIFE-SUPPORT SYSTEMS, ELECTRO-, AND PHOTO- NEUROMUSCULAR INTERFACES, AND LOCAL FEEDBACK CONTROL SYSTEMS ON BIOHYBRID ACTUATOR PERFORMANCE, ADAPTABILITY, AND LONGEVITY. FURTHERMORE, WE WILL VALIDATE OUR UNDERSTANDING BY INTEGRATING GENERAL PRINCIPLES FROM OUR
BASIC RESEARCH IN BIOHYBRID ACTUATOR FABRICATION, MATURATION, INTERFACES, AND LIFE SUPPORT, WITH COMPUTATIONAL TOOLS DESIGN. USING THESE TOOLS, WE WILL TEST OUR UNDERSTANDING BY PREDICTING AND VALIDATING THE PERFORMANCE OF BIOHYBRID ACTUATORS WITH ENHANCED SCALE, LONGEVITY, AND PERFORMANCE CAPABILITIES.

subaward_description:
THIS STATEMENT OF WORK ADDRESSES THE OBJECTIVES AND TASKS THAT WILL INCLUDE DR. BUSHNIK, DR.
IOSIFESCU AND DR. KIM, AS CO-PIS, WITH RESPECT TO STUDY CONDUCT AND COLLABORATION BETWEEN THE 2
SITES. DR. BUSHNIK AND DR. IOSIFESCU HAVE BEEN INVOLVED IN THE DESIGN OF THE RESEARCH DESIGN WITH
RESPECT TO THE SUBJECT POPULATION, ADMINISTRATION OF THE INTERVENTION, AND THE APPROPRIATE
MEASUREMENTS OF COGNITIVE FUNCTIONING. DR. BUSHNIK WILL IDENTIFY A RESEARCH ASSISTANT (RA) WHO
WILL SCREEN AND CONTACT POTENTIAL STUDY PARTICIPANTS. THE RA WILL CONSENT THE PARTICIPANTS,
SCHEDULE THE IMAGING SESSIONS, AND PERFORM NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL TESTING AT THE APPROPRIATE TIME
POINTS UNDER THE SUPERVISION OF DR.RATH. FROM THE DEPT. OF PSYCHIATRY, DR. IOSIFESCU, DR.
NAOMI GAGGI (POST-DOCTORAL FELLOW) AND AN RA, WILL CONDUCT THE STUDY INTERVENTION
(PHOTOBIOMODULATION WITH NEAR INFRARED LASER OR SHAM, ADMINISTERED THREE TIMES/WEEK FOR 6
WEEKS), AND THE NECESSARY MONITORING OF SYMPTOMS AND SIDE EFFECTS. DR. TARPEY AND A
POST-DOCTORAL FELLOW IN POPULATION HEALTH WILL PERFORM THE REQUIRED STATISTICAL ANALYSES. ALL NYU
GROSSMAN SCHOOL OF MEDICINE KEY PERSONNEL WILL BE INVOLVED IN THE ANALYSIS AND INTERPRETATION
OF THE DATA, RESULTING IN DISSEMINATION OF THE RESULTS THROUGH MANUSCRIPTS, CONFERENCE
PROCEEDINGS, AND SERVING AS THE BASIS FOR FUTURE RESEARCH

subaward_description: GORDON PENNYCOOK (LEAD PI) AND DAVID RAND (CO-PI) WILL LEAD THE BEHAVIORAL RESEARCH ARM OF THE PROPOSAL. THIS WILL INVOLVE DEVELOPING STIMULUS MATERIALS AND DESIGNING EXPERIMENTS TO INVESTIGATE CAUSAL EFFECTS OF PARASOCIAL INFLUENCE. HE WILL ALSO OVERSEE DATA ANALYSIS, WRITE JOURNAL ARTICLES, AND COMMUNICATE RESEARCH FINDINGS RELATING TO THE PARASOCIAL INFLUENCE EXPERIMENTS. FURTHERMORE, DR. SCOPE OF WORK:
1. DEVELOPMENT OF MULTIPHYSICS MODELS:
-ALTASIM WILL SUPPORT USM'S OCEAN ENTERPRISE BY DEVELOPING COMPREHENSIVE MODELS IN COMSOL THAT SIMULATE
GRAVITY MODELS-MODELING OF SEAFLOOR OBJECTS TO SIMULATE GRAVITATIONAL INTERACTIONS WITH VARYING SEAFLOOR TYPES AND OBJECTS, INCLUDING CABLES
ACOUSTICS MODELS - SIMULATION OF SOUND PROPAGATION, REFLECTION, AND SCATTERING FROM METALLIC SEAFLOOR OBJECTS, CABLES, AND VESSELS OF INTEREST, PARTICULARLY UNDER TRANSIENT CONDITIONS.
THERMAL MODELS - ANALYSIS OF HEAT TRANSFER AND THERMAL CONDUCTIVITY OF SEAFLOOR CABLES AND OBJECTS, CONSIDERING OCEANIC ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS.
ELECTROMAGNETIC MODELS - MODELING ELECTROMAGNETIC BEHAVIOR OF METALLIC SEAFLOOR OBJECTS AND CABLES, INCLUDING SIGNAL ATTENUATION AND INTERFERENCE. 
2. TRANSIENT VESSEL INTERACTION MODELS:
-DEVELOP AND REFINE MODELS FOR VESSELS OF INTEREST THAT SIMULATE DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR, INCLUDING THE IMPACT ON LOCAL ACOUSTIC, GRAVITATION, THERMAL, AND ELECTROMAGNETIC FIELDS AS THE VESSELS MOVE IN PROXIMITY TO SEAFLOOR OBJECTS.
3. EXECUTABLE APPLICATION DEVELOPMENT:
-ALTASIM WILL WORK WITH USM TO CREATE EXECUTABLE APPLICATIONS FROM THE COMSOL MODELS. THESE APPLICATIONS WILL BE:
NAVAL PARTNER APPLICATIONS: DESIGNED FOR EXTERNAL USE BY NAVAL PARTNERS TO SIMULATE AND ANALYZE SEAFLOOR INTERACTIONS, VESSEL TRACKING, AND DETECT ANOMALIES IN METALLIC SEAFLOOR OBJECTS AND CABLES.
INTERNAL USM APPLICATIONS: TOOLS FOR INTERNAL US TO AID DATA SCIENTISTS IN GENERATING LARGE VOLUMES OF LABELED DATA FROM SIMULATIONS. THIS DATA WILL BE CRITICAL FOR TRAINING MACHINE LEARNING ALGORITHMS USED FOR PATTERN RECOGNITION, ANOMALY DETECTION, AND PREDICTIVE ANALYTICS.
4. MACHINE LEARNING INTEGRATION:
-ALTASIM WILL ASSIST IN PREPARING THE MODELING OUTPUTS IN FORMATS SUITABLE FOR MACHINE LEARNING PIPELINES, ENSURING THAT DATA IS WELL-STRUCTURED FOR TRAINING MODELS AIMED AT DETECTING AND CLASSIFYING OBJECTS AND PHENOMENA IN MARINE ENVIRONMENTS.

subawardee_business_types: NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION,FOUNDATION
subaward_description:
THE JACK MILLER CENTER IS A 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO REINVIGORATING EDUCATION IN AMERICAÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S FOUNDING PRINCIPLES AND HISTORY, AN EDUCATION VITAL TO THOUGHTFUL AND ENGAGED CITIZENSHIP. THE CENTER IS MADE UP OF A NATIONWIDE NETWORK OF SCHOLARS AND TEACHERS WHO ARE DEDICATED TO ADVANCING THE CORE TEXTS AND IDEAS OF THE AMERICAN POLITICAL TRADITION. THE CENTER HAS THREE STRATEGIES:
1. BUILD AND SUSTAIN A COMMUNITY OF SCHOLARS IN THE FIELDS OF AMERICAN POLITICAL THOUGHT AND HISTORY.
2. RESTORE THE TEACHING OF AMERICAN CITIZENSHIP IN K-12 SCHOOLS THAT CENTERS ON THE COUNTRYÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S HISTORY, THE  DECLARATION OF INDEPENDENCE, THE U.S. CONSTITUTION, AND OTHER FOUNDATIONAL TEXTS IN THE AMERICAN TRADITION.
3. PARTNER WITH ORGANIZATIONS AND PHILANTHROPISTS TO ADVANCE CIVIC EDUCATION THAT IS GROUNDED IN AMERICAÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S FOUNDING PRINCIPLES AND HISTORY. THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL PARTNER WITH THE EDMOND & LILY SAFRA CENTER FOR ETHICS AND ADDITIONAL PROJECT PARTNERS ON THE PROPOSAL PROJECT TO DESIGN AND IMPLEMENT HIGH QUALITY CIVICS EDUCATION PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT ALIGNED WITH THE EDUCATING FOR AMERICAN DEMOCRACY ROADMAP (EAD).
OVER THE THREE YEARS OF THE PROJECT, THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL TAKE THE LEAD ON THE CORE TEXTS IN THE AMERICAN POLITICAL TRADITION WORKSHOP SERIES OF THE PROPOSED PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM.
THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL TAKE THE LEAD ON DESIGNING THE PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT OPPORTUNITIES FOCUSED ON THIS COMPONENT BASED ON THEIR EXPERTISE AND EXISTING RESOURCES IN THIS AREA AND PROVIDING COURSE FACILITATION FOR THIS COMPONENT.
IN YEAR 1 (SEPT 1, 2023-AUG 31, 2024), THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL PARTICIPATE IN PROJECT LEADERSHIP MEETINGS (QUARTERLY) AND MONTHLY PROJECT TEAM MEETINGS. THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL PROVIDE INPUT ON THE PROGRAM ALIGNMENT PROCESS TO ENSURE COHESION ACROSS THE COURSE OFFERINGS AND ALIGNMENT WITH THE EAD FRAMEWORK. THE JACK MILLER CENTER TEAM WILL COMPLETE INITIAL DESIGN ACTIVITIES TO ADAPT EXISTING RESOURCES FOR THE PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT COURSE, PLANNED TO BE A MIX OF SYNCHRONOUS AND ASYNCHRONOUS ACTIVITIES VIA A 6-WEEK VIRTUAL COURSE ANCHORED BY TWO 2-HOUR VIRTUAL WORKSHOPS WITH FACILITATION.
IN YEAR 2 (SEPT 1, 2024-AUG 31, 2025), THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL PARTICIPATE IN PROJECT LEADERSHIP MEETINGS (QUARTERLY) AND MONTHLY PROJECT TEAM MEETINGS. THE JACK MILLER CENTER TEAM WILL COMPLETE COURSE DESIGN ACTIVITIES AND FACILITATE ONE OFFERING OF THEIR COURSE. COURSE EVALUATION ACTIVITIES WILL BE SHARED WITH THE JACK MILLER CENTER TEAM AND USED FOR PROGRAM IMPROVEMENT AND ANY COURSE REDESIGN AS RELEVANT. THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL COMPLETE TWO CAPACITY BUILDING WORKSHOPS TO HELP PARTICIPANTS TO TRAIN OTHER TEACHERS IN THEIR LEARNING.
IN YEAR 3 (SEPT 1, 2025-AUG 31, 2026), THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL PARTICIPATE IN PROJECT LEADERSHIP MEETINGS (QUARTERLY) AND MONTHLY PROJECT TEAM MEETINGS. THE JACK MILLER CENTER TEAM WILL COMPLETE ANY COURSE REDESIGN ACTIVITIES STILL TO BE COMPLETED AND FACILITATE ONE OFFERING OF THEIR COURSE. THE JACK MILLER CENTER WILL COMPLETE TWO CAPACITY BUILDING WORKSHOPS TO HELP PARTICIPANTS TO TRAIN OTHER TEACHERS IN THEIR LEARNING.

subaward_description:
YEAR 1: INCREASE THE PERFORMANCE OF OUR COILED CNT MUSCLES BY OPTIMIZING TWIST LOADS AND AMOUNTS, COIL GEOMETRY, AND INCORPORATING HIGH CAPACITANCE YARN GUESTS. EVALUATE THE RELATIVE PERFORMANCE OF SINGLE-ACTUATING ELECTRODE AND DUAL-ACTUATING ELECTRODE MUSCLES. DEVELOP THEORETICAL UNDERSTANDING OF ALL PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION PROCESSES AND USE THIS UNDERSTANDING TO PROVIDE FURTHER PERFORMANCE ADVANCES.
YEAR 2: OPTIMIZE THE PERFORMANCE OF OUR COILED SHEATH-FREE AND SRAM MUSCLES BY APPLYING OUR TOP PROCESS. BY THEORETICAL CALCULATIONS AND EXPERIMENTS, PROVIDE THE FIRST DEMONSTRATION THAT MUSCLE PERFORMANCE CAN BE OPTIMIZED BY ENGINEERING THE RADIAL DEPENDENCE OF YARN BIAS ANGLE.
YEAR 3: INCREASE THE ENERGY CONVERSION EFFICIENCY OF OUR ELECTROCHEMICAL ARTIFICIAL MUSCLES FROM OUR PRESENTLY PUBLISHED CONTRACTILE EFFICIENCY OF 6.1% TO A CONTRACTILE EFFICIENCY OF OVER 55% AND A FULL-CYCLE EFFICIENCY OF 25%.
YEARS 4: DEMONSTRATE THAT THE OPTIMIZED MUSCLES CAN DELIVER THE NEEDED CYCLE LIFE FOR TARGETED APPLICATIONS. EVALUATE IF ALL PERFORMANCE ASPECTS (WORK CAPACITY, POWER GENERATION, EFFICIENCY, AND CYCLE LIFE) CAN BE INCREASED BY COMBINING FARADAIC CHARGE INJECTION WITH DOUBLE-LAYER CHARGE INJECTION.
TWISTRON HARVESTERS AND SELF-POWERED STRAIN SENSORS
YEARS 1-2: ESTABLISH WAYS TO TRANSFORM POWERFUL INORGANIC OR ORGANIC ELECTROLYTE UNIPOLAR MUSCLES INTO POWERFUL ENERGY HARVESTERS, SUCH AS BY USING AN EXTERNAL BIAS VOLTAGE TO INJECT INITIAL CHARGE, LIKE DONE FOR DIELECTRIC ELASTOMER HARVESTERS, BUT USING A THOUSAND TIMES LOWER VOLTAGES.
YEARS 3-4: BY REFINING THE STRUCTURAL PARAMETERS OF CANDIDATE HARVESTERS, THEIR STARTING AND FINAL DEFORMATIONS, THE USED ELECTROLYTE, AND THE POSSIBLE USE OF CONDUCTING BISCROLLED GUESTS, INCREASE THE ENERGY CONVERSION EFFICIENCY OF OUR TENSILE TWISTRON HARVESTERS FROM OUR RECENTLY PUBLISHED 16% TO OVER 30%. WHILE WE HAVE ALREADY DEMONSTRATED THE ABILITY TO HARVEST ENERGY OVER WIDE TEMPERATURE RANGES FOR OUR PREVIOUS HARVESTERS, WE WILL DEMONSTRATE THAT THIS CAPABILITY IS MAINTAIN FOR THE IMPROVED HARVESTERS AND THAT THEY CAN DELIVER THE NEEDED CYCLE LIFE TIMES.
DUAL USE OF TWIST-COILED YARNS OR TWISTED AND PLIED YARNS AS MUSCLES AND HARVESTERS
YEAR 1: IMPROVE THE HARVESTING PERFORMANCE OF OUR ORGANIC ELECTROLYTE MUSCLES BY APPLYING A LOW EXTERNAL BIAS VOLTAGE TO INJECT INITIAL CHARGE BEFORE HARVESTING.
UPSCALED YARN MUSCLES AND HARVESTERS
YEARS 2-4: DEMONSTRATE HIGH PERFORMANCE ACTUATION AND ENERGY HARVESTING FOR YARNS THAT CAN BE MUCH MORE CHEAPLY PRODUCED THAN PRESENTLY POSSIBLE FOR CNT YARNS. THE INVESTIGATED STRATEGIES FOR PRODUCING THESE COILED OR PLIED YARNS WILL INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING: (1) CARBONIZING ELECTROSPUN ORGANIC POLYMERS, (2) INCORPORATING HIGH GRAVIMETRIC CAPACITANCE GUESTS INTO INEXPENSIVE NANOFIBER YARNS, (3) STRUCTURAL OPTIMIZATION OF INEXPENSIVELY PRODUCED GRAPHENE YARNS, AND (4) INCORPORATING HIGHLY ELASTIC, ELECTRONICALLY CONDUCTING, GIANT-SURFACE-AREA, HIGH-CAPACITANCE FOAMS INTO THE VOLUME OF INEXPENSIVELY PRODUCED YARNS.

THE BRYNGELSON GROUP AT GEORGIA TECH WILL THUS DEVELOP AND PROVIDE THE COMPUTATIONAL STRATEGIES AND METHODOLOGIES FOR INCORPORATING DATA ASSIMILATION INTO THE EXPERIMENTAL OBSERVATION PIPELINE, WHICH IS THE SECOND AIM OF THE PROJECT. HE ALSO HAS EXPERIENCE USING SPARSE IDENTIFICATION OF NONLINEAR DYNAMICS, WHICH IS A CRITICAL TOOL IN AIM 3, THE IDENTIFICATION OF THE UNDERPINNING MECHANISMS LINKING CYCLIC FATIGUE TO HIGH-RATE MATERIAL BEHAVIOR. TOGETHER, THE TWO PIS PLAN TO INTEGRATE THE EXPERIMENTAL AND COMPUTATIONAL METHODS INTO AN ITERATIVE STRATEGY TO OPTIMALLY IDENTIFY SOFT MATERIAL BEHAVIOR.

subawardee_business_types: OTHER NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description: WHILE CONSIDERABLE ATTENTION HAS BEEN GIVEN TO THE ASSESSMENT OF FAST BOAT MUSCULOSKELETAL INJURY, AN EMERGING AREA IN NEED OF FUTURE INVESTIGATION IS THE EFFECTS OF REPETITIVE LOADING ON NEURAL IMPAIRMENT AND COGNITIVE FUNCTION WITHIN FAST BOAT OPERATORS AND CREW. REPEATED EXPOSURE TO WHOLE-BODY VIBRATIONS AND EXCESSIVE G-FORCES BY MILITARY PERSONNEL HAS RESULTED IN DETRIMENTAL SHORT AND LONG-TERM NEUROLOGICAL AND MUSCULOSKELETAL EFFECTS OF REPETITIVE LOADING.  REPETITIVE LOADING EVEN AT RELATIVELY LOW LOADS HAS THE POTENTIAL TO CAUSE SIGNIFICANT NEUROLOGICAL DYSFUNCTION. IN MILITARY OPERATORS REGULARLY EXPOSED TO THESE EXCESSIVE G-FORCES, THERE IS AN INCREASE IN THE SELF-REPORTING OF SYMPTOMS SUCH AS HEADACHES, POOR CONCENTRATION, NECK PAIN, LOWER LEG, AND BACK INJURY, AND FACTORS RELATED TO MTBI, AND EVIDENCE FOR IMPAIRED REACTION TIME, PHYSICAL AND NEUROCOGNITIVE PERFORMANCE [4]. IDEALLY, WORK IN THIS AREA WOULD BE TO DIRECTLY  MEASURE NEURAL DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH FAST BOAT EXPOSURE. HOWEVER, THERE IS NO SINGLE TECHNOLOGY OR MEASURE TO ACCOMPLISH THIS GOAL. CLINICAL TESTING PROVIDES SOME INSIGHT BUT DOES NOT FULLY ENCOMPASS THE RANGE OF POSSIBLE IMPAIRMENT, NOR DOES IT INDICATE THE NATURE OF THE DAMAGE TO NEURAL TISSUE. OUR APPROACH IS TO INTEGRATE A VARIETY OF COMPLEMENTARY METHODOLOGIES THAT ALLOW FOR CROSS-INDIRECT VALIDATIONS AND ADDRESS THE GAPS/LIMITATIONS OF INDIVIDUAL  METHODOLOGIES. SPECIFICALLY, WE PROPOSE TO MEASURE INDIVIDUAL EXPOSURE TO FAST BOAT USING PERSONNEL APPLIED ACCELEROMETERS DURING OPERATIONS, ASSESS POSITION AND ROLE WITHIN THE BOAT AND TO ASSESS SENSORIMOTOR FUNCTION OF PERSONNEL THROUGH BRAIN IMAGING, BALANCE CONTROL, AND EYE TRACKING PRE AND POST RIDE.

subaward_description:
"METASURFACES ARE TWOÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½DIMENSIONAL PATTERNED STRUCTURES THAT TYPICALLY CONSIST OF PERIODIC METALLIC
SHAPES ON A THIN DIELECTRIC SUBSTRATE. THEY MAY CONTAIN A GROUND PLANE, OR THEY MAY ALSO BE ENTIRELY
DIELECTRIC, SUCH AS PATTERNS OF HOLES OR RODS WITH VARYING PERMITTIVITY. METASURFACES HAVE FOUND MANY
USES IN ELECTROMAGNETICS, INCLUDING CONTROL OF SURFACE WAVES AND COUPLING BETWEEN ANTENNAS, AS WELL AS
MANIPULATION OF SCATTERING. THEY HAVE ADVANTAGES OVER CONVENTIONAL 3D METAMATERIALS IN THAT THEY ARE
PLANAR AND ELECTRICALLY THIN, AND THUS EASIER TO FABRICATE AND APPLY TO VEHICLES OR STRUCTURES AS A THIN
COATING OR SKIN.
THE EARLIEST METASURFACES WERE FREQUENCY SELECTIVE SURFACES [1] THAT ARE USED IN MANY AIRCRAFT FOR
CONTROLLING THE RADAR CROSS SECTION. THE HIGHÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½IMPEDANCE SURFACE, INVENTED BY ONE OF THE COÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½PIS,
EMERGED AS A WAY TO BLOCK SURFACE CURRENTS AND ENABLE CERTAIN KINDS OF ANTENNAS TO BE PLACED DIRECTLY
ADJACENT TO METAL SURFACES. [2] A VARIETY OF STRUCTURES HAVE BEEN DEVELOPED SUCH AS HOLOGRAPHIC
SURFACES [3] AND ANISOTROPIC PATTERNING METHODS FOR GUIDING WAVES AROUND COMPLEX SHAPES. [4]
INCORPORATING DIODES ALLOWS METASURFACES TO BE BUILT AS NONLINEAR [5] OR WAVEFORMÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½DEPENDENT [6]
ABSORBERS OR SELFÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½FOCUSING STRUCTURES ANALOGOUS TO NONLINEAR OPTICAL CRYSTALS. [7] THE MOST RECENT AREA OF GROWTH HAS BEEN IN CHIRAL AND TOPOLOGICAL METASURFACES. THE RECENTLY
DEVELOPED ÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã…Â€ŒLINE WAVEÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½ CONCEPT DEMONSTRATES THAT GUIDED WAVES EXIST AT THE INTERFACE BETWEEN
COMPLEMENTARY METASURFACES, AND THEY ARE RESISTANT TO BACKSCATTERING. [8] THEY ARE CLOSELY RELATED TO
PHOTONIC TOPOLOGICAL INSULATORS. BOTH SPIN AND VALLEY TYPE PTIS HAVE BEEN CREATED WITH SIMPLE PATTERNED
METALLIC SHEETS [9] AND SIMILAR STRUCTURES HAVE BEEN CREATED IN PATTERNED DIELECTRIC SLABS. THESE
STRUCTURES HAVE BEEN SHOWN TO ENABLE WAVEGUIDES THAT ARE RESISTANT TO SCATTERING BY DEFECTS OR
AMORPHOUS DEFORMATION. WE HAVE RECENTLY DEVELOPED NEW AND PRACTICAL MICROWAVE TOPOLOGICAL INSULATORS, SUCH AS VALLEY TYPE
STRUCTURES THAT CAN MAINTAIN THEIR PROPERTIES WHEN INCORPORATED ON A GROUND PLANE, AND ALSO DIRECT
TRANSITIONS BETWEEN STANDARD MICROSTRIP WAVEGUIDES AND TOPOLOGICAL WAVEGUIDES. WE HAVE ALSO BUILT
RESONANT CAVITIES IN THE FORM OF HIGHER ORDER TOPOLOGICAL INSULATORS, AS WELL AS DEFECT CAVITIES AND
WAVEGUIDES BY EXPLOITING THE LOCAL SYMMETRY OF THE UNIT CELLS. WE HAVE ALSO BUILT DIELECTRIC VERSIONS
BASED ON BOTH SPIN AND VALLEY TYPE STRUCTURES, AS SHOWN IN FIGURE 2. WE HAVE ALSO CREATED UNIDIRECTIONAL
WAVEGUIDES IN PLANAR STRUCTURES BASED ON ZAK PHASE, AND ON ANTIÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¯Â¿Â½PHASE BOUNDARIES IN PHOTONIC CRYSTALS."

subaward_description:
THE GOAL OF THE PROPOSED PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP FUNDAMENTAL UNDERSTANDING FOR A NEW TYPE OF MULTIFUNCTIONAL NEUROMORPHIC INTERFACEÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â‚¬Ï¿½A HYBRID BIOMOLECULAR SYNAPSEÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â‚¬Ï¿½CAPABLE OF SELECTIVE IONIC AND BIOCHEMICAL SENSING, SIGNAL PROCESSING, LEARNING, AND MEMORY IN WET, CELLULAR ENVIRONMENTS. TO TEST THESE HYPOTHESES AND THEREBY ADVANCE HYBRID BIOMOLECULAR SYNAPSES FOR USE AT THE EDGE OF BIOLOGY, WE WILL USE STATE-OF-THE-ART NANO- AND MICRO-FABRICATION, CHEMISTRY, SYNTHETIC BIOLOGY, AND DEVICE CHARACTERIZATION AND MODELING TO:
1. ENGINEER THE BIOMEMBRANE-POLYMER INTERFACE FOR EFFECTIVE TRANSDUCTION AND MEMBRANE
STABILITY

CIRCUITS FOR SENSING, SIGNAL PROCESSING, LEARNING, AND MEMORY
5. CHARACTERIZE HYBRID BIOMOLECULAR SYNAPSES IN SIMULATED CELLULAR ENVIRONMENTS.

prime_award_cfda_numbers_and_titles: 12.800: AIR FORCE DEFENSE RESEARCH SCIENCES PROGRAM
subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description: PERIOD 1: DURING THE FIRST 6 MONTH PERIOD, WE WILL FOCUS ON SEVERAL ASPECTS OF TASKS 1 AND 2 WHICH HAVE COMPONENTS THAT WILL BE CARRIED OUT IN NUMERICAL EXPERIMENTS. THROUGH THESE TASKS WE WILL LAY THE FOUNDATION FOR THE CONTINUING EFFORT OVER THE FOLLOWING THREE YEARS. THESE TASKS ARE BUILT HEAVILY ON OUR PREVIOUS WORK. BUILDING ON PREVIOUS WORK WITH LES SIMULATIONS USING CHARLES AND APPLICATION OF THE RESOLVENT ANALYSIS WE PERFORM HIGH-FIDELITY COMPUTATIONS TO OBTAIN THE BASE FLOW ABOUT WHICH TO CONDUCT RESOLVENT ANALYSIS. PREPARATION FOR PERFORMING RESOLVENT ANALYSIS (SUCH AS OPERATOR GENERATION) WILL BE UNDERTAKEN DURING THIS PERIOD. PERIOD 2: DURING THE SECOND PERIOD (12 MONTHS), WE WILL FOCUS ON MULTIPLE TASKS IN NUMERICAL ASPECTS OF THE PROGRAM THAT WILL BUILD UPON THE TASKS STARTED DURING THE INITIAL PERIOD. RESOLVENT ANALYSIS OF HIGH-SPEED CAVITY FLOW WILL BE PERFORMED AND ITS RESULTS WILL BE CAREFULLY ANALYZED TO DEVELOP EFFECTIVE FLOW CONTROL SETUPS. MOREOVER, CLUSTER-BASED ANALYSIS WILL BE UNDERTAKEN TO UNDERSTAND THE PHASE-DYNAMICS OF THE FLUCTUATIONS OVER THE CAVITY. FOCUS HERE WILL BE PLACED ON DEVELOPING CONTROL STRATEGIES THAT TAKE UNSTEADINESS (PHASE) INTO CONSIDERATION. SOME FLOW CONTROL COMPUTATIONS WILL BE INITIATED. PERIOD 3: THE EFFORTS IN THE THIRD PERIOD (12 MONTHS) WILL CONSIDER THE APPLICATIONS OF THE SENSOR AND CLUSTER-BASED ANALYSIS TO FURTHER ENHANCE OUR UNDERSTANDING. THE COMPUTATIONAL GROUP WILL BE WORKING CLOSELY WITH THE EXPERIMENTAL GROUPS TO DEVELOP AND TEST THE CONTROL STRATEGIES FOR UNSTEADINESS SUPPRESSION. A PARAMETRIC STUDY FOR RESOLVENT ANALYSIS AND LES WILL BE CONDUCTED. CLUSTER-BASED ANALYSIS WILL BE PERFORMED AND IMPROVED ALGORITHMS WILL BE DEVELOPED WITH CAPABILITIES FOR REAL TIME IMPLEMENTATION. PERIOD 4: THE EFFORTS IN THE FINAL PERIOD (12 MONTHS) WILL PUSH TOWARDS OPTIMIZED UNSTEADY CONTROL OF THE CAVITY FLOW IN BOTH SUBSONIC AND SUPERSONIC CONDITIONS. THIS WILL INVOLVE IMPLEMENTING CONTROL STRATEGIES THAT HAVE BEEN INFORMED BY THE NUMERICAL EXPERIMENTS LEADING UP TO THIS POINT IN THE PROGRAM. THE COMPUTATIONAL GROUP WILL DOCUMENT AND DEVELOP DESIGN GUIDELINES FOR FLOW CONTROL. IT IS ALSO ANTICIPATED THAT ALGORITHMS GENERATED FROM THIS PROJECT WILL BE DOCUMENTED AND DISSEMINATED AT SUCH TIME.

subaward_description:
IN YEAR 1 AND 2 OF THIS PROJECT, WE HAVE ESTABLISHED THE HYPOTHESES-DRIVEN STUDIES ON LASIS-GRR FOR RATIONAL DESIGN, AND REPRODUCIBLE SYNTHESIS OF HETERONANOSTRUCTURED AND COMPOSITE NPS. THE INTERFACIAL ENERGETICS OF THESE COMPOSITE NPS WERE TUNED BY TAILORING VARYING AL/AL4C3 AND METASTABLE AL-OXIDES NANOSTRUCTURES IN BIPHASIC OR, CORE-SHELL FORM ENCAPSULATED IN GRAPHITIC INTERLAYERED SHELLS SUCH THAT THEY CAN BE ACTIVATED UNDER DESIRED CONDITIONS (PRESSURE, TEMPERATURE ETC.). SUCH COMPOSITE AND METASTABLE NPS CAN ALLOW STRAIN ENERGY TUNING VIA STRUCTURAL REARRANGEMENTS OF SURFACE ATOMS WITHIN THE FULLERENE-LIKE LAYERS AND/OR, NANOCAGES.
SPECIFICALLY, FOR YEAR 3-4, THE SYNTHESES PLANS WILL ENTAIL THE DEVELOPMENT OF FUNDAMENTAL SCIENCE FOR MANUFACTURING NEXT-GENERATION ENERGETIC NPS COMPRISING CORE-SHELL NCS OF METAL/METALLOID CORES (AL, NI, ZR, B) COATED WITH GRAPHITIC/CERAMIC FULLERENE-LIKE SHELLS. CURRENT GOAL OF THE PROPOSED STUDY WILL BE TO FOCUS ON METAL/METALLOID NCS IN METASTABLE STATES COATED WITH GRAPHITIC SHELLS THAT WILL THEN BE EXTENDED IN FUTURE TO OTHER METAL/METALLOID COMPOSITE SYSTEMS.
STRUCTURE-PROPERTY RELATIONS FOR ENERGETIC BEHAVIORS (ENERGY CONTENTS, DENSITY, RELEASE RATES, BURN RATES) WILL GUIDE MATERIAL DESIGNS AND SYNTHESIS PARAMETERS.

subaward_description: PROFESSOR AWODEY IS AN EXPERT ON HOMOTOPY TYPE THEORY, CATEGORY THEORY, AND FORMAL LOGIC. HE HAS BEEN AN ACTIVE RESEARCHER AND DEVELOPER OF THE HOMOTOPY TYPE THEORY RESEARCH PROGRAM SINCE ITS FOUNDING AND CONTINUES TO BE AN ACTIVE CONTRIBUTOR, WITH MANY SCIENTIFIC PUBLICATIONS ON THE TOPIC. DURING THE MURI PROJECT HE WILL WORK ON THE MATHEMATICAL AREAS OUTLINED IN THE SCIENTIFIC PROPOSAL, SPECIFICALLY FOCUSSING ON CUBICAL SEMANTICS AND THEIR RELATION TO SIMPLICIAL SEMANTICS, AS WELL AS REALIZABILITY AND ARTIN GLUING. HE WILL SUPERVISE A RESEARCH TEAM CONSISTING OF ONE POSTDOCTORAL RESEARCHER AND ONE DOCTORAL STUDENT, WHO WILL WORK ON RELATED RESEARCH AREAS. TEAM MEMBERS WILL PRODUCE SCIENTIFIC PAPERS FOR PUBLICATION IN JOURNALS AND COMPUTER CODE VERIFYING THEIR MATHEMATICAL RESULTS, WHICH WILL BE PUBLICLY AVAILABLE. THEY WILL ALSO GIVE LECTURES DEVOTED TO EXPLAINING AND DISSEMINTING THESE RESULTS AT INTERNATIONAL SCIENTIFIC CONFERNCES. PROF. AWODEY WILL DEVOTE 50% OF HIS TIME DURING THE ACADEMIC YEAR TO RESEARCH ACTIVITIES AS WELL AS TWO FULL SUMMER MONTHS TO THOSE ACTIVITIES. HE WILL ALSO HOST VISITORS AND COLLABORATORS WHO WILL WORK JOINTLY WITH THE LOCAL RESEARCH TEAM ON THESE ACTIVITIES. PROJECTED ACTIVITIES INCLUDE A WEEKLY RESEARCH SEMINAR, WEEKLY WORKING GROUPS ON FORMALIZATION OF MATHEMATICS, AS WELL AS FOCUSSED WORKING GROUPS ON INDIVIDUAL RESEARCH TOPICS. subaward_description:
THE GOAL OF THE PROPOSED PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP FUNDAMENTAL UNDERSTANDING FOR A NEW TYPE OF MULTIFUNCTIONAL NEUROMORPHIC INTERFACEÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â‚¬Ï¿½A HYBRID BIOMOLECULAR 
SYNAPSEÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â‚¬Ï¿½CAPABLE OF SELECTIVE IONIC AND BIOCHEMICAL SENSING, SIGNAL PROCESSING, LEARNING, AND MEMORY IN WET, CELLULAR ENVIRONMENTS. 
CO-PI RIVNAY WILL LEAD OBJECTIVE 1, WITH THE GOAL OF STUDYING AND IMPROVING THE BIOMEMBRANE-POLYMER INTERFACE TO ENABLE EFFECTIVE TRANSDUCTION AND 
MEMBRANE STABILITY. TO ACHIEVE THIS GOAL, RIVNAY WILL SYNTHETICALLY MODIFY THE BIOMEMBRANE-POLYMER INTERFACE. THIS WILL BE DONE BY BLEND MODIFICATION, NEW 
CONDUCTIVE/SEMICONDUCTIVE CHANNEL MATERIALS, OR BY TETHERING OF LIPIDS OR BLOCK CO-POLYMERS TO ORGANIC ELECTRONICS LAYERS. THE RESULTING MEMBRANE PROPERTIES 
WILL BE ASSESSED VIA BY CASTING CONDUCTING POLYMERS WITH NOTED SYNTHETIC MODIFICATION DIRECTLY ON GLASS SLIDES OR ON QCM SENSORS FOR EXPERIMENTS OF 
FLORESCENCE RECOVERY AFTER PHOTOBLEACHING (FRAP), FORESTER RESONANT ENERGY TRANSFER (FRET), OR QUARTZ CRYSTAL MICROBALANCE (QCM) GRAVIMETRY. IMPEDANCE 
AND ELECTRICAL CHARACTERIZATION OF ELECTRODES AND ORGANIC ELECTROCHEMICAL TRANSISTORS (OECTS) TO CHARACTERIZE BIOMEMBRANE PROPERTIES AND TO ASSESS 
BIOMEMBRANE STABILITY. IF SUCCESSFUL, THIS EFFORT WILL RESULT IN MORE STABLE, INTEGRATED, AND HIGHER PERFORMING BIO-HYBRID DEVICES. RIVNAY WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR 
MICROFABRICATION OF DEVICES FOR EFFORTS IN OBJECTIVE 3 AND 4, WHERE DEVICE PERFORMANCE, SENSING AND CIRCUITS WILL BE IMPLEMENTED. 
RIVNAY WILL ADVISE 2 TRAINEES (NOMINALLY 1 GRADUATE STUDENT AND ONE POST-DOC) FOR 5 YEARS 
CO-PI KAMAT WILL LEAD OBJECTIVE 2, WITH THE GOAL TO DEMONSTRATE SUCCESSFUL INCORPORATION AND FUNCTIONAL MODIFICATION OF TRANSMEMBRANE PROTEINS (TMP) INTO 
BIOMEMBRANES AND TO UNDERSTAND HOW MEMBRANE COMPOSITION AFFECTS TMP ORIENTATION, CONCENTRATION, AND FUNCTIONALITY. KAMAT WILL USE WELL-KNOWN NEURONAL 
CHANNEL, TWIK-RELATED K+ CHANNEL 1 (TREK-1) AND DEMONSTRATE THAT TMPS ARE FUNCTIONAL ON POLYMER-SUPPORTED SLBS. SHE WILL ASSESS THE IMPACT OF 
MEMBRANE COMPOSITION AND OF AN UNDERLYING MIXED POLYMER CONDUCTING SUBSTRATE ON PROTEIN DIFFUSION, LOCATION, AND FUNCTION. IN ADDITION, SHE WILL MUTATE THE 
TREK-1 CHANNEL PORE TO MODIFY VOLTAGE SENSITIVITY AND MEASURE THE RESULTING CHANNEL PROPERTIES. IF SUCCESSFUL, THIS APPROACH PAVES THE WAY FOR USING TMPS 
FOR TUNABLE MEMRISTIVE DEVICES AS WELL AS BIOSENSORS, E.G., FOR ANALYTE QUANTIFICATION, ETC. 
KAMAT WILL ADVISE 2 TRAINEES (NOMINALLY 1 GRADUATE STUDENT AND ONE POST-DOC) FOR 5 YEARS 
BOTH CO-PIS WILL PARTICIPATE IN MONTHLY CALLS WITH THE ENTIRE TEAM, INCLUDING THE LEAD PI SARLES AND THE ENTIRE TEAM, AND WILL CONTRIBUTE TO WRITTEN REPORTS AND PRESENTATIONS PER THE FREQUENCY DICTATED BY THE SPONSOR AND THE LEAD INSTITUTION.

subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
THE TASKS TO BE PERFORMED DURING THE PROJECT ARE LISTED BELOW: 
TASK 1: INSTITUTE ADMINISTRATION. TASK 1 COMPRISES ACTIVITIES NEEDED TO ENSURE SMOOTH FUNCTIONING OF THE INSTITUTE AND TO LAUNCH THE NEXT ROUND OF RESEARCH PROJECTS. THE ANNUAL PROJECT REVIEW MEETING WILL BE HELD IN THE FIRST QUARTER OF 2022. THIS WILL ALSO BRING TOGETHER ALL OF THE PROJECT LEADS TO MAKE SURE ALL ARE AWARE OF POLICIES AND PROCEDURES OF THE NIUVT, INCLUDING EXPORT-CONTROLLED AND CLASSIFIED INFORMATION CERTIFICATION (ECC) AND TECHNOLOGY CONTROL/ SECURITY PLAN (TCSP). TO FACILITATE ALIGNMENT OF RESEARCH PROJECTS WITH NAVAL OBJECTIVES, A FACULTY TRAINING WORKSHOP WILL BE HELD TO INSTRUCT FACULTY ON THE CHALLENGES AND THREATS IN THE UNDERSEA DOMAIN, INCLUDING TYPICAL MISSIONS OF UNDERSEA VEHICLES. ADMINISTRATIVE PROFESSIONAL WILL OVERSEE THE SCHEDULING OF "QUARTERLY TECHNOLOGY REVIEW (QTR)" WITH THE TECHNICAL LEADS AND ADMINISTRATION STAFF. ENSURE BUDGETS ARE UPDATED EVERY 2 WEEKS AND SENT TO THE LEAD PL ON A REGULAR BASIS. WORK ON MONTHLY NEWSLETTER WITH UCONN. APPROVE PCARD PURCHASES AND STUDENT HIRING FROM A BUDGET STANDPOINT ON ANY NIUVT PROJECT. 
TASK 2: RESEARCH PROGRAM. TASK 2 ENCOMPASSES THE RESEARCH WORK BEING DONE ON NIUVT PROJECTS BY FACULTY AND STUDENT TEAMS. PROJECTS HAVE BEEN IDENTIFIED THROUGH A COMPETITIVE PROCESS DESCRIBED IN SECTION E (MANAGEMENT APPROACH) AND SELECTED FOR FUNDING BY ONR. RESULTS WILL BE PRESENTED TO THE NAVY ON AN ANNUAL BASIS. THE DURATION OF SEED PROJECTS IS ONE YEAR AND COMPREHENSIVE PROJECTS 2 YEARS. 
TASK 3: RESEARCH ALIGNMENT AND TECHNOLOGY TRANSLATION (RA&TT). RA&TT TASKS ARE DESIGNED TO SELECT PROJECTS OF INTEREST TO THE NAVY. KEY ACTIVITIES INCLUDE: NIUVT CONFORM, A WORKSHOP TO BRAINSTORM CONCEPTS AND TECHNOLOGIES TO SOLVE FUTURE UNDERSEA NEEDS, AND RESEARCH ALIGNMENT AND TECHNOLOGY TRANSITION MEETINGS ATTENDED BY NIUVT FACULTY NUWC, AND EB SMES. 
TASK 4: WORKFORCE AND OUTREACH ACTIVITIES. THE NIUVT TEAM WILL PARTICIPATE IN SENEDIA DEFENSE INNOVATION DAYS 2021, TO ENGAGE WITH THE NAVAL SUPPLY CHAIN. 
TASK 5: PROJECT MANAGEMENT. TASK 5 ENCOMPASSES PROJECT OVERSIGHT AND REPORTING ACTIVITIES CARRIED OUT BY THE INSTITUTE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, RESEARCH LEADS, AND INSTITUTE ADMINISTRATOR. THIS LEADERSHIP TEAM WILL MEET FORMALLY ONCE PER MONTH TO REVIEW PROGRESS; HOWEVER, THE LEADERSHIP TEAM WILL CONNECT INFORMALLY ON A WEEKLY BASIS TO ENSURE THAT THE NIUVT PROJECT IS PROCEEDING AS PLANNED. THE LEADERSHIP WILL MEET WITH THE ADVISORY BOARD THREE TIMES PER YEAR. REPORTS WILL BE PRESENTED TO ONR ON AN ANNUAL BASIS.

THIS EFFORT SHALL BE CARRIED OUT GENERALLY AS SET FORTH IN EXHIBIT B, RESEARCH DESCRIPTION DOCUMENT, DATED MAY 3, 2024, AND IN THE RECIPIENTS REVISED PROPOSAL TITLED, DENSIFIED ANISOTROPIC NANO LIGNOCELLULOSIC FIBERS, DATED JANUARY 19, 2024, COPIES OF WHICH ARE IN THE POSSESSION OF BOTH PARTIES.
subawardee_business_types: NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
US DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE OPERATIONS GENERATE LARGE AMOUNTS OF SCRAP WOOD, CARDBOARD, AND
PAPER WASTE. SIMULTANEOUSLY, DOD OPERATIONS REQUIRE LARGE QUANTITIES OF FRESH LUMBER AND WOOD
PRODUCTS. THIS OVERALL PROJECT WILL USE WASTE FEEDSTOCK THAT IS A COMBINATION OF SCRAP WOOD,
CARDBOARD, AND PAPER WASTE, AND THROUGH A SERIES OF PROCESSES CREATE A PRODUCT THAT IS STRONGER
THAN WOOD. THIS STATEMENT OF WORK IS FOR THE PORTION OF THE OVERALL PROJECT THAT WILL BE DIRECTED
BY VALERIE THOMAS, WHICH IS TO EVALUATE THE COSTS OF THIS APPROACH IN THE CONTEXT OF THE NEEDS OF
DOD OPERATIONS.
THE PROCESSES INVOLVED ARE (1) ORGANOSOLVE PRE-PROCESSING OF CHIPPED WASTE FEEDSTOCK, (2)
NANOFIBRILLATION OF THE RESULTING CELLULOSE-RICH FIBERS, (3) ALIGNMENT OF THE FIBERS, (4) HOT PRESSING
AND DRYING OF THE MATERIAL ALONG WITH COATING WITH HEMICELLULOSE-DERIVED FURFURAL, A CO-PRODUCT
OF THE ORGANOSOLV PROCESS THAT REDUCES WATER SWELLING POTENTIAL OF THE BOARD PRODUCT, AND (5)
COATING THE FINAL BOARD WITH LIGNIN, ANOTHER CO-PRODUCT OF THE ORGANOLV PROCESS, WHICH PROVIDES
WATER REPELLENCE AND FLAME RETARDANCE

subaward_description:
"SHVETSÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢ GROUP WILL PROVIDE PLASMA MODELING SUPPORT FOR THE PROJECT, AS SPECIFIED IN THE FULL
PROPOSAL. TO THAT END, HIS GROUP WILL USE A MODIFIED VERSION OF A MASSIVELY PARALLEL 3D PIC CODE
VLPL (VIRTUAL LASER-PLASMA LABORATORY) THAT HAS BEEN ORIGINALLY DEVELOPED FOR MODELING INTENSE
LASER-PLASMA INTERACTIONS, BUT HAS BEEN SINCE MODIFIED BY THE SHVETS GROUP TO INCLUDE EXTERNALLY
IMPOSED STATIC MAGNETIC FIELDS, IONIZATION, AND OTHER FEATURES. SHVETSÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢ GROUP HAS TIME ALLOCATIONS
ON STAMPEDE, AND MORE THAN A DECADE OF EXPERIENCE RUNNING VLPL SIMULATIONS ON VARIOUS
COMPUTER CLUSTERS AT THE TEXAS ADVANCED COMPUTING CENTER (TACC).
FOUR DIFFERENT MODES CAPABILITIES OF THE CODE WILL BE USED BASED ON THE DIFFERENT TYPES OF PLASMA
DESCRIPTION: FULLY KINETIC OR COLD FLUID FOR DIFFERENT CHARGED SPECIES (I.E., ELECTRONS AND IONS). THE
CODE IS FULLY SELF-CONSISTENT, I.E. THE ELECTRIC AND MAGNETIC FIELDS CALCULATED FROM THE CURRENTS
CARRIED BY THE MACRO-PARTICLES (KINETIC SPECIES) AND BY THE CHARGED FLUID ARE THEN USED TO ADVANCE
IN TIME THE MACRO-PARTICLES AND THE CHARGED FLUID. THE FLUID DESCRIPTION IS FULLY NONLINEAR; THIS IS
ESSENTIAL TO PROPERLY RESOLVE THE SINGULARLY STRONG RESPONSE OF THE ELECTRONS WHEN THE LOCAL UPPERHYBRID
FREQUENCY MATCHES THE DRIVER FREQUENCY. FOUR TYPES OF SIMULATIONS WILL BE CARRIED OUT: (A)
IMMOBILE IONS, FLUID ELECTRONS; (B) IMMOBILE IONS, KINETIC ELECTRONS, (C) FLUID IONS, KINETIC ELECTRONS,
AND (D) KINETIC ELECTRONS AND IONS.
IN ADDITION TO THIS COMPUTATIONAL EFFORT, SHVETSÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢ GROUP WILL CONDUCT EXPERIMENTS AIMED AT
DEVELOPING NOVEL PLASMA-ENHANCED AND PLASMA-BASED METAMATERIALS. THOSE WILL BE CARRIED OUT IN
SHVETSÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢ MICROWAVE LABORATORY AT CLARK HALL. THE PARTS FOR THE MICROWAVE METAMATERIALS WILL BE
FABRICATED AT THE PHYSICS DEPARTMENTÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢S MACHINE SHOP, TO WHICH SHVETSÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢ GROUP HAS HAD ACCESS FOR
SEVERAL YEARS.
IN ADDITION, SHVETSÃƑÂ¢Ã¢Â€ŠÂ¬Ã¢Â€ŽÂ¢ GROUP WILL CARRY OUT THEORETICAL/ANALYTICAL WORK AIMED AT UNDERSTANDING
COLLISIONLESS DAMPING OF THE TOPOLOGICALLY-ROBUST EDGE STATES, AND THE EFFECT OF SUCH DAMPING ON
THE TOPOLOGICAL PROPERTIES OF THESE PLASMA-ENABLED STATES."

subawardee_name: THE TRUSTEES OF COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY IN THE CITY OF NEW YORK
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
1) DEVELOPMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL SCENARIOS (SPECIFICALLY CLIMATE SCENARIOS) THAT ARE ABLE TO PROVIDE A 
GOOD REPRESENTATION OF BOTH THE EVOLUTION OF THE BASE STATE OF CLIMATE AND OF SPATIALLY CORRELATED 
AND TEMPORALLY CLUSTERED SHOCKS ASSOCIATED WITH MULTIPLE CLIMATE VARIABLES (RAIN, TEMPERATURE, WINDS). 
THESE CLIMATE SCENARIOS WILL THEN BE MAPPED INTO FOOD AND ECONOMIC OUTCOMES AT A COUNTRY OR 
REGION SCALE SO THAT POTENTIAL SPACE-TIME DYNAMICS OF THESE FACTORS THAT INFLUENCE MIGRATION DYNAMICS 
CAN BE SPECIFIED. NETWORK MODELS OF GLOBAL/REGIONAL FOOD SCARCITY AND THE ASSOCIATED PRICE AND 
CONSUMPTION SHOCKS WILL BE MODELED AS PART OF THIS WORK.
2) THE DEVELOPMENT OF STRUCTURAL MODELS TO BE USED AS A PART OF THE MIGRATION DYNAMICS MODEL. THESE 
INCLUDE BAYESIAN NETWORKS BASED ON EXPERT ELICITATION, AS WELL AS HIERARCHICAL BAYESIAN MODELS THAT USE 
OBSERVATIONS FROM MULTIPLE SOURCES AND OVER MULTIPLE SPATIAL SUPPORT TO IDENTIFY UNDERLYING LATENT 
DYNAMICS AND PROVIDE A FUNCTIONAL REPRESENTATION BETWEEN MIGRATION DYNAMICS AND THE SOCIOENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINANTS OF THE DYNAMICS.
THE WORK WILL BE COORDINATED BY UPMANU LALL AND MICHAEL PUMA, BOTH OF WHOM HAVE EXPERTISE IN 
CLIMATE AND ENVIRONMENTAL DYNAMICS. PUMA ADDITIONALLY IS AN EXPERT ON NETWORK MODELING AND FOOD 
DYNAMICS, WHILE LALL IS AN EXPERT ON MATHEMATICAL STATISTICS AND MACHINE LEARNING.
EACH OF THEM WILL BE SUPPORTED BY A POSTDOCTORAL FELLOW

subaward_description:
THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS PROPOSAL IS TO DEVELOP NEW THEORETICAL FOUNDATIONS AND ALGORITHMS FOR AI
SYSTEMS TO DECEIVE ADVERSARIES AND COUNTER THEIR DECEPTIVE TACTICS. THE ALGORITHMS WE DEVELOP
WILL (1) TAKE ACTIONS THAT INSTILL FALSE BELIEFS IN ADVERSARIES, (2) INFER ADVERSARIES’ OBJECTIVES AND
INTENTIONS, AND (3) USE NOVEL TACTICS TO COUNTER WILLFUL, MALICIOUS ADVERSARIES WHO ARE ALSO ATTEMPTING TO DECEIVE AND RESIST BEING DECEIVED.

IN PARTICULAR, HU WILL BE ACTIVELY WORKING ON THE TASKS (I) HOW TO DESIGN DECISION RULES TO DECEIVE
ADVERSARIES ABOUT OBJECTIVES; (II) HOW TO DESIGN DECISION RULES TO REVEAL ADVERSARIES’ INTENTIONS, IN
PARTICULAR IN HIGH DIMENSIONAL SETTINGS; (III) DEEP LEARNING ALGORITHMS TO COUNTER ADVERSARIES’ EFFORTS;
(IV) DEEP GENERATIVE MODELS TO STRATEGICALLY INTERLEAVING DECEPTIVE AND COUNTER-DECEPTIVE POLICIES; AND (V) EXPERIMENTS TO TEST THE DEVELOPMENTS IN (I) - (IV).

THE CO-PI HU COMMITS TO CONDUCTING THOROUGH RESEARCH, IMPLEMENTING INNOVATIVE METHODOLOGIES,
DELIVERING PROGRESS REPORTS, ADHERING TO ESTABLISHED TIMELINES, AND PRODUCING HIGH-QUALITY
DELIVERABLES IN LINE WITH PROJECT OBJECTIVES. THE CO-PI HU WILL LEVERAGE HER EXPERTISE AND RESOURCES
TO EXECUTE THE PROJECT SCOPE EFFICIENTLY AND EFFECTIVELY, ENSURING ALIGNMENT WITH THE PROJECT SPONSOR’S EXPECTATIONS AND REQUIREMENTS. THROUGHOUT THE DURATION OF THE PROJECT, THE CO-PI HU WILL MAINTAIN OPEN COMMUNICATION CHANNELS, ACTIVELY COLLABORATE WITH STAKEHOLDERS, AND DEMONSTRATE A COMMITMENT TO ACHIEVING PROJECT MILESTONES AND DELIVERABLES.

prime_award_cfda_numbers_and_titles: 12.800: AIR FORCE DEFENSE RESEARCH SCIENCES PROGRAM
subawardee_name: THE JOHNS HOPKINS UNIVERSITY APPLIED PHYSICS LABORATORY LLC
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,OTHER NOT FOR PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
SOLAR ECLIPSES PRESENT A STUNNING CELESTIAL SPECTACLE THAT CAPTIVATES THE ATTENTION OF PEOPLE WORLDWIDE. BUT THESE PERIODIC AND
PREDICTABLE EVENTS ARE MUCH MORE THAN JUST A VISUAL TREAT - THEY OFFER A UNIQUE OPPORTUNITY TO DEEPEN OUR UNDERSTANDING OF THE
CHEMICAL AND DYNAMICAL PROCESSES IN THE IONOSPHERE-THERMOSPHERE (IT) SYSTEM. WE USE THE OCTOBER 2023 AND APRIL 2024
ECLIPSES AS CASE STUDIES TO REVEAL THE PHYSICAL PROCESSES CAUSING A SIGNIFICANT INCREASE IN THE THERMOSPHERIC O/N2 RATIO
MEASURED BY GOLD, AND TRANSIENTS IN THE ENERGETIC PARTICLE BACKGROUND TELEMETRY ONBOARD DMSP SATELLITES. WE HYPOTHESIZED
THAT ONE OF THE CONTROLLING FACTORS CAUSING THESE UNEXPLAINED MEASUREMENTS IS PHOTOELECTRONS.
WE PROPOSE TO DEVISE THE MOST ACCURATE DESCRIPTION OF SOLAR EUV IRRADIANCE DURING ECLIPSES BY MERGING THE FISM2 IRRADIANCE
MODEL WITH THE PYECLIPSE OBSERVATIONS EUV MODEL OF ECLIPSE OCCULTATIONS. THIS ECLIPSE MODEL WILL BE INTEGRATED INTO THE
WAM-IPE MODEL OF EARTH'S ATMOSPHERE, IONOSPHERE, AND PLASMASPHERE. WAM-IPE IS A GLOBAL MODEL WITH THE MOST ACCURATE
PHYSICS-BASED DESCRIPTION OF PHOTOELECTRON TRANSPORT, WHICH IN TURN, IS CONTROLLED BY THE SOLAR EUV IRRADIANCE. PHOTOELECTRONS
ARE THE CONTROLLING DIVER OF ATMOSPHERIC OPTICAL EMISSIONS MEASURED BY GOLD AND CAN CARRY PARTIAL CURRENTS THAT MAY BE
CAUSING THE TRANSIENTS ONBOARD DMSP INSTRUMENTS.
WE WILL QUANTIFY THE IMPACTS OF AN ACCURATE SOLAR EUV INPUT TO THE WAMP-IPE MODEL BY COMPARING THE RESULTING IT AND
PHOTOELECTRON TRANSPORT AGAINST THE MODEL RESPONSES TO A CONVENTIONAL ECLIPSE MODEL USING A UNIFORM ECLIPSE MASK. THE
MODEL RESULTS WILL BE COMPARED AGAINST SATELLITE OBSERVATION FOR BOTH ECLIPSES. THE PROJECT RESULTS WILL BE DISSEMINATED AT
CONFERENCES AND ARTICLES PUBLISHED IN SCIENTIFIC JOURNALS.

subaward_description:
SELF-ASSEMBLY HOLDS TREMENDOUS POTENTIAL FOR THE BOTTOM-UP FABRICATION OF PHOTONIC METAMATERIALS & DEVICES THAT WOULD BE IMPOSSIBLE TO REALIZE WITH LITHOGRAPHIC METHODS, PARTICULARLY IN TECHNOLOGICALLY IMPORTANT WAVELENGTH REGIMES SUCH AS THE VISIBLE AND NEAR-INFRARED. THIS IS ESPECIALLY TRUE FOR THREE-DIMENSIONAL STRUCTURES, WHERE CURRENT FABRICATION MODALITIES ARE BASED ON OPTICAL LITHOGRAPHY (E.G., TWO-PHOTON POLYMERIZATION), & WHERE THE DIFFRACTION LIMIT CONSTRAINS STRUCTURAL FEATURE SIZES TO BE LARGER THAN THE OPTICAL WAVELENGTH. SELF-ASSEMBLING COLLOIDAL MATERIALS OVERCOME THIS CONSTRAINT BY THEIR NATURE & CAN BE SCALED UP TO LARGE SYSTEM SIZE (CM-SCALE) STRAIGHTFORWARDLY. FURTHERMORE, THEY CAN BE DIRECTLY COMPATIBLE WITH GAIN MEDIA SUCH AS ORGANIC DYES, QUANTUM DOTS, & QUANTUM WELLS. THE KEY INGREDIENTS IN THE SELF-ASSEMBLY APPROACH TO NOVEL PHOTONIC CRYSTALS & METAMATERIAL DEVICES ARE WAVELENGTH SCALE (~0.1-1ÃŽÂ¼M) DESIGNER COLLOIDAL BUILDING BLOCKS THAT SPONTANEOUSLY ASSEMBLE DESIRED 2 & 3-DIMENSIONAL STRUCTURES. 3-DIMENSIONAL STRUCTURES WILL PROVIDE COMPLETE CONFINEMENT IN ALL DIRECTIONS AND WILL REDUCE DEVICE FOOTPRINT ON A PHOTONIC CHIP. THE GOAL OF THE RESEARCH PROPOSED HERE IS TO USE COMPLEX, DESIGNER OPTICAL-SCALE COLLOIDAL Ã¢Â‚¬Å“METAATOMS,Ã¢Â‚¬Ï¿½ IN COMBINATION WITH NON-HERMITIAN ELEMENTS SUCH AS SPATIALLY VARYING LOSS & GAIN FROM QUANTUM EMITTERS, TO REALIZE NOVEL & UNCONVENTIONAL LARGE-SCALE NON-HERMITIAN PHOTONIC DEVICES. INDEED, JUST AS DIFFERENT ATOMIC ELEMENTS COMBINE TO FORM COMPLEX MOLECULES & CRYSTALLINE MATERIALS, SO WILL OPTICAL-SCALE COLLOIDAL META-ATOMS ASSEMBLE FUNCTIONAL METAMATERIALS, BOTH AMORPHOUS AND CRYSTALLINE. NON-HERMITIAN PROPERTIES (BOTH LINEAR AND NONLINEAR-PARAMETRIC) WILL GIVE RISE TO EXCEPTIONAL POINTS, LINES, RINGS, & SURFACES, & FACILITATE UNIQUE PROPERTIES SUCH AS UNIDIRECTIONAL INVISIBILITY, ENHANCED PHOTONIC CRYSTAL CAVITY SENSING BASED ON EXCEPTIONAL POINTS, & HIGH-POWER SINGLE-MODE LASING WITH HIGH SPATIAL & SPECTRAL PURITY.
AN OUTSTANDING PROBLEM IN PHOTONIC DEVICE DESIGN, PARTICULARLY WITH RESPECT TO SEMICONDUCTOR LASERS, IS THE REALIZATION OF HIGH-POWER SINGLE-MODE LASERS. AS THE ACTIVE REGION GETS LARGER (AND AN ACCOMPANYING GREATER POWER OUTPUT), MORE MODES ARE SUPPORTED, LEADING TO DIMINISHED SPECTRAL & SPATIAL MODE PURITY AS THEY LASE. AN APPROACH TO MITIGATING THIS TRADE-OFF IS THE USE OF PHOTONIC DIRAC POINTS IN TWO DIMENSIONS, WHERE THE PHOTONIC DENSITY-OF-STATES GOES TO ZERO. THIS IS FURTHER IMPROVED IN 3 DIMENSIONS VIA THE ANALOGOUS USE OF PHOTONIC WEYL POINTS, WHICH ARE TOPOLOGICALLY PROTECTED DEGENERACIES WHERE THE DENSITY-OF-STATES ALSO GOES TO ZERO. THE REALIZATION OF WEYL POINTS REQUIRES THE STRUCTURE TO BREAK INVERSION OR TIME-REVERSAL SYMMETRY. A KEY GOAL OF OUR PROPOSED WORK WILL BE TO CREATE THE COLLOIDAL BUILDING BLOCKS TO SELF-ASSEMBLE LATTICES THAT BREAK INVERSION SYMMETRY AND EXHIBIT WEYL POINTS IN THEIR PHOTONIC BAND STRUCTURES (PINE, RECHTSMAN, ZHEN). THIS WILL BE CARRIED OUT VIA THE ASSEMBLY OF THE ZINCBLENDE STRUCTURE (BIPARTITE DIAMOND, WHICH WE HAVE SHOWN THEORETICALLY TO EXHIBIT WEYL POINTS IN ITS BAND STRUCTURE. WE WILL ALSO EXPLORE THE USE OF NON-RECIPROCAL ELEMENTS (ALIVISATOS, BRAUN, FAN) TO INDUCE WEYL POINTS, WHICH WILL PROVIDE REDUNDANCY VIA AN ALTERNATIVE APPROACH, THEREFORE MITIGATING PROJECT RISK.
TO INDUCE NON-HERMITIAN BEHAVIOR, WE WILL INCORPORATE GAIN MEDIA IN OUR COLLOIDAL ASSEMBLIES IN MULTIPLE WAYS (PROVIDING REDUNDANCY OF APPROACH). WE WILL APPLY OUR KNOWLEDGE ON HOW TO GROW GAAS, INGAAS, & ALAS HETROSTRUCTURES (BRAUN) TO GROW SOLUTION-DISPERSIBLE SEMICONDUCTOR QUANTUM DOT Ã¢Â‚¬ËŒCHIPLETSÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢ WITH OPTICAL PROPERTIES RIVALING BULK HIGH-PURITY III-V MATERIALS; THESE WILL BE DIRECTLY INCORPORATED INTO THE SOLUTION SURROUNDING THE COLLOIDAL PARTICLES.

UNIVERSITY OF KENTUCKY
STATEMENT OF WORK

AS PART OF THE PROPOSED WORK, THE FOLLOWING TASK WILL BE ACCOMPLISHED AT THE UNIVERSITY
OF KENTUCKY, UNDER THE SUPERVISION OF PROF. ALEXANDRE MARTIN

• A MATERIAL MODEL FOR KATS-MR WILL BE CONSTRUCTED FOR A PSUB ASSEMBLY, FOLLOWING
AN ESTABLISHED PROCEDURE. THE MODEL WILL USE EFFECTIVE THERMAL, MECHANICAL
RADIATIVE AND FLOW PROPERTIES TO DEFINE THE PSUB. IT WILL ALSO INCLUDE A CERAMIC
COATING MODEL FOR THE SURFACE, AND AN ABLATIVE CARBON MODEL FOR THE STRUTS.

• TO VALIDATE THE NUMERICAL APPROACH, A SERIES OF INCREASINGLY COMPLEX EXPERIMENTS
WILL BE MODELED. MORE SPECIFICALLY, THE EXPERIMENTS CONDUCTED AT THE HVOF
FACILITY OF APL WILL BE SIMULATED.

• AN ARC-JET CAMPAIGN WILL BE CONDUCTED AT NASA (AMES OR/AND LANGLEY) TO TEST THE
PSUB ASSEMBLY UNDER A REALISTIC THERMO-CHEMICAL ENVIRONMENT. THESE
EXPERIMENTS WILL THEN BE MODELED.

• THE KATS MR SOLVER WILL BE FULLY COUPLED TO THE CHAMPS HYPERSONIC
AEROTHERMODYNAMIC SOLVER TO STUDY THE THERMO-STRUCTURAL RESPONSE OF THE PSUB
UNDER HYPERSONIC FLIGHT CONDITIONS.

subaward_description:
THE OBJECTIVE OF THE PROJECT IS TO ANALYZE AND DESIGN INTERACTIVE ENVIRONMENTS AND DESIGN SYSTEMS THAT BETTER ACHIEVE SOCIETAL GOALS. IMPORTANTLY, THE PARTICIPANTS IN THE STRATEGIC ENVIRONMENT CAN BE A DIVERSE SET OF AGENTS: HUMANS, DECISION ENGINES WITH HUMAN INTERVENTION (THUS SEMI-AUTONOMOUS) AND AI AGENTS (THUS AUTONOMOUS). THERE IS ALREADY A WIDE AND RAPIDLY GROWING NUMBER OF ENVIRONMENTS WHERE THIS INTERACTION AMONG HETEROGENEOUS AND STRATEGIC PARTICIPANTS IS OCCURRING, SUCH AS MARKETS, CORPORATIONS, EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS, AND GOVERNMENTS.

THE SETTINGS FOR THESE SYSTEMS ARE RAPIDLY EVOLVING AND WITH A DIVERSE MIX OF AGENT TYPES---HUMAN, SEMI-AUTONOMOUS AND AI AGENTS---WITH SOME AGENTS RELIANT ONLY ON SIMPLE HEURISTICS, AND OTHERS USING SOPHISTICATED LEARNING METHODS.

THESE COMPLEX SOCIO-TECHNOLOGICAL SYSTEMS ARE OFTEN CHARACTERIZED BY UNCERTAINTY REGARDING ACTIONS, STRATEGIES, AND THE PHYSICAL WORLD. MOREOVER, THE ANALYST MAKING PREDICTIONS OR GUIDING THE BEHAVIOR MAY HAVE LIMITED ACCESS TO INFORMATION TOO. COMPUTATIONAL LIMITATIONS CAN AFFECT BOTH HUMANS AND MACHINES, AS CAN BEHAVIORAL BIASES INHERENT IN HUMAN PSYCHOLOGY OR LEARNED BY AI AGENTS. ACCURATE PREDICTIONS CAN LEAD TO THE DESIGN OF LEARNING ALGORITHMS AND MACHINES THAT PERFORM BETER IN STRATEGIC SETTINGS, AID IN UNDERSTANDING THE UNDERLYING CAUSES OF OBSERVED OUTCOMES, AND ULTIMATELY RESULT IN BETTER DESIGNED SYSTEMS.

DIRK BERGEMANN HAS A LONG TRACK RECORD OF LEADING WORK IN MARKET DESIGN, BAYESIAN LEARNING AND INFORMATION DESIGN. HE WILL CONTRIBUTE HIS KNOWLEDGE IN GAME THEORY, MICROECONOMIC THEORY AND MECHANISM DESIGN THAT WILL FORM THE FOUNDATIONAL BASIS OF THIS RESEARCH PROJECT.

JHU/APL WILL WORK WITH THE UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO (CU) TO ACCOMPLISH THE REQUIREMENTS IN ONR ANNOUNCEMENT NUMBER: # N00014-23-S-F003, TOPIC 8: UNDERSTANDING AND TAILORING THE INTERACTIONS BETWEEN METAMATERIALS AND HYPERSONIC FLOWS. SPECIFICALLY, THE OBJECTIVE OF THE OVERALL PROPOSED EFFORT IS TO ESTABLISH, USING THEORY, COMPUTATION, AND EXPERIMENTATION, AN UNDERSTANDING AND FRAMEWORK FOR PASSIVE MANIPULATION OF HYPERSONIC FLOW AND SHOCK WAVE INTERACTIONS WITH ELASTIC METAMATERIAL-BASED PHONONIC SUBSURFACES (PSUBS) AND POROUS SURFACES FOR THE PURPOSE OF RELIABLY DELAYING BOUNDARY LAYER LAMINAR-TO-TURBULENCE TRANSITION AND CONTROLLING TURBULENCE. IN SUPPORT OF THIS OVER-ARCHING OBJECTIVE, UNDER TASK 2, JHU/APL WILL DESIGN, MODEL AND FABRICATE INNOVATIVE, CUSTOMIZED, ADDITIVELY MANUFACTURED (AM) HIGH TEMPERATURE METAL LATTICES WITH CERAMIC COATINGS FOR THE REALIZATION OF PSUBS AND POROUS SURFACES. EXTENSIVE THERMOMECHANICAL CHARACTERIZATION OF THESE MATERIALS WILL ALSO BE CONDUCTED. JHU/APL WILL ALSO FABRICATE PSUBS TO SUPPORT FLOW EXPERIMENTS IN THREE TYPES OF HYPERSONIC FACILITIES COVERING THE VARIOUS REGIMES OF FLOW CONDITIONS.

STATEMENT OF WORK
PURDUE UNIVERSITY (AND THE BAM6QT AND HYPULSE GROUPS, BOTH LED BY PROF. JOSEPH JEWELL) WILL
ASSIST THE UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO (BOULDER) AND ONR MURI TEAM COLLABORATORS IN TECHNICAL
ASPECTS OF HYPERSONIC WIND TUNNEL TEST AND EVALUATION OF HYPERSONIC BOUNDARY-LAYER CONTROLLING
METAMATERIALS, TO INCLUDE BOTH PHONONIC SUBSURFACES (PSUBS) AND . THE GOAL OF THIS WORK IS TO
MAKE MEASUREMENTS UNDER QUIET AND NOISY FLOW CONDITIONS AT MACH 6, AS WELL AS UNDER HIGHENTHALPY
CONDITIONS FROM POTENTIALLY MACH 4-12, ON A HYPERSONIC GEOMETRY INCLUDING ONE OR
MORE METAMATERIAL OR POROUS PATCHES, SPECIFICALLY TO EVALUATE THE EFFECT OF THESE FEATURES ON
THE LAMINAR/TRANSITIONAL/TURBULENT STATE OF THE BOUNDARY LAYER, HEAT TRANSFER, AND OTHER FLOW
PROPERTIES. THE GEOMETRIES TO BE STUDIED WILL BE DEVELOPED IN COORDINATION WITH THE COLORADO
TEAM IN CONSULTATION WITH NAVY PROGRAM SPONSORS, AND WILL LEVERAGE AS NEEDED PRIOR EXISTING
MODELS IN THE PURDUE INVENTORY. NEW SENSORS WILL BE INSTALLED BY PURDUE, AS WELL AS AN ADAPTOR
SECTION TO ACCOMMODATE THE INSTALLATION OF METAMATERIAL COUPONS AND, POTENTIALLY, VARYING
UPSTREAM CONDITIONS. THE PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE FOR THIS PROJECT IS A TOTAL OF 5 YEARS, AND WE
ANTICIPATE APPROXIMATELY 12-15 TOTAL WEEKS OF BAM6QT TESTING DURING THE COURSE OF THIS
PROJECT. A WEEK OF TESTING WILL ALLOW APPROXIMATELY 25 TO 35 RUNS IN THE BOEING/AFOSR MACH-6
QUIET TUNNEL (BAM6QT), AND UP TO 3 WEEKS ARE EXPECTED AS PART OF THIS WORK EACH YEAR, FOR A
TOTAL OF APPROXIMATELY 350-500 BAM6QT EXPERIMENTS COVERING BOTH QUIET FLOW AND NOISY FLOW,
AND SEVERAL CONFIGURATIONS. IN YEARS 4 AND 5 WE ALSO ANTICIPATE A TOTAL OF 8-12 HIGH-ENTHALPY

THE DELIVERABLES WILL BE CONTRIBUTIONS TO AND ANALYSIS FOR REPORTS DOCUMENTING THE EXPERIMENTS
AND RESULTS, AS WELL AS CONTRIBUTIONS TO CONFERENCE PAPERS AND ARCHIVAL JOURNAL ARTICLES IN
COLLABORATION WITH THE UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO, ONR SPONSORS, AND OTHER COLLABORATORS.

subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,1862 LAND GRANT COLLEGE,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
STATEMENT OF WORK
CO-PI BREHM, UNIVERSITY OF MARYLAND
CO-PI BREHM RESEARCH TEAM WILL BE SUPPORTING TWO MAIN TASKS OF THE MURI PROJECT: 1.) DEVELOPMENT OF
PSUB, METAMATERIALS AND POROUS SURFACES FOR FLOW CONTROL AND 2.) DEVELOPMENT OF FULLY-COUPLED CFD
(CHAMPS) MATERIAL RESPONSE MODELING (KATS) CODE. TASK 1 WILL REQUIRE THE DEVELOPMENT OF A MODEL THAT
CAN CAPTURE THE STABILITY CHARACTERISTICS OF THE TRANSITIONAL BOUNDARY LAYER COUPLED WITH THE PSUB,
METAMATERIALS AND POROUS SURFACES. A THEORETICAL FOUNDATION WILL BE CREATED BY EMPLOYING LOCAL
STABILITY THEORY WITH IDEALIZED WALL BOUNDARY CONDITIONS TO ESTABLISH THE MAXIMUM THEORETICAL LIMIT
FOR PASSIVE WALL-BASED STABILIZATION ACROSS THE FULL FREQUENCY SPECTRUM RELEVANT TO HYPERSONIC FLIGHT
CONDITIONS. THE TEAM WILL EXPLORE THIS GENERAL STABILITY ANALYSIS APPROACH IN CONJUNCTION WITH
THEORETICAL AND COMPUTATIONAL RESEARCH ON BROAD-FREQUENCY PSUB DESIGN PROVIDED BY HUSSEIN’S
TEAM, FURTHER REFINING OUR UNDERSTANDING AND APPLICATION OF THIS NOVEL PASSIVE FLOW CONTROL CONCEPT
TO HANDLE THE 1ST AND 2ND MODES, AND SUBSEQUENTLY MORE ADVANCED MODES SUCH AS GÖRTLER AND
CROSSFLOW INSTABILITIES. AFTER ESTABLISHING EFFECTIVE STRATEGIES FOR TRANSITION DELAY, A BROADBAND
FREQUENCY STRUCTURAL RESPONSE FOR CONTROLLING TURBULENT FLOWS WILL BE EXPLORED. THE RESEARCH TEAM
WILL ALSO DEVELOP AN ADJOINT OPTIMIZATION PROCEDURE THAT CAN BE USED TO OPTIMIZE THE STRUCTURE OF THE PSUBS,
METAMATERIALS AND POROUS SURFACES FOR OPTIMAL FLOW CONTROL PERFORMANCE. TASK 2 WILL INVOLVE DIRECT
INTERACTION WITH PROF. MARTIN’S RESEARCH TEAM FROM THE UNIVERSITY OF KENTUCKY WHERE THE CHAMPS CFD
SOLVER DEVELOPED AT UMD WILL BE COUPLED WITH THE KATS MATERIAL RESPONSE SOLVER DEVELOPED AT UK. THE
COUPLED SOLVER WILL BE USED TO SIMULATE THE AEROTHERMAL RESPONSE OF THE PSUB, METAMATERIALS AND POROUS
SURFACES.
