subaward_description:
THIS GRANT APPLICATION PROPOSES TO LEVERAGE THE EXPERTISE OF DR. GRICIUC RELATED TO MICROGLIA AND CD33 BIOLOGY. THE MAJOR GOAL OF THE PROPOSED WORK IS TO CHARACTERIZE NOVEL NANOPARTICLES THAT WILL EFFICIENTLY DELIVER CD33 SIRNAS TO MICROGLIA, EVALUATE MICROGLIAL UPTAKE OF NANOPARTICLES IN MOUSE BRAIN, AND ASSESS THE THERAPEUTIC EFFICACY OF CD33 SIRNA-LOADED NANOPARTICLES ON CD33 MRNA LEVELS, AMYLOID BETA (AΒ) PATHOLOGY, AND NEUROINFLAMMATION IN A MOUSE MODEL OF ALZHEIMER'S DISEASE (AD). THESE EXPERIMENTS WILL ENTAIL INVESTIGATION OF THE EFFECTS OF CD33 SIRNA-LOADED NANOPARTICLES ON NEUROINFLAMMATION AND MICROGLIAL CELL FUNCTION, AND USE OF THE HUMANIZED HUMCD33 MOUSE CROSSED TO THE 5XFAD MOUSE MODEL OF AD (5XFAD;HUMCD33), AN EXPERTISE OF DR. GRICIUC. INTERPRETATION OF THE DISCOVERIES WILL REQUIRE THE JOINT EXPERTISE OF DRS. GRICIUC AND JOSHI. THE PROPOSED WORK RELIES ON THE COLLABORATION BETWEEN THE LABORATORIES OF DRS. GRICIUC AND JOSHI. BECAUSE OF THE SEPARATE AND NON-OVERLAPPING EXPERTISE NECESSARY FOR SUCCESS OF THIS PROPOSAL, WE BELIEVE THAT THIS COLLABORATION MAXIMIZES THE POTENTIAL OF TEAM SCIENCE EFFORTS IN RESPONSE TO THE CHALLENGES OF THIS PROJECT. 
IN THIS PROPOSED STUDY, DR. GRICIUC WILL CONTRIBUTE HER EXPERTISE ON CD33 BIOLOGY, AD PATHOLOGY AND NEUROINFLAMMATION. DR. GRICIUC WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR IMPLEMENTING THE PROPOSED STUDIES, BE INVOLVED IN THE DESIGN OF IN VITRO AND VIVO EXPERIMENTS AND CONTRIBUTE TO DATA COLLECTION, ANALYSIS, AND VALIDATION. FURTHERMORE, SHE WILL OVERSEE ALL ASPECTS RELATED TO CD33 BIOLOGY DESCRIBED IN THIS RESEARCH PROPOSAL, SUCH AS DOWNREGULATION OF CD33 MRNA IN THE MICROGLIAL BV2-HUMCD33 CELL LINE AND IN MOUSE BRAIN. SHE WILL ENSURE THAT ALL THE EXPERIMENTS BASED ON MICROGLIAL CELL CULTURES AND MICROGLIA ISOLATION FROM ADULT MICE HAVE BEEN SUCCESSFULLY IMPLEMENTED. DR. GRICIUC WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR ASSESSMENT OF EFFECTS OF CD33 SIRNA-LOADED NANOPARTICLES ON AΒ PATHOLOGY, NEUROINFLAMMATION MARKERS, AND MICROGLIAL CELL FUNCTION IN HUMANIZED 5XFAD;HUMCD33 MICE. SHE WILL ALSO OVERSEE THE MAINTENANCE OF MOUSE COLONIES AND GENERATION OF 5XFAD;HUMCD33 MOUSE PROGENY BY DIRECTLY SUPERVISING TECHNICAL STAFF. MOREOVER, DR. GRICIUC WILL SUPERVISE AND MENTOR THE POSTDOCTORAL RESEARCH FELLOWS AND RESEARCH TECHNICIANS WORKING ON THIS PROJECT. FINALLY, SHE WILL CONVEY IMPORTANT FINDINGS ALONG WITH DR. NITIN JOSHI THROUGH PUBLICATIONS AND POSTER PRESENTATIONS OR TALKS AT CONFERENCES.

subaward_description:
BELYANIN’S GROUP AT TAMU WILL WORK ON DEVELOPING THEORETICAL FRAMEWORK FOR THE DESCRIPTION AND
DESIGN OF THE PROPOSED QUANTUM INFORMATION PLATFORM UTILIZING MOLECULAR QUBITS STRONGLY COUPLED
WITH PHOTONIC/PLASMONIC CAVITY MODES. THE THEORY WILL BE THEN USED TO INTERPRET THE EXPERIMENTAL
RESULTS OBTAINED BY THE WHOLE TEAM AND TO PROPOSE NEW EXPERIMENTS.
MORE SPECIFICALLY, BELYANIN’S GROUP WILL PERFORM CALCULATIONS OF THE QUANTUM DYNAMICS IN THE
SYSTEMS INCLUDING ELECTRONIC AND VIBRATIONAL DEGREES OF FREEDOM OF A MOLECULE STRONGLY COUPLED TO
A PLASMONIC CAVITY. OF PARTICULAR INTEREST IS THE EXTREME QUANTUM LIMIT OF A SINGLE ELECTRON STATE –
SINGLE PHOTON – SINGLE PHONON EXCITATIONS IN THE PRESENCE OF FINITE TEMPERATURE AND DECOHERENCE
EFFECTS. THIS ANALYSIS WILL BE THEN USED TO EVALUATE THE POSSIBILITY OF COHERENT CONTROL OF THE QUBIT
QUANTUM STATE, THE EFFICIENCY OF THE QUANTUM STATE TRANSDUCTION BETWEEN MOLECULAR AND PLASMONIC
DEGREES OF FREEDOM, AND THE IMPLEMENTATION OF SPECIFIC QUANTUM INFORMATION PROTOCOLS. THE
THEORETICAL ANALYSIS WILL BE CONDUCTED IN CLOSE COLLABORATION WITH OTHER PROJECT TEAM MEMBERS. ITS
RESULTS WILL SERVE AS INPUT FOR DESIGN, FABRICATION, AND TESTING OF THE PROPOSED MATERIAL PLATFORM FOR
QUANTUM INFORMATION PROCESSING. THE EXPERIMENTAL RESULTS WILL BE USED IN THE ITERATIVE PROCESS OF
SIMULATING, DESIGNING AND BUILDING THE SYSTEMS WITH OPTIMIZED PERFORMANCE.

SHE WILL PARTICIPATE IN THE INTERPRETATION OF THE RESULTS OF THE RESEARCH AND IN THE WRITING AND EDITING OF ABSTRACTS AND MANUSCRIPTS, PRESENTATION OF RESULTS, AND IN PREPARING REPORTS FOR THE SPONSOR AS NEEDED.
CO-PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR: ALICIA OLIVIER, DVM PHD, WHO WILL CONDUCT THE GROSS AND HISTOPATHOLOGIC ANALYSIS OF TISSUES OF CATTLE FOR ALL AIMS, ANALYZE DIFFERENCES BETWEEN GROUPS, AND PREPARE A WRITTEN REPORT OF THE PATHOLOGY FINDINGS.
LABORATORY MANAGER MERRILEE THORESEN, PHD. DR. THORESEN WILL OVERSEE ORDERING OF ALL MATERIALS NEEDED FOR THE RESEARCH, SHE WILL WORK WITH DR. WOOLUMS TO OVERSEE THE STUDENTS ASSISTING WITH TREATMENT AND EVALUATION OF CATTLE, SHE WILL ORGANIZE THE STORAGE OF ALL SAMPLES, AND SHE WILL ASSIST IN COMMUNICATION WITH THE SANTANGELO LABORATORY TO TRANSFER SAMPLES FOR PROCESSING AND ANALYSIS.
PHD GRADUATE STUDENT, CASSIE BARBER, DVM, TO ASSIST THE PI IN COLLECTING SAMPLES, MONITORING THE HEALTH OF CATTLE ON THE STUDY, TREATING ANY UNEXPECTED ILLNESS THAT OCCURS, OR ILLNESS THAT RESULTS FROM THE STUDY TREATMENTS, SUPERVISING STUDENT UNDERGRADUATE WORKERS WHO MILK LACTATING CATTLE, ORGANIZING MATERIALS FOR SAMPLING, ASSISTING WITH THE NECROPSY OF CATTLE, MAINTAINING RECORDS OF ALL TREATMENTS AND SAMPLES COLLECTED, AND PREPARING AN ABSTRACT FOR PRESENTATION AT A NATIONAL ANIMAL HEALTH MEETING.
MS GRADUATE STUDENT, KENZIE MCATEE, WHO WILL ASSIST WITH COLLECTING DAILY SAMPLES AND AT NECROPSY, ASSIST WITH KEEPING RECORDS OF ALL TREATMENTS ADMINISTERED AND SAMPLES COLLECTED, AND ASSIST WITH ASSESSMENT OF THE DAILY HEALTH OF THE CATTLE ON THE STUDY.
UNDERGRADUATE STUDENT TO MILK LACTATING COWS ON THE STUDY. COWS MUST BE MILKED TWICE DAILY, 7 DAYS A WEEK, WITH PROPER TEAT CLEANING AND DISINFECTION BEFORE AND AFTER MILKING, TO ENSURE THE MAMMARY GLANDS STAY HEALTHY DURING THE STUDY.

subaward_description:
PI SCHMIDT AGREES TO SUPPORT THE PROPOSED MURI RESEARCH LED BY CU BOULDER (THE PRIME
INSTITUTION) IN PERFORMING HYPERVELOCITY FREE FLIGHT EXPERIMENTS TO STUDY BOUNDARY LAYER
TRANSITION CONTROL VIA PHONONIC SUBSURFACES AND POROUS METASURFACES AT REALISTIC FLIGHT ENTHALPY,
AS DESCRIBED IN THE PROPOSAL. WE WILL ALSO PROVIDE EVALUATION AND INTERPRETATION OF DATA FROM BOTH
THE EXPERIMENTS PERFORMED AT CWRU AS WELL AS COMPLEMENTARY EXPERIMENTS TO BE PERFORMED IN
WIND TUNNELS AT PURDUE UNIVERSITY AND IN THE RAILGUN FACILITY AT NSWC DAHLGREN. PI SCHMIDT IS
AN EXPERT IN EXPERIMENTAL IMAGING AND FLUID DYNAMICS, AND HAS A LABORATORY EQUIPPED WITH A
HYPERSONIC AERODYNAMIC RANGE FOR CONDUCTING THE EXPERIMENTS. FURTHERMORE, THE PI HAS A HIGH
SPEED SCHLIEREN SYSTEM FEATURING AN IMACON I200 FRAMING CAMERA, CAPABLE OF FRAME RATES UP TO
200 MHZ WITH A RESOLUTION OF 1280 X 1024 PIXELS.
THE PI AND A SUPPORTED PHD STUDENT WILL WORK CLOSELY WITH OTHER MEMBERS OF THE MURI TEAM TO
DESIGN THE AERODYNAMIC RANGE MODELS AND CONDUCT THE EXPERIMENTS. BASELINE MODELS WITH NO
BOUNDARY LAYER CONTROL DEVICES WILL BE MANUFACTURED BY CWRU, WHILE THE CONTROL DEVICES
THEMSELVES WILL BE FABRICATED BY OTHER MEMBERS OF THE MURI TEAM. THE REMAINDER OF THE MODELS
THAT INCORPORATE THE CONTROL DEVICES WILL BE FABRICATED BY CWRU. WE WILL SHARE OUR RESULTS IN A
TIMELY MANNER WITH OTHER MEMBERS OF THE MURI TEAM, ESPECIALLY PI JEWELL AT PURDUE WHO IS
PERFORMING WIND TUNNEL EXPERIMENTS, AND PI BREHM AT THE UNIVERSITY OF MARYLAND WHO IS
PERFORMING CFD SIMULATIONS. FURTHERMORE, PI SCHMIDT WILL BE THE PRIMARY RESPONSIBLE PARTY FOR
COORDINATING WITH DR. MIKE LIBEAU AT NSWC DAHLGREN TO COORDINATE THE RAILGUN EXPERIMENTS AND
INCORPORATE THEM INTO THE LARGER RESEARCH PLAN.
THE TERM OF THE WORK IS EXPECTED TO BE 60 MONTHS, FROM MAY 1, 2024 TO APRIL 30, 2029, AND THE
TOTAL BUDGET REQUEST IS $618,554, WHICH PROVIDES 0.5 MONTHS SUMMER SALARY SUPPORT FOR THE PI IN
EACH YEAR, AND FULL SUPPORT FOR A PHD STUDENT. THE DETAILED RESEARCH PLAN AND BUDGET ARE INCLUDED
IN THE PROPOSAL.

subawardee_business_types: OTHER NOT FOR PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
THIS PROJECT BUILDS ON THE PREVIOUS MLRCP PROJECT AND AIMS TO DEVELOP AN IMPROVED LASER THREAT DETECTOR
USING A DIFFRACTIVE SENSOR. THE DIFFRACTIVE SENSOR AIMS TO UTILIZE KNOWABLE DIFFRACTION CHARACTERISTICS TO
DISTINGUISH BETWEEN A MONOCHROME (I.E. LASER) AND BROADBAND SOURCES. THE PROJECT USES THE LASER-INJECTION
CAMERA SYSTEM DEVELOPED IN THE PREVIOUS PROJECT BUT WILL MODIFY THE SENSOR WITH A DIFFRACTION GRATING. THEN
WE WILL COLLECT SIGNATURE DATA FOR EXAMPLE LASERS AND A BROADBAND SOURCE. SIGNATURE DATA WILL BE USED BUILD
MODELS FOR SYNTHETIC SIGNAL INJECTION OVERLAID ONTO REAL IMAGERY. THE DATA WILL ALSO BE ANALYZED TO SELECT AND
QUANTIFY KEY FEATURES TO DETECT LASER THREATS AND, IN PARTICULAR, DISCRIMINATE FROM BROADBAND SOURCES LIKE
LIGHTS AT NIGHT AND GLARE DURING THE DAY. NEXT, ENHANCED LASER THREAT DETECTOR ALGORITHMS WILL BE DEVELOPED
AND TESTED USING SYNTHETIC SIGNALS INJECTED ONTO REAL IMAGERY. THE DETECTOR PERFORMANCE WILL BE VALIDATED ON
REAL LASER SIGNALS INJECTED INTO THE SENSOR. FINALLY, WE WILL DEVELOP AND TEST GEOLOCATION ALGORITHMS USING
FLIGHT DATA PROVIDED BY AFRL.

subawardee_business_types: MINORITY INSTITUTION,U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,ALASKAN NATIVE SERVICING INSTITUTION,HISPANIC SERVICING INSTITUTION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
THE OBJECTIVES OF THIS PROPOSED 1-YEAR EXTENSION ARE TO (1) CHARACTERIZE EXTENDED STRUCTURAL
DEFECTS IN GESN FILMS GROWN BY REMOTE PLASMA ENHANCED CHEMICAL VAPOR DEPOSITION (RPECVD) ON SI(111)
SUBSTRATES IN ORDER TO IDENTIFY GROWTH CONDITIONS AND PATHWAYS BY WHICH DEVICE-QUALITY GESN FILMS CAN BE
REALIZED; (2) EXPLORE SELECTIVE AREA GROWTH ON PATTERNED SI(100) AND SI(111) SUBSTRATES AND RAPID THERMAL
ANNEALING (RTA) AS A MEANS TO MITIGATE THE FORMATION OF EXTENDED DEFECTS IN GESN FILMS. EPITAXIAL FILMS OF
GESN GROWN ON SI(111) BY AFRL WILL BE SENT TO UT ARLINGTON AND CHARACTERIZED USING HIGH-RESOLUTION
TRANSMISSION ELECTRON MICROSCOPY (HRTEM), X-RAY PHOTOEMISSION SPECTROSCOPY (XPS), AND SCANNING
TRANSMISSION MICROSCOPY (SEM). THESE VARIOUS CHARACTERIZATION TECHNIQUES WILL PROVIDE FEEDBACK TO AFRL
RESEARCHERS TO IDENTIFY GROWTH CONDITIONS THAT LEAD TO DEVICE-QUALITY FILMS. SEPARATELY, SI(100) AND SI(111)
SUBSTRATES WILL BE PATTERNED USING PHOTO-LITHOGRAPHY AND E-BEAM LITHOGRAPHY IN CONJUNCTION WITH DRY-ETCH
TECHNIQUES TO REALIZE PERIODIC STRUCTURES THAT WILL ENABLE SELECTIVE AREA GROWTH. THE AIM OF SELECTIVE AREA
GROWTH IS TO UTILIZE SUBSTRATE MORPHOLOGY TO CONFINE EXTENDED DEFECTS, THEREBY LEADING TO PERIODIC EPITAXIAL
STRUCTURES OF GESN THAT HAVE MINIMAL STRUCTURAL DEFECTS.

subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,HOSPITAL,NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
TITLE:
PROGRAMMING MULTISTABLE ORIGAMI AND KIRIGAMI STRUCTURES VIA TOPOLOGICAL DESIGN
KATIFORI WILL BE MAINLY INVOLVED IN THRUST 1 AND SECONDARILY THRUST 3 OF THE PROPOSED
WORK. HER WORK WILL BE PRIMARILY COMPUTATIONAL/THEORETICAL.
IN THRUST 1, WHICH INVOLVES THE STUDY OF COMPLEX, MULTISTABLE CONFIGURATIONAL LANDSCAPES VIA TOPOLOGICAL METHODS, SHE WILL ESTABLISH MATHEMATICAL METHODS
BASED ON PERSISTENT HOMOLOGY TO IDENTIFY FOLDING AND CUT PATTERNS LEADING TO ORIGAMI
AND KIRIGAMI STRUCTURES WITH MULTIPLE STABLE TARGET CONFIGURATIONS. THE MURI TEAM
WILL IDENTIFY SCHEMES OF LOCAL AND GLOBAL SENSING, CONTROL, AND ACTUATION IN ORDER TO
EFFICIENTLY TRANSFORM THE STRUCTURES BETWEEN THEIR STABLE CONFIGURATIONS. IN PARTICULAR,
KATIFORI WILL EMPLOY PERSISTENT HOMOLOGY (TOPOLOGICAL DATA ANALYSIS) TO OPTIMIZE
SENSOR/ACTUATOR PLACEMENT AND DYNAMIC WAVE ACTUATION TO EVENTUALLY DESIGN
IMPROVED STRUCTURES FOR EXPERIMENTAL IMPLEMENTATION.
IN ORDER TO APPLY THESE TOPOLOGICAL METHODS AND PRINCIPLES TO ORIGAMI/KIRIGAMI,
KATIFORI WILL TREAT ACTUATORS AS SOURCES IN THE RESPONSE FUNCTION, MEASURE THE
POSSIBLE RESPONSES AROUND EACH STABLE STATE AND EXPLORE HOW THESE CAN BE USED
TO PUSH THE SYSTEM INTO NEW STATES. ORIGAMI AND KIRIGAMI SHEETS WILL BE GENERATED
NUMERICALLY AND THE GEOMETRY AND TOPOLOGY OF THEIR CUT AND CREASE PATTERNS WILL BE
ADJUSTED TO GENERATE A LIBRARY OF REPRESENTATIVE STRUCTURES. WE WILL MEASURE THE
NONLINEAR RESPONSE NUMERICALLY TO CONSTRUCT AN APPROPRIATE DATA SET, AND USE
PERSISTENT HOMOL-OGY TECHNIQUES TO IDENTIFY THE ROBUST FEATURES OF THE RESPONSE,
E.G., COLLECTIONS OF EDGES WHOSE DEFORMATION IS FUNCTIONALLY CORRELATED, AND
CORRESPONDS TO A COHERENT RESPONSE. THE NATURE OF THE RESPONSE TO DIFFERENT
ACTUATORS WILL BE QUANTIFIED BY THE SIGNIFICANT FEATURES, OR SECTORS, WHICH
ENCAPSULATE THE RESPONSE. WE EXPECT THAT THE RELATIVE RELATIONSHIPS BETWEEN THE
SECTORS (E.G., THEIR RELATIVE PLACEMENT) WILL REVEAL THE ASPECTS OF THE SALIENT FEATURES
THAT ENABLE THE DESIRED RESPONSE.

FURTHERMORE, KATIFORI WILL USE OPTIMIZATION METHODS TO CONSTRUCT MULTIFUNCTIONAL
MECHANICAL NETWORKS, WHOSE RESPONSE TO ACTUATION WILL BE TARGETED TO SPECIFIC
SITES. THESE OPTIMIZATION METHODS WILL INCLUDE GRADIENT DESCENT METHODOLOGIES THAT
INVOLVED OBJECTIVE FUNCTIONS THAT PENALIZED EXCURSIONS FROM THE DESIRED RESPONSE,
AND COST CONSTRAINTS IMPOSED VIA LAGRANGE MULTIPLIERS.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: UNDERSTANDING AND ENGINEERING TRANSIENT MECHANICAL RESPONSES IN NANOPARTICLE-REINFORCED HETEROGENOUS PARTICULATE SYSTEMS
subawardee_business_types: PRIVATE UNIVERSITY OR COLLEGE,NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,BUSINESS OR ORGANIZATION,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION
subaward_description:
THE UNIVERSITY OF CHICAGO COMPONENT OF THE PROJECT ENTITLED "UNDERSTANDING AND ENGINEERING TRANSIENT MECHANICAL RESPONSES IN NANOPARTICLE-REINFORCED HETEROGENEOUS PARTICULATE SYSTEMS"
WILL BE CARRIED OUT BY THE RESEARCH GROUP OF PROFESSOR HEINRICH JAEGER. JAEGER WILL PARTICIPATE IN THRUST 1 AND SERVE AS THE THRUST 2 LEADER FOR THE PROJECT.
TOGETHER WITH THE OTHER PARTICIPANTS IN THRUST 1, JAEGER WILL PERFORM SIMULATIONS AND PARTICIPATE IN MODELING OF NANOPARTICLE-REINFORCED HETEROGENEOUS PARTICULATE SYSTEMS. THE SPECIFIC FOCUS OF HIS WORK WILL BE ON STOKESIAN DYNAMICS SIMULATIONS OF CONCENTRATED SUSPENSIONS OF PARTICLES IN A LIQUID.
IN THRUST 2, JAEGER WILL WORK ON THE EXPERIMENTAL COMPONENT OF THE MURI.AS PART OF THIS EFFORT HIS RESEARCH GROUP WILL SYNTHESIZE MODEL SILICA PARTICLES IN THE SIZE RANGE 10NM – 10ΜM, AND
CHARACTERIZE THE PARTICLES SHAPE, SIZE AND SURFACE CHARACTERISTICS. HE WILLTHEN SUSPEND THE PARTICLES IN DIFFERENT SOLVENTS AND PERFORM RHEOLOGICALMEASUREMENTS ON CONCENTRATED SUSPENSIONS WITH HIGH HETEROGENEOUSPARTICLE TYPES. JAEGER’S WORK WILL EMPHASIZE MESOSCALE MODELEXPERIMENTS IN WHICH THE ROLE OF PARTICLE PROPERTIES CAN BE VARIEDSYSTEMATICALLY. THIS WILL BE TACKLED THROUGH A CONCERTED EFFORT THAT USESSTEADY-STATE RHEOMETRY AS WELL AS IMPACT AND PENETRATION TESTS TO OBTAIN
THE TRANSIENT RESPONSE TO APPLIED STRESS. PRECISION TRIAXIAL LOAD TESTS WILLBE COMBINED WITH SIMULTANEOUS X-RAY IMAGING. HIGH-SPEED ULTRASOUNDIMAGING WILL BE USED IN COMBINATION WITH
RHEOMETRY AND IMPACT TESTING FOR THE CASE OF FULLY LIQUID-SATURATEDPARTICULATE MATERIALS.
THE EFFORT COMPRISES 5 PERIODS AND WELL AS 3 OPTIONAL PERIODS OFPERFORMANCE, AS FOLLOWS:
IN PERIOD 1 (5 MONTHS) JAEGER WILL FOCUS ON SETTING UP THE STOKESIANDYNAMICS SIMULATIONS IN THRUST 1 AND ON STARTING THE SYNTHESIS OF SILICAPARTICLES IN THE SIZE RANGE 10NM – 1ΜM IN THRUST 2. IN PERIOD 2 (12 MONTHS) JAEGER WILL RUN EXTENSIVE STOKESIAN DYNAMICSSIMULATIONS OF HETEROGENEOUS SPHERE SYSTEMS WITH SIZE RATIO UP TO 5. THESYNTHESIS EFFORT WILL BE EXTENDED TO PARTICLE SIZES UP TO 10ΜM.
IN PERIOD 3 (12 MONTHS) JAEGER WILL BROADEN THE EFFORT IN THRUST 1 BASEDON STOKESIAN DYNAMICS SIMULATIONS TO INCLUDE SIZE RATIOS UP TO 10. INTHRUST 2, THE FOCUS WILL SHIFT TO IMPACT AND SOIL PENETRATION EXPERIMENTS INORDER TO START ELUCIDATING THE ROLE TRANSIENTS.
IN PERIOD 4 (7 MONTHS) JAEGER WILL FURTHER EXTEND THE PARTICLE SIZE RATIO INTHE STOKESIAN DYNAMICS SIMULATIONS. IN THRUST 2, THE EXPERIMENTS WILLFOCUS ON TRACKING THE EVOLUTION OF THE CONTACT FORCE NETWORKS, USINGTRANSPARENT SUSPENSIONS AND OPTICALLY ACTIVE MECHANOPHORES.
IN OPTIONAL PERIOD 5 (5 MONTHS), JAEGER PLANS TO EXTEND THE STOKESIANDYNAMICS SIMULATIONS BEYOND SPHERICAL PARTICLES AND INTRODUCEANISOTROPIC COMPOUND PARTICLES USING A GLUED-SPHERE
MODEL. IN THRUST 2, JAEGER WILL INVESTIGATE QUASI-STATIC DEFORMATIONS ANDLOW-SPEED IMPACTS INTO FULLY SATURATED PARTICULATE MATERIALS ANDSUSPENSIONS, USING TRIAXIAL TESTS IN COMBINATION WITH X-RAY IMAGING AS WELLAS ULTRASOUND IMAGING.
IN OPTIONAL PERIOD 6 (12 MONTHS), JAEGER PLANS TO CONTINUE THE SIMULATIONEFFORT FROM PERIOD 5, IMPLEMENTING THE RESULTS FROM STATISTICAL MECHANICSTHEORY DEVELOPED BY OTHERS IN THRUST 1; IN
ADDITION, THE OUTCOMES FROM STOKESIAN DYNAMICS SIMULATIONS WILL BE USEDTO PROVIDE INPUT FOR SIMULATIONS OF MUCH LARGER, INERT GRANULAR PARTICLES,WHICH ARE DEVELOPED CONCURRENTLY IN THRUST 1
BY OTHERS. IN TERMS OF EXPERIMENTS IN THRUST 2, JAEGER’S GROUP WILLCONTINUE TO INVESTIGATE QUASISTATIC DEFORMATIONS AND LOW-SPEED IMPACTSINTO FULLY SATURATED PARTICULATE MATERIALS AND
SUSPENSIONS, WITH THE GOAL TO PROVIDE BENCHMARK DATA FOR THE VALIDATION OFTHE SIMULATIONS.
IN OPTIONAL PERIOD 7 (7 MONTHS), THE EFFORT RELATED TO BOTH THRUST 1 AND 2WILL BE CONTINUED AND FINALIZED.
THROUGHOUT THE PROJECT, JAEGER AND HIS GROUP AT THE UNIVERSITY OF CHICAGOWILL ACTIVELY PARTICIPATE IN COLLABORATION MEETINGS AS DIRECTED BY THE LEADPI, PROFESSOR JENNIFER LEWIS.

OUR OBJECTIVE IS TO MEASURE VEGETATION OVERRIDE FORCES IN A WAY THAT IS INDEPENDENT OF THE VEHICLE. THAT
IS, WE WILL NOT USE TIRE SLIP, VEHICLE ACCELERATION, OR ANY OTHER PROXY FOR EXERTED FORCE THAT INVOLVES THE
VEHICLE PLATFORM. RATHER WE WILL USE DIRECT MEASUREMENTS OF THE FORCE, AS MEASURED BY A PUSHBAR WITH
EMBEDDED FORCE SENSORS, TO DIRECTLY MEASURE THE OVERRIDE FORCES. IN ADDITION, WE PROPOSE TO USE MACHINE
LEARNING TECHNIQUES TO CORRELATE MEASURED FORCES TO SENSOR SIGNATURES (LIDAR AND/OR CAMERA) FOR ESTIMATING
VEGETATION OVERRIDE RISK IN REAL-TIME.

subaward_description:
"AT UCSB WE WILL LEVERAGE INTERACTIONS, CORRELATIONS, AND ENTANGLEMENT BETWEEN NV CENTERS AND
SEVERAL TYPES OF ENVIRONMENTAL SPINS TO PRODUCE ROBUST QUANTUM STATES THAT REALIZE METROLOGICAL
BENEFITS. WE WILL FORM ENSEMBLES OF HIGHLY COHERENT SPINS IN 2D AND 3D INSIDE DIAMOND AND ON
THE DIAMOND SURFACE, WITH CONTROL OVER DENSITY AND DIMENSIONALITY. NV CENTER AND P1 VENTERS
(SUBSTITUTIONAL NITROGEN DEFECTS) WILL BE INTRODUCED VIA ENGINEERED CHEMICAL VAPOR DEPOSITION
GROWTH OF DIAMOND WITH NITROGEN DOPING, WHERE DENSITY AND DIMENSIONALITY ARE CONTROLLED VIA
FLOW RATES AND DOPING TIMES. SURFACE SPINS WILL BE INTRODUCED VIA SURFACE TERMINATION AS WELL AS
PATTERNING WITH DNA ORIGAMI. THESE SURFACE SPINS COUPLED TO NEAR-SURFACE NV CENTERS WILL BE
USED TO ENHANCE THE SPATIAL RESOLUTION OF NV-BASED IMAGING, SERVING AS LOCAL QUANTUM SENSORS
THAT CAN BE BROUGHT WITHIN LESS THAN A NANOMETER OF THE SENSING TARGET. DRIVING AND ENTANGLEMENT
PROTOCOLS WILL BE IMPLEMENTED AND BENCHMARKED ON THESE SOLID-STATE SYSTEMS AND THEIR
ROBUSTNESS AND METROLOGICAL UTILITY WILL BE EVALUATED."

subaward_description:
THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO COMBINE CELLEC’S 0V-STABLE Z-SAFE TECHNOLOGY WITH A NOVEL GEL POLYMER ELECTROLYTE (GPE) FROM JOHNS HOPKINS UNIVERSITY APPLIED PHYSICS LABORATORY (JHU/APL) TO ENABLE EXTENDED PERFORMANCE IN EXTREME ENVIRONMENTS FOR LITHIUM-ION CYLINDRICAL CELLS. THE SYNERGISTIC BENEFITS OF THESE TWO TECHNOLOGIES HAVE BEEN PROVEN OUT IN THE POUCH CELL FORM FACTOR ON A PREVIOUS EFFORT SUPPORTED BY THE INTELLIGENCE COMMUNITY. LIQUID CARBONATE ELECTROLYTES ARE LIMITED IN THEIR OPERATIONAL TEMPERATURE RANGE AND SOLID ELECTROLYTES ARE LIMITED IN THEIR CHARGE/DISCHARGE POWER AND STILL NEED YEARS OF DEVELOPMENT TO REACH BROAD COMMERCIAL VIABILITY. CELLEC IS PROPOSING A HYBRID APPROACH TO BRIDGE THE GAP BETWEEN THE PERFORMANCE OF LIQUID AND SOLID ELECTROLYTES FOR EXTREME ENVIRONMENTS AND AIR FORCE APPLICATIONS. CELLEC PLANS TO EVALUATE THE THERMAL CAPABILITY OF THESE MATERIALS IN CYLINDRICAL CELL FORM FACTORS (I.E. 18650 CELLS). INTEGRATING THE PROPOSED GPE WITH CELLEC’S 0-VOLT TECHNOLOGY, IF SUCCESSFUL, COULD EXTEND THE OPERATING TEMPERATURE WINDOW BEYOND CONVENTIONAL LITHIUM-ION CELLS AND INTO THERMALLY ‘EXTREME’ ENVIRONMENTS.

SPECIFIC TASKS THAT WILL BE COMPLETED USING THE BATTERY DEVELOPMENT CENTER (BDC) PROTOTYPING FACILITIES AT THE ROCHESTER INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (RIT) ARE OUTLINED BELOW. THE RIT BDC IS A $3.5 MILLION OPEN USER FACILITY FUNDED BY NEW YORK STATE WITH SEMI-AUTOMATED BATTERY MANUFACTURING EQUIPMENT. CELL TESTING AND DATA ANALYSIS WILL TAKE PLACE AT CELLEC’S TESTING FACILITY IN ROCHESTER, NY. 

CELLEC WILL EVALUATE CELL PERFORMANCE METRICS AS A FUNCTION OF TOTAL EXPOSURE TIME TO 0-VOLT CONDITIONS, AS WELL AS TOTAL NUMBER OF EXPOSURES TO DEAD-BUS EVENTS. CELLS WILL BE GRADED A SECOND TIME AND THOSE PASSING 0V-STABILITY CRITERIA OF GREATER THAN 95% CAPACITY RETENTION AFTER A 0V-HOLD WILL MOVE ON TO THERMAL CAPABILITY TESTING WITH CELLS REPEATING A REGIMEN OF TESTS ASSOCIATED WITH CYCLE LIFE, RATE CAPABILITY AND 0V-STABILITY AT EXTENDED TEMPERATURE RANGES DOWN TO -20 °C AND UP TO 70 °C. DATA ANALYSIS WILL RUN CONCURRENTLY DURING THE EFFORT AND FINAL REPORTING WILL COMMENCE NEAR THE CLOSING OF THE PROGRAM. CELLS WITH PROMISING TESTING RESULTS WILL CONTINUE CALENDAR LIFE TESTING POST THE AFRL RNMA PROGRAM AND STEPS WILL BE INITIATED TO BEGIN UN 38.3 CERTIFICATION OF A CELL OFFERING FOR CUSTOMER USE.

subaward_description:
"MANUEL ENDRES AND HIS GROUP WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR IMPLEMENTING A VARIETY OF OPTICAL AND LASER
CONTROL TECHNIQUES FOR TRAPPING OF ULTRACOLD ALKALINE-EARTH ATOMS:
SPECIFICALLY, STRONTIUM ATOMS WILL BE TRAPPED IN ONE-, TWO-, AND THREE-DIMENSIONAL LATTICES OF
OPTICAL MICROTRAPS. AS DESCRIBED IN THE ‘TECHNICAL IMPLEMENTATION’ SECTION OF THE PROJECT
DESCRIPTION, THESE LATTICES WILL BE CREATED THROUGH ARRAYS OF OPTICAL TWEEZERS GENERATED WITH MULTIFREQUENCY
DRIVING OF AN ACOUSTIC OPTICAL DEFLECTOR OR VIA HOLOGRAPHIC GENERATION VIA A SPATIAL LIGHT
MODULATOR. THIS ARRAY WILL BE DIRECTLY LOADED FROM A COLD MAGNETO-OPTICAL TRAP RUNNING ON THE
NARROW 7KHZ, 689NM LINE OF STRONTIUM.

subaward_description:
THIS PROPOSAL OUTLINES FOUR INTERWEAVING TASKS.
TASK 1. A HUMAN-ROBOT TEAMING FRAMEWORK FOR MULTI-VIEWPOINT BOUNDING OVERWATCH. WE WILL
FORMULATE THE HUMAN MULTI-ROBOT MULTI-VIEWPOINT BOUNDING OVERWATCH PROBLEM AND PROPOSE A NOVEL
HUMAN-ROBOT TEAMING FRAMEWORK FOR ENHANCED BOUNDING OVERWATCH.
TASK 2. MODELING OF HUMAN TRUST IN ROBOTS AND MULTI-ROBOT TRUSTWORTHINESS FOR TRUST CALIBRATION.
TASK 3. META-RL FOR ADAPTIVE ROBOT MOTION PLANNING IN DYNAMIC OFFROAD ENVIRONMENTS. THIS TASK WILL
CREATE TRUST-CALIBRATED META-RL WITH LTL-BASED REWARD SHAPING TO AUTOMATICALLY DISCOVER PROVABLY
CORRECT ROBOT MOTION PLANS THAT CAN ADAPT TO REAL-TIME CHANGES ACROSS DIFFERENT TASKS OR ENVIRONMENTS.
TASK 4. HUMAN MULTI-ROBOT BOUNDING OVERWATCH SIMULATOR AND COMPARATIVE STUDIES. IN THIS TASK, WE
WILL DEVELOP A COMPARATIVE STUDY INVOLVING A MULTI-ROBOT SIMULATION TESTBED WITH HUMANS-IN-THE-LOOP.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: TREATMENT OF TBI-RELATED TINNITUS AND COMORBID PTSD: EXAMINATION OF NEUROBIOLOGICAL MARKERS RELATED TO SYMPTOM IMPROVEMENT
subawardee_dba_name: THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE UNIVERSITY OF ILLINOIS
subawardee_business_types: U.S. STATE GOVERNMENT,EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION,STATE CONTROLLED INSTITUTION OF HIGHER LEARNING
subaward_description:
SYMPTOMS. TO IDENTIFY THE COGNITIVE, BEHAVIORAL, AND EMOTIONAL
SYMPTOMS THAT ARE BOTH SHARED AND UNIQUE TO PTSD AND TBI‐RELATED TINNITUS
DISTRESS.
TIMELINE
(MONTHS)
UIUC
MAJOR TASK 1: THERE WILL BE SIGNIFICANT OVERLAP BETWEEN TBI‐RELATED TINNITUS DISTRESS
AND PTSD SYMPTOMS, AS MEASURED BY THE CLINICIAN ADMINISTERED PTSD SCALE FOR
DSM‐5 (CAPS‐5), THE PTSD CHECKLIST FOR DSM‐5 (PCL‐5), AND THE TINNITUS FUNCTIONAL
INDEX (TFI).
1‐48
SUBTASK 1 – OBTAIN IRB APPROVAL, AT UTHSCSA AND HRPO (UIUC WILL DEFER UTHSCSA) 1‐6 X
SUBTASK 2 – ENSURE ALL STUDY PERSONNEL RECEIVE THE NECESSARY TRAINING IN REGULATORY,
GOOD CLINICAL PRACTICE, CONSENTING PARTICIPANTS, AND PROTOCOLS 1‐6 X
SUBTASK 3 – COMPLETE A KICK‐OFF MEETING TO REVIEW THE PROCEDURES OF THE STUDY
WITH ALL PERSONNEL 6 X
SUBTASK 4 – COLLECT MEASURES FROM ALL CONSENTING PARTICIPANTS, WHO ARE ELIGIBLE FOR
THE STUDY. MEASURES TO TEST THIS HYPOTHESIS INCLUDE THE CAPS‐5, PCL‐5, AND TFI.
PARTICIPANTS WHO ARE RANDOMIZED TO BOTH THE CONDITION OF COGNITIVE PROCESSING
THERAPY (CPT) AND TREATMENT AS USUAL (TAU) WILL COMPLETE THESE MEASURES AT
BASELINE, POSTTREATMENT, AND ONE‐MONTH FOLLOW‐UP.
6‐40

subaward_description:
"THE USC TEAM WILL BE LED BY PROFESSOR ROGER GHANEM AND WILL BE ASSISTED BY A POSTDOC AND
A PHD STUDENT. THE USC TEAM WILL FOCUS ON THE DEVELOPMENT OF PROBABILISTIC LEARNING ALGORITHMS
TO BE INTEGRATED INTO THE WORKFLOW OF THE MURI TEAM. SPECIFICALLY, WE WILL DEVELOP PROBABILISTIC
LEARNING ON MANIFOLDS, POLYNOMIAL CHAOS, AND SWITCHING DIFFUSION MODELS AND ALGORITHMS THAT ARE
ADAPTED TO THE NEEDS OF THIS PROJECT. OUR TIMELINE WILL BE AS FOLLOWS:
YEAR 1 DESIGN AND COLLECT TRAINING SETS FOR LEARNING. CONSTRUCT PCE WORKFLOW AND DEMON-
STRATE IT ON PROTOTYPE PROBLEM.
YEAR 2 CARRY OUT PLOM LEARNING ON PROTOTYPE PROBLEM AND IDENTIFY INTRINSIC STRUCTURE
FOR EACH MODEL COMPONENT. IMPLEMENT PCE ON LARGE SCALE MODEL. START WORK ON
SWITCHING DIFFUSIONS FOR COUPLING SCALES.
YEAR 3 DEVELOP STATISTICAL SENSITIVITIES USING PCE AND ASSESS SIGNIFICANCE OF MODEL ERROR.
DEVELOP STOCHASTIC SENSTIVITIES USING PLOM. USE PCE TO AUGMENT THE TRAINING SET
FOR PROBABILISTIC LEARNING.
YEAR 4 USE PLOM AS A MODEL FOR THE TRANSITION PROBABILITIES OF THE SWITCHING DIFFUSIONS.
ESTIMATE THIS MODEL USING THE AUGMENTED DATA FROM PCE.
YEAR 5 INTEGRATE THE STOCHASTIC CONSTRUCTIONS INTO THE PREDICTIVE CAPABILITIES OF THE MURI
TEAM"

DETONATIONS UNDER CONDITIONS RELEVANT TO ROTATING DETONATION-BASED COMBUSTORS (RDCS) SUCH AS
AUGMENTERS, RAMJETS, AND ROCKET ENGINES. THE TEAM FOR THE PROPOSED WORK IS BASED AT SIX INSTITUTIONS:
STANFORD UNIVERSITY (PRIMARY), UNIVERSITY OF CENTRAL FLORIDA, UNIVERSITY OF VIRGINIA (UVA),

TEAM IS LED BY DR. CHLOE DEDIC, ASSISTANT PROFESSOR OF MECHANICAL AND AEROSPACE ENGINEERING. DR.
DEDIC’S RESEARCH FOCUSES ON THE DEVELOPMENT AND APPLICATION OF ADVANCED LASER-BASED DIAGNOSTICS
AND NONLINEAR SPECTROSCOPY TECHNIQUES TO STUDY NONEQUILIBRIUM, REACTING, AND HIGH-SPEED FLOW
ENVIRONMENTS
